Leads
Use this option manage leads, which includes the process of tracking and managing prospectivecustomers. This activity is very crucial as it might end up in bringing good customers to thefi nancial institution.
Note
: A
Lead
can also be automatically generated by a customer through internet channel(See,
Internet Channel Leads
).
Create a Lead
Follow these steps to create a new lead:
1.
Go to
Applications >>
Leads
.
The
Leads
page displays the existing leads tasks in the
Assigned
,
Pool
,
Hold
,
Archive
and
Reject
tabs based on theircurrent status.
The
Leads
page displays the following details of the existing leads in the system:
Column Name
Description
Lead Number
Displays the lead number.
Lead Channel
Displays the channel through which the lead was created.
Product Type
Displays the product type in which the lead is interested.
Customer Name
Displays the name of the customer.
Created By
Displays the name of the user who created the lead.
Created On
Displays the date on which the lead was created.
Task Due Date
Displays the date on which the task is due.
Branch
Displays the branch in which the lead was created.
Conversion Potential
Displays the potential of converting the lead into customer.
Lead Status
Displays the current status of the lead.
Product Processor
Displays the product processor of the lead.
Actions
Displays various options to assign leads to a user.
The leads on this page displays the details in the following tabs:
Grid Columns and Descriptions
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 4/766
·
Assigned
: Displays the leads that are assigned to the logged in user.
·
Pool
: Displays the leads that are in pool and are not assigned to any user.
·
Hold
: Displays the leads that are put on hold.
·
Archive
: Displays the leads are archived in the system.
·
Reject
: Displays the leads that are rejected.

Leads
page, click

as either

or

applicants for

product type.

and set the following fi elds:

from the drop down list.

and select the loan currency from thedrop down list.

Field Name

and City

or

Proposals
Use this option to create, view or edit
Consumer Durable Finance
proposals. Once a proposal issubmitted, the system validates the captured information based on certain checks, andaccordingly it generates an Application ID for further processing.
Create a new Proposal
Follow these steps to create a new proposal:
1.
Go to
Applications >>
Proposals
and
click
+
that appears on hovering over the menu option.
Or
Go to
Applications >>
Proposals.
O
n the
Proposals
page, the
Assigned
tab appears as active by default and the grid onthis tab displays the following detail:
Columns
Description
Proposal ID
Displays the unique proposal ID clicking which user can view/editthe application details and further process the application.
Customer Name
Displays the name of the customer.
Phone Number
Displays the phone number of the customer.
Created By
Displays the name of the user who created the application.
Created On
Displays when the application was created.
Proposal State
Displays the state of the proposal.
Product Processor
Displays the product processor.
Grid Columns and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 8/766
Note
:
·
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
Select Preference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
·
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial.
2.
Click
Create
Consumer
Durable
to create a new proposal.
Note
: The details in all the following accordions can also be saved as draft. The progress baron the top of the screen represents the completion of the application.
Field Name
Field Value
DSA/RO
*
Select the DSA/RO from the drop-down list.
The values available for section are maintained in theOffi cer master.
Sales Offi cer/DME
*
Displays the user ID of the user creating the applicationproposal.
Mobile Number
Input the mobile number of the DSA/RO along with thecountry code.
Branch
*
Select the branch from the drop-down list.
Dealer
*
Input the dealer name, which is maintained in the
DealersDetails Mapping
master.
Showroom
*
Input a showroom, which is maintained in the Showroommaster.
City
Displays the city of the branch automatically on selectingthe location.
Field Name
Field Value
Product
Input the product (which is governed from Product masterof FinnOne Neo Common Master module) under which theproposal has to be created.
Scheme
Input the scheme (which is governed from Scheme masterof FinnOne Neo Common Master module) under which theproposal has to be created.

,Make/Manufacturer

Variant
, then the selling price

MOP
The fi eld gets auto-populated with the maximum off erprice based on dealer's discounted scheme for the asset.The value in this fi eld is derived based on the generic datauploaded in the system.
Note:
MOP cannot be more than MSRP.
Asset Cost
*

*

in the

Loan Details section
(
Use this section to add loan related details.
)
Rate of Interest
Displays the rate of interest for the loan application.
This gets auto-populated by the system based on
RepaymentPolicy
attached to the selected Scheme.
Tenure (Months)
*
Displays the number of months of the loan tenure.
This fi eld gets auto-populated based on
Repayment Policy
attached tothe selected Scheme. However this can be edited and changed, ifrequired.
Disbursal Type,Number ofDisbursals
Select the whether the disbursal type is
Single
or
Multiple
. Input theNumber of Disbursals in case the disbursal type is
Multiple
.
Finance
Details
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 12/766
InstallmentFrequency
*
Select a frequency to pay the installments.
Installment Mode
*
Select the installment mode either as
Arrears
or
Advance
.
No. of Advanced EMI
Displays the number of advanced EMI.
This fi eld gets auto-populated based on
Repayment Policy
attached tothe selected Scheme. However, this can be edited and changed, ifrequired.
Down Payment
This fi eld displays the auto-calculated value based on LTV % and AssetCost. This can however be edited, if required.
LTV (%)
*
This fi eld gets auto-populated based on the selected Asset Model orVariant.
In case it is not been maintained for the Variant, then the systempopulates the LTV attached to the Asset Model. However this can beedited and changed, if required.
Note:
LTV% = ((
Funding Amount * Asset Cost
)
*100
).
This gets changed if there is any change in Asset Cost and FundingAmount.
Also, on changing the LTV %, the Funding Amount and Down Paymentgets changed as well. On the other hand, on changing the FundingAmount or Down Payment, LTV % changes automatically.
Funding Amount
This fi eld gets auto-populated and the value in this fi eld signifi es theamount, the customer wants to get fi nanced.
This may also be derived if the Total Cost and Down Payment arespecifi ed. It is then calculated as (
Total Cost
-
Down Payment
). Else, it isderived based on the LTV % and Asset Cost. This fi eld can however bealso edited, if required.
Eff ective/Final LoanAmount, Installment
Displays the Eff ective/Final Loan Amount and Installment Amount for theloan application.
Processing Fee
Displays the processing fee of the loan.
This value in this fi eld gets auto-populated based on the
ChargePolicies
master and can be edited, if required.
Tax Amount
Displays the service tax to be paid by the customer.
This fi eld gets auto-populated based on the
Charge Policies
master and isread-only.
Additional Fee
Displays the additional fee, if any.
Due Day
Select a day on which the installment gets due.
Interest Start Date
Select a date from which interest will be calculated with the installment.
First InstallmentDate
Displays the due day of the next month as the fi rst installment date.
View RepaymentSchedule
Click to view the repayment schedule as per the selected loan details.
Save Details
Click to save the loan details.

system. In this case, it appears as read-only and cannotbe edited.

andMaturity/Expiry Date

or

to combine this liability withan exiting liability.

lick

*

On successful submission, the system automatically runs the
Dedupe
,
Credit
Bureau, Score Card
and
Credit Policy
checks,after which the application ID gets generated and the application either moves to
Credit Approval
or
Disbursal Author
stage depending on Manual or Auto Approval confi guration.
In between this process, if there are any details that are either still pending to be captured or are incomplete, theapplication automatically moves to the
Curing
stage during the Auto Approval process or is manually sent to this stagefrom the Credit Approval stage during the Manual Approval process. The application may also land at
Reject Review
incaseof auto rejection during the Auto Approval process or when the application is manually rejected from the Credit Approvalstage during the Manual Approval process.
Once the Dedupe process completes, the
Credit Bureau
icon (
) appears on the screen. Click this icon to run the creditbureau process. Once this process is complete, user can view the Credit Bureau report or rerun the process, if required.
See Next
Credit Approval
Curing
See Also
DisbursalAuthor

Applicable for
All
Product Types

option or through

, are captured. Also at this stage, a decision is taken to

a Lead.

gets generated, and the lead moves to the nextstage as per the workfl ow defi ned in the system for the chosen product type.

time.

Use the
Document
tab to add the documents required for the loan application.

This tab is common to both
Individual
and
Non Individual
Applicant Type.
On this tab, user can perform the following tasks:
Update checklist
: Click this option to update the document checklist.
Accept Additional Document
: Use this option to accept multiple additional documentsby providing
Reference
Type
,
Entity
Type
and
Documents
. The additional document canalso be marked as
Mandatory
.
Download
: Select the documents in the left pane and click the
Download All
todownload the selected document(s).
Note
:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.
Cancel Rescan Request
: Click the
Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.
Request Status
: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
Columns Name
Description
Initiator Name
Displays the name of the user whoinitiated the rescan request.
Preferred Mode Of Communication
Documents
Grid Columns and Description

Completion Date

Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescan request is initiated.
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Completed Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescanning request iscompleted
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Remarks
Displays the remarks to rescan thedocument.
Cancel Remarks
Displays the remarks to cancel therescanning request.
Status
Displays the current status of the rescanrequest.
Action

Cancel Request
link to cancel allthe available rescan requests.
Click the
Show Cancelled Details
link toview the details of the cancelled rescanrequests.

: Use this option to displays the summary of all the documents of theloan application. User can
Print
the summary and
Save as PDF
, if required.
View and Select Documents
: List of documents are displayed in the left pane groupedby Customer and Applicant. Select the document to change the status of the documentin the middle pane.
Link and Swap Images
: The document images can be linked and swapped with otherdocument images by clicking the
and selecting the required option.
Update Document Status
: Select a document in the left pane and select any of thefollowing status:
o
Pending (
)
: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 31/766
o
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Received On
*
Select the date on which the document isreceived.
Original/Photocopy
Select Original if the original document isreceived or select Photocopy if the photocopyof the document is received.
Verifi cation Status
*
Select a verifi cation of the document.
Processing State
Select a processing state of the document.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
o
WaivedOff (
)
: Select when the document is no more required from thecustomer. Also, select the
Waive Off Reasons
and input the
Waive Off Remarks
*
and other
Remarks
in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark thedocument as
Mandatory
.
o
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by thecustomer on a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Deferred Reasons
Select reasons to mark this document asdeferred.
Tentative Date
*
Select a date on which this document isexpected to be received.
Deferred Remarks
*
Input remarks of marking the document asdeferred.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
Note
:
To mark the status of multiple documents, select multipledocuments using the check box, click
Mark Status
that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.
Incomplete Doc (
): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.
Complete Doc
: Click to mark an incomplete document as complete. This option appearsonly if a received documents has been marked as incomplete.
Fields Name and Description
Fields Name and Description

As the lead moves from
Lead stage
to next Application stage, the status of the Lead changes to
CONVERTED.

Application ID
gets generated.

:

a note of this
Application ID
number as it can be used at later stages for searchingthe Application record in the system.
More Actions on Application
To Put a Lead on Hold
Click
More Options
>>
Hold
to put a hold on the Lead.
Reject Lead
To Reject the Lead
Click
More Options >> Reject
to
send the Lead to
Lead
Rejection Queue
.
1
On the pop up screen that appears, select
Decision Reason
(reason for rejection) from thedrop down list and click
Done
. On successfully completing the action, a
Success
messageappears on the screen and the system redirects to the
Leads
screen. Else, errors (if any) arenotifi ed.
2
On the
Leads
screen, click
Reject
tab to view the rejected Lead in the grid. The
Status
ofthe rejected Lead is displayed as
REJECT REVIEW
.
3
Click
Send Back
in the
Actions
column, to send the Lead back to the lead user. Or, toremove the Lead from the system, click
Reject
in the
Actions
column.
On rejection, the system automatically generates a notifi cation informing the user about therejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
Hold Lead
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 39/766
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Lead stops until the Lead is on Hold.
1.
On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold, fromthe drop down list and specify the
Reassignment Date
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
:
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date.On this date the hold on the Application gets removed and it is re-assignedto the Application user.
2.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on thescreen and the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) arenotifi ed.
3.
On the
Leads
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Lead on hold in the grid. The Status ofthe Lead is displayed as
HOLD
.
Note
:
To unhold a lead,
click
Lead Number
in the grid and then click
MoreOptions >> Unhold
.
To View Workfl ow History Record of the Application
Click
More Actions >>
Workfl ow History Records
to view the workfl ow history of theApplication.
The
Workfl ow History Records
pop-up screen appears showing the list of all the activitiesperformed on the application.
See Next
Lead Details
Lead Details
At this stage, detailed information associated with the fi nance, such as
Applicant Information
,
Finance Details
,
Documents
,
Summary Analysis
,
References
, etc.
needs to be added.
Note:
The Applications Grid displays applications in various process stages. The rows of theApplications Grid can be colour coded based on the priority of an application. The user mustcreate a record in the Assignment Matrix master for a particular color in a stage and then map anevent code with the record for assigning the colour.
Follow these steps to process an application at this stage using following steps:
1.
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
Workfl ow History Records
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 40/766
2.
On the
Applications
page, the
Assigned
tab appears as active by default and the grid on this tab displays the followingdetail:
Columns
Description
Application ID
Displays the unique application ID clicking which user can viewthe application details and further process the application.
Loan Account Number
Displays the loan account number.
Application Form Number
Displays the application form number
.
Neo Cust ID
Displays the customer ID associated with the respectiveapplication.
Host Id
Displays the host identifi cation number.
Customer Name
Displays the name of the customer.
Stage
Displays the current stage of the application.
Current Stage Timestamp
Displays the current stage timestamp of the application.
Query Status
Displays the status of the query
Query Responded
Displays the name who has responded the query
Query Raised By
Displays the name who has raised the query.
Previous User
Displays the name of the previous user.
In-Queue Status
Displays the status of the application whether the application isnewly introduced or returned from a stage.
Product Type
Displays the product type associated with the respectiveapplication.
Product Name
Displays the product name associated with the respectiveapplication.
Account Type
Displays the account type as
FAS
,
FOS
, or
BOTH
in case of the FASapplication.
Request Type
Displays the application request type.
Primary Mobile Number
Displays the Primary Mobile Number.
Sourcing Date
Displays the date on which the application is created.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the channel through which the application is created.

Collateral Details tab >> Lien Details tab
(
Use this section to capture the Lien Details of the collateral.
)

: This tab can be hidden/shown from backend confi gurations.
Institution Name, Application ID,Total Loan Amount and Date
Input the institution name, application ID, total loanamount and date in the respective fi elds.
Secured Amount, Margin (%), andLien Amount
Input the amount in the respective fi elds.
Note
:
Secured Amount
must be less than or equal tothe
Total Loan Amount
. The
Lien Amount
is calculated as
Secured Amount/1 - Margin%
.

Displays the amount without lien.
Charge Type
Select a charge type for the lien.

: To add another record, click
. And, to delete an existing record, click
in the

column.

, DocumentDistrict and Document State

. And, to delete an existing record, click

master

Use the
Document
tab to add the documents required for the loan application.
Note:
This tab is common to both
Individual
and
Non Individual
Applicant Type.
On this tab, user can perform the following tasks:
Update checklist
: Click this option to update the document checklist.
Accept Additional Document
: Use this option to accept multiple additional documentsby providing
Reference
Type
,
Entity
Type
and
Documents
. The additional document canalso be marked as
Mandatory
.
Download
: Select the documents in the left pane and click the
Download All
todownload the selected document(s).
Note
:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.
Documents
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 149/766
Cancel Rescan Request
: Click the
Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.
Request Status
: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
View Summary
: Use this option to displays the summary of all the documents of theloan application. User can
Print
the summary and
Save as PDF
, if required.
View and Select Documents
: List of documents are displayed in the left pane groupedby Customer and Applicant. Select the document to change the status of the documentin the middle pane.
Link and Swap Images
: The document images can be linked and swapped with otherdocument images by clicking the
and selecting the required option.
Update Document Status
: Select a document in the left pane and select any of thefollowing status:
o
Pending (
)
: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
o
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Received On
*
Select the date on which the document isreceived.
Original/Photocopy
Select Original if the original document isreceived or select Photocopy if the photocopyof the document is received.
Verifi cation Status
*
Select a verifi cation of the document.
Processing State
Select a processing state of the document.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
Latitude & Longitude
To fetch the Latitude & Longitude details of theapplicant's location from where applicationhave been sourced, click
Fetch Latitude &Longitude
.
o
WaivedOff (
)
: Select when the document is no more required from thecustomer. Also, select the
Waive Off Reasons
and input the
Waive Off Remarks
*
and other
Remarks
in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark thedocument as
Mandatory
.
o
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by thecustomer on a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Grid Columns and Description
Fields Name and Description
Fields Name and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 150/766
Deferred Reasons
Select reasons to mark this document asdeferred.
Tentative Date
*
Select a date on which this document isexpected to be received.
Deferred Remarks
*
Input remarks of marking the document asdeferred.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
Latitude & Longitude
To fetch the Latitude & Longitude details of theapplicant's location from where applicationhave been sourced, click
Fetch Latitude &Longitude
.
Note
: To mark the status of multiple documents, select multipledocuments using the check box, click
Mark Status
that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.
The documents that are marked as RCU Eligible are displayed in thedocument list with RCU identifi cation tick.
Incomplete Doc (
): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.
Complete Doc
: Click to mark an incomplete document as complete. This option appearsonly if a received documents has been marked as incomplete.
Add, View, Edit and Compare Documents
: Click
to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are
Received
. User can view and edit the image in the
DocumentViewer
section at the right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent versionof the documents and their details. Moreover, there is a drop down list with the name ofall the uploaded images. Select the fi le to view in the document viewer.
Note
: Only users with the appropriate authority can view thedocument images.
Once the details of the documents is updated, click
Submit
.

Repayment Track Record
*
Select either of the following repayment track record of SHG:
·
ATR
: Ability To Repay (ATR) ensure that the existing debt ofSHG (if any) plus the new loan amount does not surpassthe reasonable percentage of the their income.
·
ETR
: In case of Excellent Track Record (ETR), peak delay inrepayment is 45 days and average delay is 15 days.
·
GTR
: In case of Good Track Record (GTR), peak delay inrepayment is 60 days and average delay is 15 days.
·
PTR
: In case of Poor Track Record (PTR), peak delay inrepayment is 45 days and average delay is 30 days.
Type of Last Facility Availed
*
Select the last facility availed by SHG from the drop down list.

Input the last linkage amount.

Select either
Yes
or
No
indicating whether the meeting recordbook is present or not.

*
Select either
Yes
or
No
indicating whether the CC or RF thatcomes under the govt. scheme exists for SHG.

Yes
or

More Actions on Application
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing.
On successfully completing theaction,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
hesystem redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listedeither in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
Task Assignment Master
.
Note:
§
A notifi cation is sent to inform the Relationship Manager regarding the current stage of theapplication.
§
An automatic mail is sent to the primary e-mail ID captured while creating the application.

More Actions >> Cancel Application
to
add the Application in the
Cancelled Applications
list.
On the

pop-up screen that appears, select

(reason forcancellation) from the drop down list. The

gets automatically populated.

Additionally, add the additional comments and click

.

To View the Cancelled Application:
1.
Go to
Applications >> Cancelled Applications
.
2.
Click
Application ID
link to view the details of the cancelled application.

On the

page, select and action from the Select Actiondrop-down list as either

or

.
In case

. is selected, select an activity from the

drop-down list to send to the application to that stage.

Input

and click

.

SMS
or
Email
.
To Put an Application on Hold
1.
Click
More Actions >>
Hold Application.
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Application stops until the Application is onHold.
2.
On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold from thedrop down list.
3.
Specify the
Reassignment Date, Time of Reassign
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
: The application can be put on hold for the current date as well.
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date. On thisdate the hold on the Application gets removed and it is re-assigned to theApplication user.
4.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on thescreen and the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) arenotifi ed.
5.
On the
Applications
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Application on hold in the grid.The Status of the Application is displayed as
HOLD
.

:

click
Application
Number
in the grid andthen click

To Raise a Query
1.
Click
More Actions >>
Raise Query
2.
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
3.
Input the name in the

fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.

Click

to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Note
:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.
To Unquery
1.
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
2.
Select
Reason
from the drop down list and input
Comments
.

Click
Done
.

:

above the grid.

in the Action column of the grid.

Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
orcombination of both.
3.
Input
Remarks
and click
Reassign Query
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk reassign by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Reassign
above the grid.
To Re Run priority
Click
More Actions >>
Re Run Priority
to update the priority of the application on the basis ofchanged parameters.
To view/download Loan Application Form
Click
Loan Application Form
to download/view a PDF version containing all the details of theapplication.
Note:
Once generated, the log will be refl ected in the
Customer Communication History
section.
See Next
LoginAcceptance
See Also
Detailed DataEntry (DDE)
Re Run Priority
Loan Application Form
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 173/766
Login Acceptance
At this stage, a basic check is done on details that were captured previously and using the
Acceptance Sheet
, any one of the following decisions is taken:
Approve and move the Lead to next stage
Discrepant and send the Lead to
Sales Queue
for rework
Reject the Lead
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
1.
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
2.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Application FormNumber, Finance Account Number, Neo Cust ID, Identifi cation No., Customer Name, Stage, Query Status, QueryResponded, Current Stage Timestamp, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type,Phone No., Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority
and
Actions
,
for eachapplication.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
3.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Login Acceptance
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Login Acceptance
stage.
In case of
Any
Finance product type, use this screen to check or update the Application details under the
ApplicantInformation
,
Finance Details, Documents,
and
References
tabs as explained in the
Lead Details
stage.
In case of
Credit Card
product type, use this screen to check or update the Application details under the
Lead Details
tab as explained in the
Lead Conversion
stage.
4.
Go to
Acceptance Sheet
tab to proceed.
The
History section
in this tab displays the history log of the
Decisions
marked for this Application.
The grid displays
Decision, Reason
,
Comments
,
Discrepant Comment, Decision Taken By
and
Date
for each decisiontaken.
Note
: The Discrepant Comment is the comment provided against every decision by thediscrepant at the Sales Queue stage.
5.
In the
Acceptance Checklist
section, choose either
N/A
,
Yes
or
No
for all checklist points as displayed there.
6.
Choose
Decision
*
as either
Approve, Reject, File Not Complete or Discrepant
from the drop down list.
7.
Choose
Reason
*
for the Decision taken from the drop down list.
The
Description
fi eld is populated automatically based on the selected Reason and is read only.
Also, input
Comments
, if required.
8.
Optionally, click the
Print Checklist
link to view and print the checklist of current as well the previous stages on whichthe checklist was marked.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 174/766
9.
Click
Save as Draft
to save the partially fi lled acceptance sheet.
10.
Click
Save
and
Proceed
to save the details.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
he system redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listed either in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
Task Assignment Master
.
For
Decision
chosen as
File Not Complete or Discrepant,
the
Stage
of the Application changes to
SALES QUEUE
. TheApplication is sent to the lead users for rework.
For
Decision
chosen as
Approve
, the
Stage
of the
Application
changes to the next stage (
as per the defi ned workfl ow
) inthe grid.
To Cancel the Application
Click
More Actions >> Cancel Application
to
add the Application in the
Cancelled Applications
list.
1.
On the
Cancel Application
pop-up screen that appears, select
Reason Code
(reason for cancellation) from the dropdown list. The
Reason Description
gets automatically populated.
2.
Additionally, add the additional comments and click
Done
.
On successfully completing the action, a
Success
message appears on the screen and the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
To view the cancelled applications,
1.
Go to
Applications >> Cancelled Applications
.
2.
Click
Application ID
link to view the details of the cancelled application.
3.
On the
Review Cancelled Application
page, select and action from the Select Action drop-down list as either
Confi rm Cancellation
or
Send Back for Processing to activity
.
In case
Send Back for Processing to activity
. is selected, select an activity from the
Select Activity
drop-down list tosend to the application to that stage.
4.
Input
Remarks
and click
Save
.
On cancellation, the system automatically generates a notifi cation informing the user about the rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 175/766
To Put an Application on Hold
Click
More Actions >>
Hold
to put a hold on the Application.
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Application stops until the Application is on Hold.
1.
On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold, from the drop down listand specify the
Reassignment Date
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
:
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date. On this date the holdon the Application gets removed and it is re-assigned to the Application user.
2.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action, a
Success
message appears on the screen and the systemredirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
3.
On the
Applications
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. The Status of theApplication is displayed as
HOLD
.
Note
:
To unhold a Application,
click
Application
Number
in the grid and then click
MoreOptions >> Unhold
.
To Raise a Query
Click
More Actions >>
Raise Query
to raise a query.
1.
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in the respective fi elds.
2.
Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query is resolved.
3.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Note
:
To remove the query, click
Unquery
from the Unquery column of the grid.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications

Sales Queue
The Applications that have been marked as
discrepant
or
incomplete
at Login Acceptance Stagemove to Sales Queue.
At this stage, the authorized user can work on the Application, update the incomplete details inthe Application and view the decision details history of Login Acceptance Stage.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
1.
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
2.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Neo Cust ID,Identifi cation No., Customer Name, Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type,
Product Name, Account Type, RequestType, Phone No., Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
foreach application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
3.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Sales Queue
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Sales
Queue
stage.
4.
On the
Sales Queue
screen, all the fi elds in the
Acceptance Sheet
tab is in read-only mode except the DiscrepantComment fi eld. The user can view the decision and comments inserted at the
Login Acceptance
stage and provide
Discrepant Comments
. All the other tab(s) are active to edit and update the incomplete Application information.
Update the incomplete information and click
Save
to save the details.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
he system redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listed either in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
Task Assignment Master
.
To Reject the Application
Click
More Options >> Reject
to
send the Application to
Rejection Queue
.

At this stage, the complete sourcing, applicant, documents and references details of theApplication are captured. Details of additional applicants can also be added at this stage, ifrequired.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
1.
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
2.
On the
Applications
page, the
Assigned
tab appears as active by default and the grid on this tab displays the followingdetail:
Columns
Description
Application ID
Displays the unique application ID clicking which user can viewthe application details and further process the application.
Neo Cust ID
Displays the customer ID associated with the respectiveapplication.
Customer Name
Displays the name of the customer.
Stage
Displays the current stage of the application.
In-Queue Status
Displays the status of the application whether the application isnewly introduced or returned from a stage.
Product Type
Displays the product type associated with the respectiveapplication.
Product Name
Displays the product name associated with the respectiveapplication.
Request Type
Displays the application request type.
Phone No.
Displays the phone number of the customer.
Sourcing Date
Displays the date on which the application is created.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the channel through which the application is created.
Sourcing Branch
Displays the sourcing branch through which the application iscreated.
Grid Columns and Description

Displays the product processor for the application. For example:Internal, mCAS and so on.
Branch
Displays the branch at which the application is created.
Assignee
Displays the name of the user to whom the application assigned.
Created By
Displays the name of the user who created the application.
Priority
Displays the priority of the application.
Actions
Displays the actions that can be performed on the record.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.

of a particular application to navigate to the

screen.

: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the

stage.

and

andMaturity/Expiry Date

dates.

On this tab, user can perform the following tasks:

,

and
Documents

.

Note
:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.

Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.

: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
Columns Name
Description

Displays the name of the user whoinitiated the rescan request.
Initiation Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is initiated.
Completion Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is completed.

Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescan request is initiated.

Completed Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescanning request iscompleted
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.

and selecting the required option.

o

)

, if required.
o

)

Fields Name

)

and

to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are

section at the right side of the screen. Click

14.
Know Your Customer (KYC)
At this stage, KYC check is done to check whether the applicant, partner/director or sellor/lessorhas any
existing relationships
with the bank or whether the applicant is a part of any caution listor negative database. This check also determines that the required identifi cation documents ofthe applicant are received.
Using this stage, the number of matching applicants and their detail on respective databases(
Internal and Negative
) can be viewed.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 225/766
Follow these steps to process an application at this stage:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The grid displays the following details:
Columns
Description
Application ID
Displays the unique ID of the application.
Loan Account Number
Displays the Loan Account Number of the applications thatare either partially or fully disbursed.

Displays the ID of the customer generated by FinnOneNeo
TM
.

Displays the identifi cation number provided by thecustomer.

Displays the name of the customer.

Displays the current stage of the application.

Introduced
,
Returned
or
Forwarded
from later stages. In case theapplication is Forwarded, hover over the status to viewother details, such as
Returned Details, From Stage, ToStage
and
Reason
.

Displays the type of application requests, as follows:
New Application
: Displays when the application requestsfor a new loan.
Top-up without Closure
: Displays when the applicationrequests for the top-up.
Multiple Assets
: Displays when the application requests formultiple assets.
Balance Transfer with Top-up
: Displays when theapplication requests for transferring the loan balanceamount to other bank with top-up.
New Credit Card Application
: Displays when theapplication requests for a new credit card.
Phone No
Displays the phone number of the customer
Sourcing Date
Displays the date on which the sourcing is done for theapplication.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the sourcing channel of the application.
Branch
Displays the branch from where the application wasinitiated.
Credit Offi cer
Displays the name of the credit offi cer of the application.
Product Type
Displays the product type of the application
Product Name
Displays the product name of the application
G
rid Columns and Description

Select the Reason and Reason Description fromthe drop-down list. Click
to add multipleReasons and Reason Descriptions.

/TopUp

Update checklist

Reference
Type
,
Entity
Type
and
Documents
. The additional document canalso be marked as
Mandatory
.
Download

Download All
todownload the selected document(s).

:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.
Documents
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 260/766
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.
Cancel Rescan Request
: Click the
Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.
Request Status
: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
Columns Name
Description
Initiator Name
Displays the name of the user whoinitiated the rescan request.
Initiation Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is initiated.
Completion Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is completed.
Requested Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescan request is initiated.
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Completed Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescanning request iscompleted
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Remarks
Displays the remarks to rescan thedocument.
Cancel Remarks
Displays the remarks to cancel therescanning request.
Status
Displays the current status of the rescanrequest.
Action
Click the
Cancel Request
link to cancel allthe available rescan requests.
Click the
Show Cancelled Details
link toview the details of the cancelled rescanrequests.

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 261/766
View Summary
: Use this option to displays the summary of all the documents of theloan application. User can
Print
the summary and
Save as PDF
, if required.
View and Select Documents
: List of documents are displayed in the left pane groupedby Customer and Applicant. Select the document to change the status of the documentin the middle pane.
Link and Swap Images
: The document images can be linked and swapped with otherdocument images by clicking the
and selecting the required option.
Update Document Status
: Select a document in the left pane and select any of thefollowing status:
Pending (
)
: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Received On
*
Select the date on which the document isreceived.
Original/Photocopy
Select Original if the original document isreceived or select Photocopy if the photocopyof the document is received.
Verifi cation Status
*
Select a verifi cation of the document.
Processing State
Select a processing state of the document.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
WaivedOff (
)
: Select when the document is no more required from the customer.Also, select the
Waive Off Reasons
and input the
Waive Off Remarks
*
and other
Remarks
in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark the document as
Mandatory
.
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by the customeron a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Deferred Reasons
Select reasons to mark this document asdeferred.
Tentative Date
*
Select a date on which this document isexpected to be received.
Deferred Remarks
*
Input remarks of marking the document asdeferred.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
Fields Name and Description
Fields Name and Description

): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the

and

.

SMS

To Put an Application on Hold
Click
More Actions >>
Hold Application.
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Application stops until the Application is onHold.
On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold from thedrop down list.
Specify the
Reassignment Date, Time of Reassign
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
: The application can be put on hold for the current date as well.
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date. On thisdate the hold on the Application gets removed and it is re-assigned to theApplication user.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screenand the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.

Applications
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. TheStatus of the Application is displayed as
HOLD
.

Number
in the grid andthen click

To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing.
On successfully completing theaction,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
hesystem redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listedeither in

or

tab, depending on the confi guration in the

.
Note:
A notifi cation is sent to inform the Relationship Manager regarding the current stage of theapplication.
An automatic mail is sent to the primary e-mail ID captured while creating the application.

On the

pop-up screen that appears, input

and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
Input the name in the

fi eld for whom the query is being raised.

check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.

to add the query in the grid.

Query Code
link to view the query details.

Query History Records

:

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 269/766
At this stage, a bank user takes a decision whether to approve the application for furtherprocessing or not after verifying the applicant's credit history and report as received from theCredit Bureau in response to the Credit Bureau Check, which was either initiated earlier or hasbeen initiated from here itself. In case the Bureau Check was not initiated before, a bank usercan initiate the check from this screen itself and wait for the report. If required, a bank user canalso re-initiate the check for single, multiple or all the applicants.
This stage only appears if it has been confi gured as a part of the application workfl ow.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,
Customer Name,
Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate
to verify details on the
Credit Bureau ReferralActivity
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Credit BureauReferral Activity
stage.
On the
Credit Bureau Referral Activity
screen, under the
CIBIL
section, the system displays the following details of the
Primary
and
Secondary
applicants depending on their Applicant Type:
Note
:
If required, a user can also
Re-Run
the
Credit Bureau Check
for all the applicants.
Field Name
Field Value
Applicant
accordion
Name
Gets displayed as captured in the loan application.

Gets displayed as captured in the loan application.

Gets displayed as recorded by CIBIL.

:

On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold from thedrop down list.
Specify the
Reassignment Date, Time of Reassign
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
: The application can be put on hold for the current date as well.
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date. On thisdate the hold on the Application gets removed and it is re-assigned to theApplication user.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screenand the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
On the
Applications
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. TheStatus of the Application is displayed as
HOLD
.
Note
:
To unhold a Application,
click
Application
Number
in the grid andthen click
More Options >> Unhold
.
Only user with appropriate authority can hold/unhold the applications.
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing.
On successfully completing theaction,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
hesystem redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listedeither in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
Task Assignment Master
.
Note:
A notifi cation is sent to inform the Relationship Manager regarding the current stage of theapplication.
An automatic mail is sent to the primary e-mail ID captured while creating the application.
To Raise a Query
Click
More Actions >>
Raise Query
Move Application to Next Stage
Raise a Query
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 274/766
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Note
:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.
To Unquery
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
Select
Reason
from the drop down list and input
Comments
.
Click
Done
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk unquery by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Unquery
above the grid.
To Reassign Query
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
To Reassign a query user has to select either
Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
orcombination of both.
Input
Remarks
and click
Reassign Query
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk reassign by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Reassign
above the grid.
To Re Run priority
Click
More Actions >>
Re Run Priority
to update the priority of the application on the basis ofchanged parameters.
Re Run Priority
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 275/766
Detailed Data Entry (DDE)
At this stage, the child tabs that were disabled in previous stages are now enabled for capturingproperty details for Home Loans and Loans Against Property, Insurance details of specifi ed Assetin case of Consumer and Commercial Vehicle fi nance and other important fi nance parameters.
Also, the Application details captured at previous stages can be edited or updated, if required.
Follow these steps to process an application at this stage:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
On the
Applications
page, in the
Assigned, Pool and Hold
tabs appear and the grid on these tabs displays the followingdetail:
Columns
Description
Application ID
Displays the unique application ID clicking which user can viewthe application details and further process the application.
Application Form Number
Displays the Application Form Number.
Loan Account Number
Displays the Loan Application Number.
Neo Cust ID
Displays the customer ID associated with the respectiveapplication.
Customer Name
Displays the name of the customer.
Stage
Displays the current stage of the application.
Current Stage Timestamp
Displays the Current Stage Timestamp of the application.
Query Responded
Displays the name of the user who has responded the query
Query Raised By
Displays the name of the user who has raised the query
In-Queue Status
Displays the status of the application whether the application isnewly introduced or returned from a stage.
Product Type
Displays the product type associated with the respectiveapplication.
Product Name
Displays the product name associated with the respectiveapplication.
Scheme
Displays the scheme name associated with the respectiveapplication.
Account Type
Displays the account type as
FAS
,
FOS
, or
BOTH
in case of the FASapplication.
Request
Type
Displays the application request type.
Phone Mobile No.
Displays the phone mobile number of the customer.
Sourcing Date
Displays the date on which the application is created.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the channel through which the application is created.
Grid Columns and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 276/766
Key Product Processor
Displays the product processor for the application. For example:Internal, mCAS and so on.
Branch
Displays the branch at which the application is created.
Parent Branch
Displays the name of the Parent Branch.
Credit Offi cer
Displays the name of the Credit Offi cer.
Assignee
Displays the name of the user to whom the application assigned.
Created By
Displays the name of the user who created the application.
Priority
Displays the priority of the application.
Actions
Click
Reassign For User
to assign the application to another useror click
Assign To Pool
to assign the application to pool.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popupscreen.
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Detailed Data Entry
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Detailed DataEntry
stage.
Any existing information at this stage can be edited or updated under
Applicant Information
,
Collateral, Documents,
and
References
tabs as explained in
Lead Details
.
The
Detailed Data Entry
page displays following tabs to view, edit or capture any required information:
Go to the
Loan Details
tab to capture Sourcing, Asset, Loan Parameters and Receipt details intheir respective child tabs.
All fi elds in these child tabs are common to both
Individual
and
Non Individual
Applicant Type.
Sourcing Details
Use this option to view, edit or capture the sourcing details of theapplication.
Note:
The schemes that are mapped to a user are available for selection inthis stage.
Loan
Details
Asset Details
(Appears only for Consumer Vehicle, Commercial Vehicle, Commercial Equipment and Farm EquipmentLoan)
Sourcing Details

to link theapplication with the limit reference number forfuture references.

*

or

.

It is a read-only fi eld and gets autopopulated by the system.
Application ID
Displays the unique application ID.
Note:
It is a read-only fi eld and gets autopopulated by the system.

*

or

.

Reference

,
Entity

and
Documents

and

4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 329/766
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.
Cancel Rescan Request
: Click the
Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.
Request Status
: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
Columns Name
Description
Initiator Name
Displays the name of the user whoinitiated the rescan request.
Initiation Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is initiated.
Completion Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is completed.
Requested Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescan request is initiated.
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Completed Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescanning request iscompleted
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Remarks
Displays the remarks to rescan thedocument.
Cancel Remarks
Displays the remarks to cancel therescanning request.
Status
Displays the current status of the rescanrequest.
Action
Click the
Cancel Request
link to cancel allthe available rescan requests.
Click the
Show Cancelled Details
link toview the details of the cancelled rescanrequests.

View Summary
: Use this option to displays the summary of all the documents of theloan application. User can
Print

Save as PDF
, if required.
View and Select Documents
: List of documents are displayed in the left pane groupedby Customer and Applicant. Select the document to change the status of the documentin the middle pane.
Link and Swap Images
: The document images can be linked and swapped with otherdocument images by clicking the
and selecting the required option.
Update Document Status
: Select a document in the left pane and select any of thefollowing status:
Pending (
)
: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Received On
*
Select the date on which the document isreceived.
Original/Photocopy
Select Original if the original document isreceived or select Photocopy if the photocopyof the document is received.
Verifi cation Status
*
Select a verifi cation of the document.
Processing State
Select a processing state of the document.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
Latitude & Longitude
To fetch the Latitude & Longitude details of theapplicant's location from where applicationhave been sourced, click
Fetch Latitude &Longitude
.
Note:
Password-protected can not be uploaded. You can also Drag andDrop the documents.
WaivedOff (
)
: Select when the document is no more required from the customer.Also, select the
Waive Off Reasons
and input the
Waive Off Remarks
*
and other
Remarks
in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark the document as
Mandatory
.
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by the customeron a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Deferred Reasons
Select reasons to mark this document asdeferred.
Tentative Date
*
Select a date on which this document isexpected to be received.

that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.

): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the

and

in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.

Add, View, Edit and Compare Documents
: Click
to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are
Received
. User can view and edit the image in the
DocumentViewer
section at the right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent versionof the documents and their details. Moreover, there is a drop down list with the name ofall the uploaded images. Select the fi le to view in the document viewer.

: Only users with the appropriate authority can view thedocument images.
Once the details of the documents is updated, click
Submit

ntermediary Type

or

Repayment Track Record
*
Select either of the following repayment track record of SHG:
ATR
: Ability To Repay (ATR) ensure that the existing debt ofSHG (if any) plus the new loan amount does not surpassthe reasonable percentage of the their income.
ETR
: In case of Excellent Track Record (ETR), peak delay inrepayment is 45 days and average delay is 15 days.
GTR
: In case of Good Track Record (GTR), peak delay inrepayment is 60 days and average delay is 15 days.

This button is active only for the new customersapplying for a loan.
Validate Bank Account
Click to validate the bank account with the parent system.
Note
: This is an authority-based feature which is visible onlyto users with the requisite authority.
Fetch Bank
Click to fetch the bank details from the respective bankdatabase.
Note:
If no details are provided in the
Bank Details
section,then the detail of all the banks get fetched based on theidentifi cation details provided by the customer.
If details of multiple banks are provided in the
Bank Details
section, then details of the selected bank(s) record getfetched.
Info
: A service is created that searches for the bankaccounts linked with the Identifi cation details and bankaccount details provided by the customer and fetches thedetail of all the bank accounts that are linked with thoseidentifi cation details.

Displays unique id of the facility.

Displays the parent id in case the facility type is child.
Sanctioned Amount
Displays the sanctioned amount of the facility.
Drawing Power
Displays drawing power of the customer.
In Process Limit
Displays the facility limit amount that is in process.
Consumed Limit
Displays the amount consumed.
Available Limit
Displays the available amount.
Facility Details
Use this section view and initiate the facility. In addition, Repayment Parameters and otherparameters, such as Charges, Receipts, Facility Sourcing, Covenant, etc.
Facility ID
Displays the unique facility id.
Click to capture and view various facility parameters indiff erent tabs.
Product Type
Displays product type of the facility.
Facility Type
Displays whether the facility is Fund Based or Non Fund Based.
Facility Group
Displays group of the facility.
Product
Displays the product associated with the facility.
Conditions
This tab displays the standard and special conditions associated with the loan account. Onthis tab, select either
Yes
or
No
option in the
Condition Met
column indicating whether thecondition is met or not.
F
acility Initiation
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 367/766
Facility Sub Group
Displays sub group of the facility.
Scheme
Displays the scheme mapped with the facility.
Sanction Details (Amount / ROI/ Tenure)
Displays the sanctioned amount, rate of interest and tenure ofthe facility.
Parent Facility ID
Displays the parent facility id in case the current facility type ischild.
Facility Expiry Date
Displays expiry date of the facility.
Facility Status
Displays current status of the facility
Disbursal Status
Displays current disbursal status of the application.
Initiate
Click to initiate the selected facilities in the grid. User caninitiate single or multiple facilities by selecting them in thegrid.
Short Term Secured Loan (STSL) tab is used to capture all the income and expenses of the loanapplicants. This tab support the DBR and eligibility calculation for STSL Schemes and is accessibleto the authorized users only.
E-Sign
This section displays the details of the E-sign request raised and you can Re-Initiate the E-signrequest for documents
.
Fields Name
Description
Name, Request Type,Documents, Stage, Initiated On,Initiated By, Status , StatusUpdated On, Action
Displays the details in E-sign request initiated.
Fetch Signing Details
Click
to fetch the signing details and
Initiate For
*
fi eld isdisplayed.
Initiate For
*
Select the option for which you want to initiate the E-signrequest.
The system displays the details of the signer.
Initiate
Click
button to initiate the E-sign request.
The system initiates the E-sign request for the document.
On successfully completing the action, a Success messageappears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
State
*
Displays the name of the state for which e-stamping isapplicable.
Denomination
*
Displays the stamp value/ denomination for e-stamping.
Change Stamp Details
Mark the check box if you want to Change the Stamp Details.
The State and Denomination fi eld gets enabled.
STSL Income Assessment
E-Sign

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 368/766
Click
Save
to save the details.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors(if any) are notifi ed.
Note
: Apart from the tabs that are explained above,there are many miscellaneous tabs thatexist in the system and may appear as per the product defi nition confi guration. In such cases,information relevant for those tabs can be viewed and modifi ed as well.
More Actions on Application
To View Workfl ow History Record of the Application
Click
More Actions >>
Workfl ow History Records
to view the workfl ow history of theApplication.
The
Workfl ow History Records
pop-up screen appears showing the list of all the activitiesperformed on the application.
To Cancel the Application
Click
More Actions >> Cancel Application
to
add the Application in the
Cancelled Applications
list.
On the
Cancel Application
pop-up screen that appears, select
Reason Code
(reason forcancellation) from the drop down list. The
Reason Description
gets automatically populated.
1.
Additionally, add the additional comments and click
Done
.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen andthe system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
To View the Cancelled Application:
1.
Go to
Applications >> Cancelled Applications
.
2.
Click
Application ID
link to view the details of the cancelled application.
3.
On the
Review Cancelled Application
page, select and action from the Select Actiondrop-down list as either
Confi rm Cancellation
or
Send Back for Processing to activity
.
In case
Send Back for Processing to activity
. is selected, select an activity from the
Select Activity
drop-down list to send to the application to that stage.
4.
Input
Remarks
and click
Save
.
On cancellation, the system automatically generates a notifi cation informing the user aboutthe rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
Workfl ow History Records
Cancel Application
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 369/766
To Put an Application on Hold
Click
More Actions >>
Hold Application.
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Application stops until the Application is onHold.
On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold from thedrop down list.
Specify the
Reassignment Date, Time of Reassign
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
: The application can be put on hold for the current date as well.
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date. On thisdate the hold on the Application gets removed and it is re-assigned to theApplication user.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screenand the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
On the
Applications
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. TheStatus of the Application is displayed as
HOLD
.
Note
:
To unhold a Application,
click
Application
Number
in the grid andthen click
More Options >> Unhold
.
Only user with appropriate authority can hold/unhold the applications.
To Generate CAM Report
Click
CAM Report
to
generate the CAM Report for the respective home loan or FAS application.
Note
: Only authorized users can generate and/or print the CAM Report.
A link to open CAM Report appears in
Customer Communication History
section.
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing.
On successfully completing theaction,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
hesystem redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listedeither in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
Task Assignment Master
.
Note:
Hold Application
CAM Report (for HL and FAS Applications only)
Move Application to Next Stage
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 370/766
A notifi cation is sent to inform the Relationship Manager regarding the current stage of theapplication.
An automatic mail is sent to the primary e-mail ID captured while creating the application.
To Raise a Query
Click
More Actions >>
Raise Query
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Note
:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.
To Unquery
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
Select
Reason
from the drop down list and input
Comments
.
Click
Done
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk unquery by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Unquery
above the grid.
To Reassign Query
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
To Reassign a query user has to select either
Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
orcombination of both.
Input
Remarks
and click
Reassign Query
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk reassign by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Reassign
above the grid.
Raise a Query

to download/view a PDF version containing all the details of theapplication.

section.

Use this stage to make a decision based on the details captured in the
Detailed Data Entry
stage.This stage appears only for Finance Against Securities (FAS) applications. All the details capturedin the previous stages can be viewed.
