Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
Re Run Priority
Loan Application Form
Limit Tree
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 372/766
On the
Applications
page, in the
Assigned, Pool and Hold
tabs appear and the grid on these tabs displays the followingdetail:
Columns
Description
Application ID
Displays the unique application ID clicking which user can viewthe application details and further process the application.
Neo Cust ID
Displays the customer ID associated with the respectiveapplication.
Customer Name
Displays the name of the customer.
Stage
Displays the current stage of the application.
In-Queue Status
Displays the status of the application whether the application isnewly introduced or returned from a stage.
Product Type
Displays the product type associated with the respectiveapplication.
Product Name
Displays the product name associated with the respectiveapplication.
Account Type
Displays the account type as
FAS
,
FOS
, or
BOTH
in case of the FASapplication.
Request Type
Displays the application request type.
Phone No.
Displays the phone number of the customer.
Sourcing Date
Displays the date on which the application is created.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the channel through which the application is created.
Key Product Processor
Displays the product processor for the application. For example:Internal, mCAS and so on.
Branch
Displays the branch at which the application is created.
Assignee
Displays the name of the user to whom the application assigned.
Created By
Displays the name of the user who created the application.
Priority
Displays the priority of the application.
Actions
Click
Reassign For User
to assign the application to another useror click
Assign To Pool
to assign the application to pool.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popupscreen.
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
DDE QC
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
DDEQC
stage.
Grid Columns and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 373/766
Any existing information at this stage can be edited or updated under
Applicant Information
,
Finance Details,Collateral,
and
Documents
tabs as explained in
Lead Details
.
The
DDE QC
page displays all the of following tabs of
Detailed Data Entry
stage in view mode. To take decision based onthe captured data, set the following fi eld in the Decision tab:
Field Name
Description
Decision
*
Select the decision from the drop down.
Reason
*
Select the reason for selecting the respective decision fromthe drop down.
Description
*
The description is auto populated based on the selected
Reason
.
Click
and
to add or delete a Reason andDescription respectively.
Decision Comments
Input the decision comments, if any.
Use the
Document
tab to add the documents required for the loan application.
Note:
This tab is common to both
Individual
and
Non Individual
Applicant Type.
On this tab, user can perform the following tasks:
Update checklist
: Click this option to update the document checklist.
Accept Additional Document
: Use this option to accept multiple additional documentsby providing
Reference
Type
,
Entity
Type
and
Documents
. The additional document canalso be marked as
Mandatory
and
Scan mandatory
.
Note
: On clicking the
Move to Next Stage
button, all the pendingdocuments that are marked as
Scan Mandatory
will be sent toEnterprise Content Management (ECM) module for scanning.
Download
: Select the documents in the left pane and click the
Download All
todownload the selected document(s).
Note
:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.
Cancel Rescan Request
: Click the
Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.
Stage Decision
D
ocuments
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 374/766
Request Status
: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
View Summary
: Use this option to displays the summary of all the documents of theloan application. User can
Print
the summary and
Save as PDF
, if required.
View and Select Documents
: List of documents are displayed in the left pane groupedby Customer and Applicant. Select the document to change the status of the documentin the middle pane.
Link and Swap Images
: The document images can be linked and swapped with otherdocument images by clicking the
and selecting the required option.
Update Document Status
: Select a document in the left pane and select any of thefollowing status:
Pending (
)
: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
WaivedOff (
)
: Select when the document is no more required from the customer.Also, select the
Waive Off Reasons
and input the
Waive Off Remarks
*
and other
Remarks
in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark the document as
Mandatory
.
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by the customeron a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Note
:
To mark the status of multiple documents, select multipledocuments using the check box, click
Mark Status
that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.
Incomplete Doc (
): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.
Complete Doc
: Click to mark an incomplete document as complete. This option appearsonly if a received documents has been marked as incomplete.
Add, View, Edit and Compare Documents
: Click
to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are
Received
. User can view and edit the image in the
DocumentViewer
section at the right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent versionof the documents and their details. Moreover, there is a drop down list with the name ofall the uploaded images. Select the fi le to view in the document viewer.
Note
: Only users with the appropriate authority can view thedocument images.
Once the details of the documents is updated, click
Submit
.

Fields Name and Description

(Use this section to apply charges on the FAS application.

The type of the charge applied.

The type of the applicant or business partner.

Diff erential Rate

Eff ective Charge Amount

Contractual Charges
(Use this section to apply contractual charges on the FAS application.

Apply Charges
to apply the charges added in the grid.)
Charge Name
Displays the name of the charge applied.
Charge Type
Displays the type of the charge applied.
Charge Defi nition Type
Displays the type of the charge defi nition applied.
Applicant/BP Type
Displays the type of the applicant or business partner.
Applicant/BP Name
Displays the name of the applicant or business partner.
Actual Rate/ Amount
Displays the actual rate and amount of charge applied.
Diff erential Rate/ Amount
Displays the diff erential rate/ amount applied.

The Deviation sub-tab contains
Credit Deviation
and
Business Deviation
subsections.
Both sub-tabs contain
Assigned to Me
,
All Deviations
and
Pending Deviations
subsections.
Click
Raise Manual Deviation
to raise manual deviations. The Add Manual Deviation pop-up is displayed.
Field Name
Description
Deviation defi nition
*
Select the deviation defi nition from the drop-down list.
Deviation Description
Displays the Deviation Description based on the deviationdefi nition selected.
Role
Displays the role based on the deviation defi nitionselected. This fi eld cannot be edited.
User
*

Charges
Deviations

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 376/766

to rerun the deviation policy.

to view deviation history of the application.
Fields Name
Description
Bank Parameters tab
This tab displays the bank parameters and only the authorized user can update/edit the details.
Minimum Scrips
*
Displays minimum number of scrips.
Maximum Scrips
*
Displays maximum number of scrips.
Grace Period after which CL Drops
*
Displays number of grace days after the creditlimit drops.
DP Round off Factor
Displays the DP round off factor.
Non Excess Amount
Check in case of non excess amount.
Single Scrip Wt. Contribution(%)
*
Displays the percentage of single scrip weightcontribution.
Special Scrip Wt. Contribution over single(%)
*
Displays the percentage of special scrip weightcontribution over single.
Single Scrip Calculation Override
Check to allow single scrip calculation override.
Minimum variation in CL(%)
Displays the positive and Negative percentageof minimum variation in credit limit.
Loan to Portfolio Margin % Buckets
Displays the percentage of loan to portfoliomargin buckets.
Broker DP Calculation
Displays the slab wise calculation of DP.
Sanctioned Credit Limit From
Displays the starting amount of sanctionedcredit limit.
Sanctioned Credit Limit To
Displays the ending amount of the sanctionedcredit limit.
Minimum No of Scrip
Displays the minimum number of scrips for thesanctioned amount range.
Single Scrip Wt Contribution
Displays the single scrip wt contribution for thesanctioned amount range.
Actions
Displays icons to perform various actions, suchas Delete and Edit.
Click
Save
to save the details.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors(if any) are notifi ed.

: Apart from the tabs that are explained above,there are many miscellaneous tabs thatexist in the system and may appear as per the product defi nition confi guration. In such cases,information relevant for those tabs can be viewed and modifi ed as well.

ank Parameters

Displays the product name associated with the respectiveapplication.

Displays the account type as
FAS
,
FOS
, or
BOTH
in case of the FASapplication.
Request Type
Displays the application request type.
Grid Columns and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 381/766
Phone No.
Displays the phone number of the customer.
Sourcing Date
Displays the date on which the application is created.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the channel through which the application is created.
Key Product Processor
Displays the product processor for the application. For example:Internal, mCAS and so on.
Branch
Displays the branch at which the application is created.
Assignee
Displays the name of the user to whom the application assigned.
Created By
Displays the name of the user who created the application.
Priority
Displays the priority of the application.
Actions
Click
Reassign For User
to assign the application to another useror click
Assign To Pool
to assign the application to pool.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Gold Valuation
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Gold Valuation
stage.
The
Gold Valuation
page, set the following fi elds in the modal window, before valuation:
Field Name
Field Value
Branch
*
Input the branch where loan account is created.
Product Type
*
Input the product type in which the loan account exists, from thedrop down.
Product
*
Input the product in which the loan account exists, from thedrop down.
Scheme
*
Input the scheme in which the loan account exists, from the dropdown.
Note
: These fi elds appear in case the Gold Valuation stage is confi gured as the fi rst stage forthe Gold Finance application.
Set, edit or update the following fi elds:
Field Name
Field Value
Gold Valuation
(Use this section to add the valuation details of gold.
Note: To add multiple valuations, click
Add Valuation
, after adding the fi rst valuation.)
Collateral ID
Input the collateral ID.
Global Collateral Number
Input the global Collateral number.
Packet Number
Input the packet number of the gold.
Note:
Click
Packet Number History
to view the history of thepacket in the pop-up.
Field(s) Name and Description
Field(s) Name and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 382/766
Number of Packets
Input the number of packets used to keep the gold.
Tray Number
*
Input the tray number where the packet is kept.
Vault Number
*
Input the vault number where the packet is kept.
Valuation By
*
Input the name of the agency who did the valuation.
Valuer Name
*
Input the name of the agent who did the valuation.
LTV (%)
*
Input the Loan to Value in percentage.
Loan Amount (INR)
*
The loan amount in varies depending upon the LTV.
Renewal Counter
Input the number of times the gold/ornament is renewed.
Third Party Renewal Counter
Input the number of times the gold/ornament is renewed by thethird party.
Ornament Details
(Use this section to capture the ornament details.)
Ornament Name
*
Input the ornament name.
Quantity
*
Input the quantity of the ornament.
LTV (%)
*
LTV is set as 100, by default.
Packet Number
Input the packet number where the ornament is kept.
Actual Fineness
Input the actual fi neness of gold of the ornament.
Fineness
Input the fi neness of the gold.
Rate/gms
*
Rate/gms is auto populated depending upon the current rate ofgold rate maintained in the system.
Gross Wt. (gms)
*
Input the gross weight in gms.
Stone Wt. (gms)
*
Input the weight of the stone, if any, in gms.
Impurity Wt. (gms)
*
Input the weight of impurity in gms.
Other Wt. (gms)
*
Input the other weight, if any, in gms.
Net Wt. (gms)
*
Input the net weight in gms.
Value of Gold
*
Value of gold is calculated based on the rate and net weight ofgold.
Gold Purity Test
Select the test performed of the verifying the purity of gold.
Is Hallmark
Check if there is hallmark on the ornament.
Is Sampled
Check if the ornament is sampled.
Photo
Click
Add Photo
link to add the photo.
Remarks
Click
Add Remarks
link to add the remarks
Additional Field
Input the additional data in this fi eld.
Actions
Click the icons to perform respective tasks.
Done
Click Done to save the valuation details.
Note:
The saved details appear in the grid. To add anothervaluation click
Add Valuation
. In case of multiple valuations,select the valuation to be considered using
Considered
.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 383/766
Valuation Certifi cate,Borrower Receipt
Click
Valuation Certifi cate, Borrower Receipt to
view therespective document. Click
View Details
to view the informationof respective valuation.
Waive Off Other Valuations
(Use this button to waive off already added valuation. While renewing, any shortfall in theamount of ornament will be handled as per the policies defi ned in the Charge Policies master)
Reason Code
*
Input the reason code for waiving Off the valuation.
Reason Description
*
Input the description for waiving off the valuation.
Actions
Click the icons to perform respective tasks.
Click to add record.
Additional Comments
Input additional comments, if any, for the valuation.
Packet Open
(Use this button to renew the already added ornaments.)
Reason Code
*
Input the reason code for renewing the valuation.
Reason Description
*
Input the description for renewing the valuation.
Actions
Click the icons to perform respective tasks.
Click to add multiple reasons.
Remarks
Input additional comments, if any, for the renewal.
Note
: Use the
History
section view the history of previous renewals.
Grid
(This sections appears if a valuation is already added.
Note
: In this section, Use
Add Valuation
to add another valuation and
Waive Off OtherValuation
to waive off a valuation.)
Collateral ID, GlobalCollateral Number, PacketNumber, Number of Packets,Tray Number, Vault Number,Valuation By, Valuer Name,Final Valuation Amount LTV(%), Loan Amount (INR)
Displays the respective details in the fi elds.
Considered
In case of multiple valuations, the radio button shows whichvaluation is being considered.
Actions
Displays the icons of the actions that could be performed.
View Details
Click to view the details in the pop up.
Customer Exposure
Click this button to generate the consumer gold/ornamentexposure report.
Click
Save
to save the details.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors(if any) are notifi ed.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 384/766
Pre Sanction Visit (PSV)
This stage is an intermediary stage for SLI applications. In this stage, the group and applicantdetails captured in the previous stages are reviewed and the fi nance applications are processedaccordingly. The approved applications are sent to the Recommendation stage.
Note:
Only the Credit offi cer and Sales Personal Discussion fi elds can be edited in the PSVstage. All the other details displayed are as captured in the previous stages and are read only.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,
Customer Name,
Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Key Product Processor, Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for eachapplication.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popupscreen.
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Pre Sanction Visit
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Pre Sanction Visit
stage.
Any existing information on this screen can be viewed under
Applicant Information
,
Documents
,
Domain Check
,
Summary Analysis
,
Finance Details, Regulatory Categorization, Related/Controlling Person, Checklist, Upload, CropDetails
and
SHG Details
as explained in
Detailed Data Entry
.
Go to
Finance Details
tab >>
Sourcing
tab to select the Credit Offi cer to whom the application is mapped.
Note:
The Credit Offi cer fi eld requires an authority based access and will be displayed to userswith appropriate authority.
Go to the
Sales
Personal Discussion
tab to review the applicant details.
Note:
The
Personal Discussion
tab can be confi gured and the fi elds that appear in this tab aredynamic fi elds that are also confi gured as per the requirements.
The decision as
Approve
,
Reject
and
Recommend
can be mentioned on the Personal Discussion tab along with the
PDDone On
date.
Click
Save
to save the details.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors(if any) are notifi ed.
Note
: Apart from the tabs that are explained above,there are many miscellaneous tabs thatexist in the system and may appear as per the product defi nition confi guration. In such cases,information relevant for those tabs can be viewed and modifi ed as well.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 385/766
More Actions on Application
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing.
On successfully completing theaction,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
hesystem redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listedeither in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
Task Assignment Master
.
Note:
A notifi cation is sent to inform the Relationship Manager regarding the current stage of theapplication.
An automatic mail is sent to the primary e-mail ID captured while creating the application.
To Reject the Application
Click
More Options >> Reject Application
to send the Application to
Rejection Queue
.
On the pop up screen that appears, select
Decision Reason
(reason for rejection) from thedrop down list and click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
messageappears on the screen and the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any)are notifi ed.
On the
Applications
screen, click
Reject
tab to view the rejected Application in the grid. The
Status
of the rejected Application is displayed as
REJECT REVIEW
.
Click
Send Back
in the
Actions
column, to send the Application back to the lead user. Or, toremove the Application from the system, click
Reject
in the
Actions
column.
For
Decision
as
Reject,
the Application moves to
REJECTION QUEUE
and can be viewed using
Rejected Applications
option. Also, on rejection, the system automatically generates anotifi cation informing the user about the rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
To Put an Application on Hold
Click
More Actions >>
Hold Application.
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Application stops until the Application is onHold.
Move Application to Next Stage
Reject Application

Reason(s)
*
for hold from thedrop down list.
Specify the
Reassignment Date, Time of Reassign
and
Remarks
, if any.

: The application can be put on hold for the current date as well.

Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a

message appears on the screenand the system redirects to the

screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.

Applications
screen, go to

tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. TheStatus of the Application is displayed as

.

:

click

Number
in the grid andthen click

More Actions >>
Workfl ow History Records
to view the workfl ow history of theApplication.

pop-up screen appears showing the list of all the activitiesperformed on the application.

More Actions >>
Raise Query
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.

in the Action column of the grid.

from the drop down list and input

above the grid.

Policy Referral/Execution
The purpose of this stage depends on how a bank or FI wants to use it within its applicationprocessing workfl ow. This stage can either be used to check results of diff erent policies that wereexecuted while moving the application from a particular stage, or it can only be used whenpolicies execution gets failed after a particular stage. In both cases, a bank user needs to take adecision on whether the application should be rejected or sent for further processing.
This page displays the all rules associated with the policy and only the authorised users can viewthe details of these rules.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,
Customer Name,
Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions,
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Policy Referral
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Policy Referral
stage.
On the
Policy Referral
page, check results of diff erent executed policies, and the reason for failure for failed policies, if any.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 392/766
The
Policy Set Rule
section displays the list of rules along with their description and shows the Expected and Actualresults of each rule.
Accordingly, choose
Decision
*
as either
Approve or Reject
from the drop down list. Also,
choose a
Reason
*
to justify theselected decision from the drop down list.

Description
fi
eld gets populated automatically based on the selected Reason and is read only.
Also, input
Decision
Comments
, if required.
Click
Save
to save the decision.

a

message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.

Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Note
:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.
To Unquery
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
Select
Reason
from the drop down list and input
Comments
.
Click
Done
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk unquery by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Unquery
above the grid.
To Reassign Query
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
To Reassign a query user has to select either
Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
orcombination of both.
Input
Remarks
and click
Reassign Query
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk reassign by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Reassign
above the grid.
Raise a Query
Re Run Priority

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 395/766

Click
More Actions >>

to update the priority of the application on the basis ofchanged parameters.
Collateral Investigation Initiation (CII)
Collateral Investigation
is done to verify the collateral information shared by the applicant.
Following are some of the Verifi cation Types that are pre-confi gured in the FinnOne Neo CAS:
Legal Verifi cation
(applicable only for Property collateral type)
Valuation
In case the verifi cation is not required, there is an option to waive off the verifi cation/valuation.
You can also add the
Document
tab at
Collateral Investigation Initiation (CII)
screen as per thebusiness requirement.
Note
: In case the application is rejected or cancelled, the system allows the user or agency tocomplete the Collateral Investigation Verifi cation.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Neo Cust ID,Identifi cation No., Customer Name, Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type RequestType, Phone No., Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
foreach application.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
CII
page.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
CII
stage.
On the
Collateral Investigation Initiation
page, the grid displays
Collateral Number
,
Collateral Type
,
Collateral SubType
,
Last Verifi cation Date,
Current Verifi cation Status, Initiated On
and
Updated On
for each applicant.
On the
Collateral Investigation Initiation
page, user can perform the following tasks:
Wave Off Valuation/Verifi cation
: Click to wave off the respective collateral investigation verifi cation orvaluation.
On the pop up screen that appears, select a
Reason Code
for waiving off the verifi cation.
ReasonDescription
will be populated automatically based on the
Reason Code
selected.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 396/766
Wave Off All:
Click to wave off all the collateral investigation verifi cations or valuations.
On the pop up screen that appears, select a
Reason Code
for waiving off all the verifi cations.
ReasonDescription
will be populated automatically based on the
Reason Code
selected.
Initiate Verifi cation
: Click to initiate the verifi cation or valuation process.
At the time of initiation, the application can be assigned to the agency manually or automaticallythrough the predefi ned allocation strategy as confi gured in the system.
On the
Collateral Investigation
popup screen that appears, set the following fi elds:
Fields Name
Description
Verifi cation Type
*
Select the type of verifi cation to be initiated for the customer.
Note
: The available Verifi cation Types and their respectivefi elds to be captured are confi gurable in the system. The systemallows you to create a Dynamic Form in which the fi elds that arerequired to be captured for a particular
Verifi cation Type
can bedefi ned (using
Workfl ow >> Create Form Confi guration
option).This Form can be mapped by the System Administrator to therequired Product and Verifi cation Type (using
SystemAdministrator >>Collateral Type Dynamic Form Mapping
option).
Agency
Select the agency for the verifi cation.
Note
: The list of Agencies get fi ltered on the basis of themapped Verifi cation Type, City/PinCode and Product.
Actions
Click
to delete the added verifi cation.
Auto-Allocation
Check to automatically allocates an appropriate agency to theverifi cation type.
By default, it remain checked and cannot be edited.
Show History
Click to view the history of verifi cation for the collateral.
Initiate Verifi cation
Click to initiate the verifi cation/valuation for the collateral.
Note
: A notifi cation is sent to the agency to which theverifi cation is assigned.
Click to add more verifi cations for the collateral.
Valuation
(Use this section to capture the agency for collateral valuation.)
Click to add more valuations for the collateral.
Agency
Select the agency for the valuation.
Note
: The list of Agencies get fi ltered on the basis of themapped Verifi cation Type, City/PinCode and Product.
Field(s) Name and Description

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 397/766
Actions
Click
to delete the added valuation.
Auto-Allocation
Check to automatically allocates an appropriate agency to thevaluation type.
By default, it remain checked and cannot be edited.
Show History
Click to view the history of valuation for the collateral.
Initiate Verifi cation
Click to initiate the valuation for the collateral.
Note
: A notifi cation is sent to the agency to which thevaluation is assigned.

: Applications that are already moved to the

link to display the customer detail on

page.

Valuation Completion
After processing the Valuation of Collateral at the
Collateral Investigation
stage, the lead movesto the Valuation Completion stage for completion purpose. At this stage, an Application usercalculates the
Market Value
of the Collateral based on the valuation done by
single
or
multiple
agencies. Also, there is a provision to
Re-initiate
the Valuation and assign it to
single or multiple
agencies.
Note
: In case the application is rejected or cancelled, the system allows the user or agency tocomplete the Collateral Investigation.
Attachments
Decision
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 402/766
To complete the valuation for a Collateral, follow these steps:
Go to
Applications >>
Valuation Completion
.
On Collateral Valuation Completion screen that appears, the grid displays
Application ID
,
Collateral Number
,
CollateralType
,
Collateral SubType, Initiated On, Updated On
and
Actions
for each Collateral Valuation processed at
CollateralInvestigation
stage.
In the grid, locate the required
Collateral Number
with
Complete
status, and click the
Calculation
link in the
Actions
column.
On the screen that appears, the grid displays
Valuation Agency
,
Evaluated By
,
Evaluation Method
,
Valuation Date
,
Evaluated Value
and
Status
for the Valuation done by each Agency (as captured at the
Collateral Investigation
stage).
To calculate the market value, choose
Evaluated Value Type
*
as either
Min, Max, Avg or Others
from the drop down list.
Value
*
is calculated automatically by the system based on the selected
Evaluated Value Type
and is read-only.
For
Evaluated Value Type
as
Others
, manually enter the
Value
*
. Also, set the Currency for this fi eld.
The
Last Valuation Date
and
Accepted Value
fi elds are auto-populated by the system and are read-only fi elds.
OLV Grid Value
gets auto populate in case the Assignment Matrix is maintained.
Note
:
OLV Grid Value
is calculated on basis of the parameters defi ned in Assignment Matrixalong with other fi elds value captured during transaction.
Input
Insured Declare Value
and
Overall Accept Value
and select
Overall Evaluated value Type
from the respective fi elds.
Optionally, click
Documents
to view the current status and other details of documents. In addition, click
Raise Query
toraise a query associated with the application, if any.
Click
Save
to complete and close the Valuation in the system.
Based on the
Value
calculated using Valuation Completion option, the
Market Value
of the Collateral is automaticallypopulated in the Application.
To Re-Initiate the Valuation
In order to assign this Valuation to
another
Agency or
multiple
Agencies, you can
Re-Initiate
Valuation
by following thesesteps.
1.
Click
Re-Initiate
Valuation
.
2.
On the pop up screen that appears, select
Agency
from the drop down list.
Click
to add multiple agencies. Or, to
remove
an existing agency, click the corresponding
icon.
3.
Click
Show History
to view a log of the Valuations previously done for this Collateral.
On the History screen that appears, the grid displays
Valuation Agency
,
Evaluated By
,
Evaluation Method
,
Valuation Date
,
Evaluated Value
and
Status
for each Valuation.
4.
Click
Close
to return to the previous screen.
5.
Click
Initiate Valuation
, once all the required Agencies have been added for Valuation. The Valuation initiatedhere can be processed using
Collateral Investigation
option.

CollateralInvestigation
Collateral Investigation Completion (CIC)
Once the collateral investigation is done, the application appears in Collateral InvestigationCompletion (CIC) page to complete the collateral investigation process. Only the authorized useris eligible to mark the process as completed.
To complete the valuation for a Collateral, follow these steps:
Go to
Applications >>
Investigation >>
Collateral Investigation Completion
.
On the
Collateral Investigation Completion
screen that appears, the grid displays
Application ID
,
Collateral ID
,
Collateral Number
,
Verifi cation Type
,
Collateral Type
,
Collateral SubType, Initiated On, Last Updated On
and
Actions
for each Collateral investigation processed.
In the grid, locate the required
application
and click the
Application ID
link.
If the
Verifi cation Type
of the application is
Verifi cation
, the
Collateral Verifi cation Completion
page appears.
If the
Verifi cation Type
of the application is
Valuation
, the
Collateral Valuation Completion
page appears.
Collateral Verifi cation Completion
On the
Collateral Verifi cation Completion
page, the grid displays the details, such as Verifi cation Agency, Verifi cationAgent, Verifi cation Type, Verifi cation Date, Remarks, Result, and Status of all the verifi cations done on the selectedapplication.
To complete the verifi cation, set the following fi elds:
Fields Name
Description
Decision
*
Select a decision from the drop down list.
Reason
*
Select a reason from the drop down list.
Reason Description
Displays description of the selected reason.
Click to add other additional reasons.
Click to delete the additional reasons.
Decision Comment
Input the comments, if any.
Raise Query
Click to raise a query at this stage. On clicking, a pop-up screenappears in which enter the details as mentioned in the
RaiseQuery
section.
Show History
Click to view history of the collateral investigation.
Field(s) Name and Description

Click to re-initiate the verifi cation process.
Save
Click to save the changes and selected values.
Save and Proceed
Click to save the details and complete the verifi cation process.
On successfully completing the action, a notifi cation appears on the screen accordingly. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
If required, the authorised user can reopen the completed investigation by clicking the
Reopen
link in the
Status
Column.The
Reason Code
*
needs to be mentioned in the

popup that appears.

Displays the accepted evaluated value type.

Displays the accepted value.
Last Valuation Date
Displays last valuation date of the collateral.

RaiseQuery
section.

Save

Click to save the details and complete the valuation process.
On successfully completing the action, a notifi cation appears on the screen accordingly. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.

link in the

*
needs to be mentioned in the

popup that appears.

Field Investigation Initiation (FII)
Field Investigation
or
Contact Point Verifi cation (CPV)
is done to verify the information sharedby the applicant.
Following are some of the Verifi cation Types that are pre-confi gured in the FinnOne Neo CAS:
Personal Verifi cation
Alternate Business Verifi cation
Business Verifi cation
CA Assessment
Property Verifi cation
Residential Verifi cation
Borrower Eligibility Verifi cation
In case the verifi cation is not required, there is an option to waive off the verifi cation.
Note
: In case the application is rejected or cancelled, the system allows the user or agency tocomplete the FI Verifi cation.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Neo Cust ID,Identifi cation No., Customer Name, Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, RequestType, Phone No., Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
foreach application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popupscreen.
If you have the required authorizations, the system will display the following searchoptions on the grid:
Type of Cases:
Input the type of case you want to search, whether initiated or cancelled.
Agency Name:
Input the name of the agency to which the case is allocated.
The criteria for search results displayed are the combination of both the above inputs.
If cancelled cases are searched using the
Type of Cases
option as described above, the
Bulk Re-Initiate
button is activated.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
FII
screen.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 406/766
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
FII
stage.
On this screen, the grid displays
Neo
Cust ID
,
Type
,

,
Last Verifi cation Date
and

for each applicant.
On the
Field Investigation Initiation
page, user can perform the following tasks:
Wave Off Verifi cation
: Click to wave off the respective fi eld investigation verifi cation.
On the pop up screen that appears, select the
Verifi cation Type
to waive off the type wise verifi cationand select a

for waiving off the verifi cation.

will be populatedautomatically based on the
Reason Code
selected.

Click to wave off all the fi eld investigation verifi cations.
On the pop up screen that appears, select a
Reason Code
for waiving off all the verifi cations.
ReasonDescription
will be populated automatically based on the
Reason Code
selected.
Initiate Verifi cation
: Click to initiate the verifi cation process.
At the time of initiation, the application can be assigned to the agency manually or automaticallythrough the predefi ned allocation strategy as confi gured in the system.
Note
: If you have the required authorizations, you can use the Bulk Re-initiation option toinitiate verifi cation for the selected verifi cation type and assigned agency.
On the verifi cation popup screen that appears, set the following fi elds:
Fields Name
Description
Verifi cation Type
*
Select the type of verifi cation to be initiated for the customer.
Note
: The available Verifi cation Types and their respectivefi elds to be captured are confi gurable in the system. The systemallows you to create a Dynamic Form in which the fi elds that arerequired to be captured for a particular
Verifi cation Type
can bedefi ned (using
Workfl ow >> Create Form Confi guration
option).This Form can be mapped by the System Administrator to therequired Product and Verifi cation Type (using
SystemAdministrator >>FIV Dynamic Form Confi guration
option).
Purpose
*
Select the verifi cation purpose from the drop-down list.
Agency
Select the agency for the verifi cation.
Note
: The list of Agencies get fi ltered on the basis of themapped Verifi cation Type, City/PinCode and Product.
Verifi cation Initiated On
Displays the date and time on which the verifi cation is initiated.
Address
Displays the address of the selected agency.
Remarks
Input remarks, if required.
Status
Displays the current status of the verifi cation process.
User can click
to delete the verifi cation.
Auto-Allocation
Check to automatically allocates an appropriate agency to theverifi cation type.
Field(s) Name and Description

By default, it remain checked and cannot be edited.

Initiate Verifi cation

A popup box appears displaying the addresses of the applicantfor selecting and initiation the verifi cation.
Note
: A notifi cation is sent to the agency to which theverifi cation is assigned.
Cancel
Click to cancel the verifi cation initiation process.
Click to add more verifi cations for the customer.

: Applications that are already moved to the
Field Investigation Verifi cation

.

Field Investigation
page appears displaying the list of applications.
Click the
Application ID
link to display the customer detail on
Field Investigation Initiation
page.
On the
Field Investigation Initiation
page, user can initiate the verifi cation and view its details.

.

for hold from thedrop down list.

and

, if any.

FI Allocation Grid
For
Verifi cation Agency
Use Only
Once the fi eld investigation of the applicant(s) has been initiated at the
Field InvestigationInitiation
stage, the agency uses the
FI Allocation Grid
page to further allocate the verifi cationcases to its agents.
To allocate verifi cations to agents, follow these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Investigation >>
FI Allocation Grid
.
On the
Field Investigation
page that appears, search Applications with pending verifi cation using
Application ID, NeoCust ID, Customer Name, Product Type, Zip Code
and
Verifi cation Type
.
If required, check the
Advance Search
option to include more search parameters like
FI Initiation Date, Sales Offi cer,Source Branch, Assigned To, Product, Scheme/Card Type
etc.
The search results appear in the grid below. The grid displays
Verifi cation Type, Application Number, Neo Customer Id,Applicant/BP Name, Agency Name, Assigned To, Product Type, Product, Scheme/Card Type, Sales Offi cer, Branch, ZipCode, FI Date and Date of Birth
for each application.
In the grid, locate the application that needs to be assigned to an agent, and click
in its corresponding
Assigned To
column. Or, use check box to select the application and click the
Assign To
button.
Any single or multiple applications that appears in the grid, can be assigned to a agent. The check box in the girdheader, the
Assign All
button allows assigning multiple applications to a agent at the same time.
In the
Assign User to Selected Application
popup page that appears, locate the required user who is need to be assignedthe application(s) and use the corresponding
Select
button to assign application to him/her.
Click
Done
to save the assignment.
Re Run Priority

On successful action, a
Success
message notifying about the action performed appears on the screen and the systemredirects to the
Field Investigation
screen. Else, errors if any, are notifi ed accordingly. Also the Application can be seenlisted in
Agent Field Investigation Verifi cation
screen where the agent process its verifi cation.
Agent Field Investigation Verifi cation
For
Verifi cation Agent
Use Only
Once the agency assigns applications to an agent using the
FI Allocation Grid
page, the sameapplications can be seen listed by the specifi c agent on this page to mark/capture the verifi cationdetails and results of the investigation.
To mark result of verifi cation, follow these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Investigation >>
Agent Field Investigation Verifi cation
.
Note
: The
Agent
Field Investigation Verifi cation
option is visible only to the agents withappropriate rights.
On
Agent Field Investigation Verifi cation
screen that appears, the grid displays
Verifi cation Type, Application Number,Customer Number
and
Applicant Name
for each application assigned to the agent.
In the grid, use
Application Number
to locate the
Application
whose verifi cation needs to be processed.
Click
Verifi cation Type
of the located Application to input the investigation details specifi c to it.
Note
: The
available Verifi cation Types and their respective fi elds to be captured areconfi gurable in the system. The system allows you to create a dynamic Form in which the fi eldsthat are required to be captured for a particular
Verifi cation Type
can be defi ned (using
Workfl ow>> Create Form Confi guration
option). This Form can be then be mapped by the SystemAdministrator to the required Product and Verifi cation Type (using
System Administrator >>FIVDynamic Form Confi guration
option).
In the
Attachments
section, click
Add Attachment
to attach multiple verifi cation documents to latermake them available as reference for marking the user verifi cation as complete in the next stage.
In the
Decision
section, input details related to the verifi cation activity performed by the agency.
Field Name
Field Value
Verifi cation Agency
*
and Verifi cationAgent
*
Choose verifi cation agency and agent from therespective drop down list.

*
Input the number of attempts that were made by theagency to verify the applicant information.
Verifi cation Date
*
and Time of Visit
Input the date and time of verifi cation as it took place.
Result
*
Choose as either
POSITIVE, NEUTRAL or NEGATIVE
fromthe drop down list.
Remarks
Input any additional information as free text, ifrequired.

For both

and

Once the fi eld investigation of the applicant(s) has been initiated at the
Field InvestigationInitiation
stage, the user with appropriate rights can mark/capture the verifi cation details andresult of the investigation using this screen.
To mark/capture the details and result of the investigation, follow these steps:

Applications >> Investigation >>
Field Investigation Verifi cation
.

: The
Field Investigation Verifi cation
option is visible only to the user with appropriaterights.
On
Field Investigation
page that appears, the grid displays

and
Applicant Name
for each Verifi cation Type initiated at the FII stage.

: To perform bulk reallocation of multiple cases to another agency, select the check boxesof the required records and click
Reallocate
at the top of the grid. In the Bulk ReAllocation pop-up window that appears, input the Verifi cation type and Agency to bulk reallocate.
In the grid, use
Application Number
to locate the
Application
whose verifi cation has been initiated at
Field InvestigationInitiation
stage.
Click the
Verifi cation Type
hyperlink of the located application to input its respective verifi cation details.

can bedefi ned (using

option). This form can be then bemapped by the System Administrator to the required Product and Verifi cation Type (using

. You can alsoinput the details.

master.

Note
An authorized user with appropriate permissions can cancel Field Investigation Verifi cation by eitherselecting the check box of Field Investigations and clicking
Cancel FI
that appears above the grid o
r byclicking the Verifi cation Type link and clicking

Field Investigation Completion (FIC)
After the details of the verifi cation activity have been captured by the allocated agency or agent,the Application moves to
Field Investigation Completion
(FIC) stage.
At this stage, an Application user can view the history and details of the verifi cation for eachapplicant along with the attached verifi cation documents. Based on these details, the user canthen mark the fi nal decision to complete the Field Investigation activity for the Application. Ifrequired, the system also provides option to re-initiate any verifi cation.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,Customer Name, Stage, Phone No., Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Product Type, Product Name
and
Created On
,
for each application.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Field Investigation Details
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
FIC
stage.
On the
Field Investigation Details
screen, the grid displays
Applicant Name
,
Applicant Type
,
Verifi cation Date
,
Verifi cationType
,
Verifi cation Agency
,
Verifi cation Agent
and
Result
for each verifi cation type performed for this Application.
On this screen, the system also display applicants for which the
Field Investigation
was
Waived-Off
along with the
Waive-Off Reason
.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 415/766
Click
View Details
in the
Result
column to view the Field Investigation Verifi cation details ad captured at the previousstage.
In the
Decision
section, choose
Decision
*
as either
APPROVE or REJECT
from the drop down list.
Choose
Reason
*
from the drop down list.
The
Reason Description
fi eld is populated automatically based on the selected Reason. This fi eld is read-only.
Add free text in the
Decision Comments
fi eld to capture any additional information regarding the decision.
Click
Save
to save the details.

Recommendation (Loans)
At this stage, a bank user provides a decision as
Recommended
or
Not
Recommended
on thefi nance application, after verifying the
Finance Information
,
Applicant Information
,
AbilityIndicators
and the results of
Eligibility Policy Setup
and
Intent
Indicators
like Credit ApprovalPolicy, Credit Bureau Check, RCU, Collateral Verifi cation, KYC Check, Credit Score, FieldInvestigation.
If required, a bank user can also edit and add details in certain sections as described below andcan also re-initiate any activity or check.
Note
: Always run a
RCU
screening on the Application, before working on this stage.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,
Customer Name,
Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Recommendation
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Recommendation
stage.
The
Recommendation
page displays following tabs to view, edit or capture any required information:
Section/Sub-Section Description and Usage
Updated Details
Check this to update the existing details at this stage as per theconfi gurations.
Note
: Only the user with appropriate authority can updatethe details.
Loan Information section
(
This section displays Product, Scheme, Amount, Installment, Tenure, Purpose of Loan andprovides links to view or modify Sourcing Details, Loan Parameters, Receipt Details, FinancialSummary, GST, Cattle Details (for Agriculture Loans) and Property/Asset Details.
Recommendation
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 419/766
Note
: In case of omni loan user can delete omni sub loans on pre approval stages and basedon confi guration policies and calculations can be re executed.)
Applicant Information section
(
This section displays Name, Age, Cust ID and Marital Status of the Primary Applicant. It alsoprovides Applicant Details link to view or modify all the information captured for PrimaryApplication or Co-Applicant/Guarantor (if any).
)
Ability Indicators section
(
This section display the ability indicators calculated by the system for the Applicant like, LTV(Loan To Value ratio), IIR (Installment to Income Ratio), Aggregate LTV and DBR (Debt BurdenRatio). It also, provides the Financial Details link to view or modify all the fi nancial informationpreviously captured under Income Details, Deductions, Assets, Liabilities and the Income ExpenseSummary.
)
Exposure Details section
This section displays the Exposure Details, received from LMS or External Service, of PrimaryApplicants, Co-applicant and Guarantor of an application so that the data can be used forfurther calculation.
The exposure details of each individual including liability details can beviewed by clicking the customer name link. This section also provides a button to Re-Initiatethe Exposure Details and a link to view the breakup of Exposure Details of the applicant.
Intent Indicators section
(
This section displays the results for Credit Approval Policy, Field Investigation, CollateralVerifi cation, RCU
,
Credit Bureau Check, Dedupe Check and Credit Score run.
These validations can be re-run by the user, if required.
Additionally, this section also provides option to generate (view and download) CAM (CreditApproval Memo) Report that displays the application information that has been captured till thisstage and the Collected Document button to view the documents collected for the Application.
)
Underwriter Decision tab >> Miscellaneous Information section
(
This section displays the miscellaneous information captured in previous stages.)
Decision/Forward History section
(
Use this section to view log of decisions taken on the Application in the previous stages and theforward history of the Application
.
)
Approval Checklist section
(
Use this section to check the status of the points listed in the Approval Checklist and markresponse against each.
Based on the response marked, the Deviations for the Application are raised automatically bythe system.
A response can be marked as either
Yes
,
No
or
NA
against any checklist point.
Click
Submit/Update Deviations
and the system automatically raises the Deviations based onresponse marked. These Deviations can be viewed in the
Approve Deviations tab
.
Optionally, click the

screening on the Application, before working on this stage.

Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.

Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like

,
for each application.

:

placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.

:

How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click

of a particular application to navigate to the

screen.

: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Recommendation
stage.

page, fi rst go to the following sections to view, edit or add information.

and

details for a bank user to view or updateinformation.

as part of intent indicators. A user can also re-initiate the activities , if required.

documents
.

helps in taking a decision
This section displays the Exposure Details, received from LMS or External Service, of PrimaryApplicants, Co-applicant and Guarantor of an application so that the data can be used for furthercalculation.

,

and

ascalculated by the system. The user also has an option to re-run the Limit and FOIR calculationsas done by the system.

To unhold a Application,
click
Application
Number
in the grid andthen click
More Options >> Unhold
.
Only user with appropriate authority can hold/unhold the applications.
To Raise a Query
Click
More Actions >>
Raise Query
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
Hold Application
Raise a Query
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 434/766
Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Note
:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.
To Unquery
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
Select
Reason
from the drop down list and input
Comments
.
Click
Done
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk unquery by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Unquery
above the grid.
To Reassign Query
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
To Reassign a query user has to select either
Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
orcombination of both.
Input
Remarks
and click
Reassign Query
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk reassign by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Reassign
above the grid.
See Next
Credit Approval
Credit Approval (Loans)
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 435/766
Note
: In case of a Consumer Durable Finance, the application only lands at this stage if thesystem has been confi gured for the manual approval process
. To know more, see
Credit ApprovalFor Consumer Durable Loans
.
At the Credit Approval stage, the
Credit Approver
or
Underwriter
evaluates the results of thevarious checks performed on the fi nance application and then accordingly takes a credit decisionfor sanctioning the amount as requested by customer.
At this stage, the system displays all the captured information and the verifi cation details on asingle screen.
At this stage, the Credit Approver/Underwriter can take the following actions on the Application:
Mark the approval decision as either
In-Principle Approve, Approve or Reject
andprovide a detailed justifi cation for the decision so that there is a record of the sameavailable in the system.
Note
:
The decision is marked as
In-Principle Approve
for thoseProperty Based Finance Applications where the Property has not beenidentifi ed yet. After
In-Principle Approval
, the Application moves to
Incomplete Collateral Details Activity
.
Mark responses for the
Approval Checklist
questions based on which the deviationsmay be raised.
Approve the assigned deviations raised from
Credit Approval Policy
Check
. Also,raise deviations manually, if applicable.
Generate
Credit Approval Memo (CAM)
, to be referred by a Underwriter.
Add
Special Condition(s)
that need to be met at further stages of the Applicationprocessing.
View results of
Credit Approval Policy Check,
Dedupe Check, Field Investigation
.These checks can also be re-run , if required.
Note
:
If any of the confi gured parameters is changed at Credit Approval,the system will prompt the user to re-initiate fi eld investigation whenmoving the application to the next stage.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Credit Approval
.
The
Assigned
and
Pool
tab appear in the grid displaying details like
Application ID,
Finance Account Number, ApplicationForm Number, Neo Cust ID, Identifi cation No., Customer Name,
Finance Amount, Stage, Current Stage Timestamp,Query Status, Query Responded, In-Queue Status, Previous User, Product Type, Product Name, Scheme, AccountType, Request Type, Phone No., Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority, DisbursalStatus, Group Description, Image Based Processing, Product Processor, Credit , Offi cer, Agreement Booklet SNo, FIStatus, Collateral Status, RCU Status and Actions
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
Show/ Hide
placed at the top right of the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate
to the
Credit Approval
screen for applicationprocessing.
On
Credit Approval
page, displays following tabs to view, edit or capture any required information:
Underwriter Decision

Application Details
This section displays details of the application. User can click
,
CAM Report
and
Application Summary
to generate and view the respective reports. Moreover, click verticalellipses (
) to view the details captured in Sourcing Details, Repayment Parameters, PropertyDetails, Financial Summary, Personal Discussion and Collateral Details.
Note
: There arevarious confi gurations in CAS to display the Application Summary either for all product types orfor some specifi c product types. Moreover, in case of omni loan user can delete omni sub loanson pre approval stages and based on confi guration policies and calculations can be re executed.
View Summary
(Click View Summary,to view and comparethe documents.)
Purpose of loan
Displays the purpose of the loan.
Loan Amount
Displays the total amount of the loan.
ROI/Tenure
Displays the ROI and tenure of the loan.
Installment
Displays the loan installment
Eligibility
Displays total eligibility amount.
Total Exposure

Note
: Click
to refresh the amount and click ellipses (...) toview the total exposure and posidex exposure and view ExposureHistory in the pop-up screen.
Score
Displays score of the application.

Re RunPolicy
.
Note
: In case of Omni Loan application, the credit policy runsseparately on omni level and product level.

This section displays details of the application. User can click
CAM Report
and
ApplicationSummary
to generate and view the respective reports. Moreover, click vertical ellipses (
) toview the details captured in Sourcing Details, Repayment Parameters and Receipt Details.

Displays the amount for which the applicant is eligible.
Total Exposure
Displays the exposure amount.
Click ellipses (
...
) to view the exposure details, exposure history,Posidex exposure. Click
or Re Initiate link to re initiate andupdate the exposure details.

to re-run the FI. There is also a provision to Waive Off asingle FI of Wave Off All the FIs, if required.

to re-run the RCU to reinitiate the RCU process forselected documents where the RCU status is either

or

. This allows the user to reinitiate RCU for caseswith discrepancies.

) to view thematched and Re Run the dedupe process.

...

: Click
Re-initiate
to re-initiate the credit score of therespective applicant.
Exposure
Displays the exposure amount.

: In addition to these fi elds, if more ability indicators are mapped, then they will appearin the section, if applicable.
BSA Details
This section displays the Bank Statement Analyzer (BSA) details and allows to edit the BSAquestions also.
Applicant/ Group Name
Displays the name of the applicant or group.
Banking Score
Displays the
banking score of the applicant or the group.
Questions
Displays the
BSA questions, which are editable also.
Deal Limit Details
This section displays the deal limit details of selected deal.
Requested Deal Amount
Displays the deal amount requested in the application.
Requested Fund BasedAmount
Displays the fund based amount requested in the application.
Requested Non-Fund BasedAmount
Displays the non-fund based amount requested in the application.
Sanctioned Deal Amount
Displays the amount sanctioned for the application.
Sanctioned Fund BasedAmount
Displays the fund based amount sanctioned for the application.
Sanctioned Non-Fund BasedAmount
Displays the non-fund based amount sanctioned for theapplication.
Deal Expiry Date
Displays the deal expiry date.
Actions
Click
to update the Deal Expiry Date and Next Review Date inthe side panel that appears. Click
Save
to save the updateddetails.
Click
More Details
link to view other details on the
Deals & Facility Details
tab.
Facility Details
This section displays the facility details.
Facility ID
Displays unique facility ID.
Product Type, Product,Scheme
Displays product type, product and scheme associated with thefacility in the respective columns.
Facility Type, Facility Group,Facility Sub Group
Display type, group and sub group of the facility.
Parent Facility ID
Displays the parent facility in case the facility level is child.
Requested Facility Details(Amount / Rate / Tenure)
Displays requested amount, rate and tenure of the facility.
Sanctioned Facility Details(Amount / Rate / Tenure)
Displays sanctioned amount, rate and tenure of the facility.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 450/766
Actions
Click
to update the facility sanction details in the side panelthat appears. Click
Save
to save the updated details.
Click
More Details
link to view other details on the
Deals & Facility Details
tab.
Stock Details
This section displays the stock details as captured in the previous stages.
Facility ID
Displays unique ID of the cash credit facility.
Collateral ID
Displays unique collateral ID.
Collateral Number
Displays global collateral number.
Collateral Sub Type
Display sub type of the collateral. In this case, the sub type mustbe
Stock and Book Debts
.
Stock as on Date
Displays the date for which the stock details were captured.
Stock Eligible for DP
Displays amount of stock eligible for DP.
Debtor Eligible for DP
Displays the amount of debtors eligible for DP.
Total Stock Value
Displays the total stock value on the mentioned date.
Max Drawing Power
Displays the maximum drawing power of the customer.
Bill Details
This section displays the bill details as captured in the previous stages.
Facility ID
Displays unique facility ID.
Collateral ID
Displays unique collateral ID.
Collateral Number
Displays global collateral number.
Collateral Sub Type
Display sub type of the collateral. In this case, the sub type mustbe
Bills
.
No. of Bill
Displays the number of bills captured for the facility.
Bill Amount
Displays the total bill amount.
Documents Details
This section displays the details of documents collected/captured in previous stages of theapplication and also provides an option to collect the pending required documents.
Deal/Facility ID
Displays the deal or facility ID associated with the loanapplication.
This column appears in case of the Multi Facility loan only.
Applicants/Collateral
Displays the name of the applicant or collateral to whom/whichthe document is associated.
Loan Documents
Displays the number of required documents and receiveddocuments related to the loan application.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 451/766
Applicant Documents
Displays the number of required documents and receiveddocuments related to the applicants.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Collateral Documents
Displays the number of required documents and receiveddocuments related to the collateral.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Other Documents
Displays the number of other required and received documents.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Actions
Click the
View Details
link to view the existing documents as wellas collect the pending documents.
Cattle Details
This section displays the details of cattle captured in previous stages of the application. Thissection appears in case of Agriculture Loan only.
Cattle Type
Displays the type of the cattle.
Breed
Displays the breed of the cattle.
No. of Cattles
Displays the number of cattle.
Cattle Cost
Displays the cost of cattle.
Scale of Finance
Displays the scale of fi nance.
Percentage
Displays the percentage.
District
Displays the district of the cattle.
State
Displays the state of the cattle.
Recommended Limit
Displays the recommended limit for cattle.
Land Holding Details
This section displays the details of land holdings and lease out lands captured in previous stagesof the application. This section appears in case of Agriculture Loan only.
Applicant
Displays the name of the applicant who own the land or to whomthe land is on lease.
Ownership Type
Displays the ownership type of the applicant with the land.
State
Displays the
state in which the land is located.
District/Tehsil/Village
Displays the district, tehsil and village
in which the land is located.
Survey No.
Displays the survey number in which the land is surveyed.
Land Area (Acres)
Displays the total area of the land.
Irrigated
Displays the land area that is used for irrigation.
Non Irrigated
Displays the land area that is not used for irrigation.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 452/766
Deviation Details
This section is used to view and approve or reject the deviations raised in the system. Thissection displays the deviations in following categories:
Credit
: Displays the credit deviations, which is Approved/Rejected by theinternal/external credit team.
Business
: Displays the sales deviations, which is Approved/Rejected by theinternal/external sales team.
Approval Sheet
: The user can raise the deviations and add users in Through, To andSeen categories to authorize the deviations. In this section, a consolidated status alsoappears that displays the details of Through, To and Seen users along with the decisioncomments.
Deviation
Displays the name of the deviation.
Deviation Level
Displays the level of the deviation.
Source System
Displays the source system to which the deviation is associated.
Status
Displays the current status of the deviation.
Mitigant
Input the mitigant for the respective deviation.
Click the
Other Mitigants
link to defi ne other mitigants for thedeviation.
Note
: The
Other Mitigants
link appears for the deviations towhich the
Other Mitigants
check box is selected while defi ningthe deviations.
Comment
Input free-text as remarks, if any .
Note
: Comments for deviations can be updated by any userwhether taking any decision or not.
Assigned To
Displays the name of the user to whom the deviation is assigned.
Stage
Displays the stage on which the deviation was raised.
Entity Name
Displays the name of the entity.
Action Date
Displays the date on which last action was performed on thedeviation.
Action By
Displays name of the user who performed some action on thedeviation.
Actions
Displays the icon to delete the deviation.
View History
Click this link to view the deviation history.
Raise Deviation
Click to manually raise deviations.
Re Run Policy
Click re run and refresh the deviation policy.
Re Run External Deviation
Click to re run the external deviation.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 453/766
Non Critical Deviation
Check if the deviation is a non critical deviation.
Note
: All non critical deviations are auto approved by thesystem.
Asset Approval Decision List section
(
This section appears only in case
Multiple Assets, Consumer Durables
and
Farm Equipment
loan application types and it displays the list of Assets Requested by the Applicant. Anunderwriter is required to either approve or reject any single, multiple or all requested assets.)
Miscellaneous Information
This section displays miscellaneous information captured in the previous stages of theapplication, such as Current Business Date and NSDL response.
Decision History
This section displays the following details in separate tab:
Decision history
: Displays the log of decisions taken on the Application in the previousstages of the Application
.
Forward History
: Displays the log of the forward history of the Application
.
Committee Decision History:
Displays
the details of members of the committee and listof committee decisions taken along with comments and special conditions
.
Limit Details
This section displays the following details in separate tab:
General
: Displays the Limit Details inputted in the previous stages of the application,such as Dealer/Manufacturer, Code, Amount Limit (Exposed/Maximum), Count Limit(Exposed/Maximum), DPD Allowed (Exposed/Maximum), Linked Loan Exposure Limit,Document Name and Document Ageing.
Special
: Displays the special details of the Application
.
Underwriter Decision
Click
at the bottom right side of the screen and provide details in the following sections:
Justifi cation
: Use this section to input strengths and weaknesses of the applicant andthe executive summary.
Special Condition
: Use this section to add single or multiple Special Conditions to theapplication, applicant or collateral that must be met till the specifi ed Application Stage.These conditions are tracked at post approval and disbursal activities
. There is aprovision to enable the users with appropriate authorities to edit and delete the addedspecial conditions.
A
sset Approval
: Use this section to view the details of multiple assets, such as AssetName, Dealer Name, Total Asset Cost, Credit Days, LTV and also view and update theAmount Approved for the assets.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 454/766
Course Approval:
Use this section to view the course fee details and update theapproved amount in the respective fi eld. In addition, click the
Payment
link and inputthe amount in case the course fee is to be paid in multiple payments.
Note
: This section appears in case of Education Loan only.
Tentative Disbursal Schedule Entry
: Use this section to view and add the tentativedisbursal schedule in case of multiple disbursal loan. The user can add details such asTranche/ Disbursal Number, Tranche Disbursal Date, Total Tranche Payment, FinanceRequested (%), Finance Requested (Amount), Approved Tranche Payment, FinanceApproved (%), Finance Approved (Amount) and Margin Money.
Note
: This section appears in case of Education Loan only.
Tranche Schedule:
Use this section to defi ne the disbursal date and amount for thetranche and also displays the requested and approved amount.
Approval Checklist
: Use this section to check the status of the points listed in theApproval Checklist and mark response against each. Based on the response marked,the Deviations for the Application are raised automatically by the system. A responsecan be marked as either
Yes
,
No
or
NA
against any checklist point.
Member Approval
: Use this section to view and update the approved loan amount andDelink/Relink members by specifying valid reasons. Also, there is an option to view thehistory of relinking/delinking the members. The
Default Identifi cation Number
alsoappears along with the name of each member.
Decision
: Use this section to mark the credit approval decision as either
Approve
,
Reject
or
In-Principle Approve
and specify the drawing power, loan amount, tenureand the interest rate that should be sanctioned for this Application.
Onapproval/rejection, the system automatically generates a notifi cation, informing theuser about the approval/rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
In case of Multi Facility loan, select the single or multiple facilities in the grid and markthe Decision as either Approve or Reject and select a Reason from the drop down list. Inaddition, select the
Sanctioned Date
and
Sanctioned Expiry Date
in the respectivefi elds.
This section also allows the credit underwriter to
Forward
the application to anotherunderwriter to provide consent or approval for the same,
Hold
the application (alsomention the hold reason, reassignment date and time and remarks),
InitiateCommittee Decision
to allocate the loan application to a committee which can bebased on the defi ned Assignment Matrix or one that is selected by the user in
Committee Code
*
fi eld,
Auto Initiate Committee Decision
to automatically initiate thecommittee approval process.
Note
: I
n case of loan accounts booked under Overdraft product type, input the limitreview frequency and next review date. The Limit Expiry Date is auto populated basedon the repayment schedule.

).

: This is an authority-based feature which is visible onlyto users with the requisite authority.

Bank Details
section,then the detail of all the banks get fetched based on theidentifi cation details provided by the customer.
If details of multiple banks are provided in the
Bank Details
section, then details of the selected bank(s) record getfetched.
Info
: A service is created that searches for the bankaccounts linked with the Identifi cation details and bankaccount details provided by the customer and fetches thedetail of all the bank accounts that are linked with thoseidentifi cation details.
