Expected Value
Input the expected value.
Actual Value
Displays the actual value.
Question Checklist
(Use this section to capture the responses of the required questions maintained in the respectivemaster.)
Question Code
Displays unique code of the question.
Question Description
Displays description of the question.
Response
Input the question response. The fi elds in the column appearsas per the type of the question.
Comment/Remark
Input any comment or remarks.
Penalty Details
(Use this section to capture the additional penal interest and penal application method.)
Additional Penal Interest
Input the additional penal interest.
Penal Application Method
Select the penal application method as either
Prospective
or
R
etrospective
.
Charge Details
(Use this section to capture the charges for the covenant.)
Penalty Charge Code
Input the penalty charge code.
Rate/Amount Based
Select whether the charge is rate based or amount based.
Candidate Attribute
Input the candidate attribute.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 463/766
Charge Amount/Rate(%)
Input the charge amount or charge rate percentage.
Waiver Amount/Rate (%)
Input the waiver amount or charge rate percentage.
Actual Charge Amount/Rate (%)
Displays the actual charge amount or charge rate percentage.
Status
(Use this section to capture the status of the covenant.)
Status,
Sub Status,
Final Status
*
Select the status, sub status and fi nal status of the covenantfrom the respective drop down lists.
Committed Date
Displays the committed date of the covenant.
Breach Closure Date
Select the breach closure date of the covenant.
Course of Action/Remarks
Input the course of action or remarks related to the covenant.
Covenant History
(This section displays history of the covenants.)
Click
Save
to save the details.
A
Success
message appears on the screen notifying about the action performed. Else, errors if any, are notifi edaccordingly.

*
for hold from thedrop down list.

and

, if any.

. On successfully completing the action,

screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.

screen, go to

tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. TheStatus of the Application is displayed as

.

:
To unhold a Application,

Number
in the grid andthen click

.

to

he

.

in the Action column of the grid.

*
which auto populates the

*
.

to add multiple reasons and click
in the

columnto delete the reason.

and Click

.

.

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 467/766
Click to view limit details of Customer Group, Deal and Facility in the pop-up screen
Credit Approval (Consumer Finance)
In case of a Consumer Finance, if the system has been confi gured for the manual approvalprocess, the application lands at the Credit Approval stage. At this stage, the
Credit Approver
or
Underwriter
evaluates the results of the various checks performed on the fi nance applicationand then accordingly takes a credit decision for sanctioning the amount as requested bycustomer.
At this stage, the system displays the saved proposal information and the verifi cation details on asingle screen for a Credit Approver or Underwriter, to take the following actions on theApplication:
Mark the approval decision as either
Approve or Reject
and provide a detailedjustifi cation for the decision.
Approve the assigned deviations raised from
Credit Policy
Check
. Also, raisedeviations manually, if applicable, a
View result details of
Credit Policy Check
,
Credit Bureau Check
,
Dedupe Check
, and
Telephonic Verifi cation
. These checks can also be re-run, if required.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Credit Approval
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Neo Cust ID,Identifi cation No., Customer Name, Stage,
In-Queue Status,
Product Type,
Product Name,
Account Type,
RequestType, Phone No., Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
foreach application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Credit Approval
screen for applicationprocessing.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Credit Approval
stage.
On
Credit Approval
screen, view details under the
Proposal Information,
and
Evaluation Results
sections respectively.
View or modify the details in these sections as required.
Limit Tree

section.
Field Name
Field Description
Decision Details section
(
Use this section to mark the credit approval decision as either
Approve
or
Reject
and specifythe loan amount, tenure and the interest rate that should be sanctioned for this Application.
Also, on approval/rejection, the system automatically generates a notifi cation, informing theuser about the approval/rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
)
Sanction Amount
Input the amount that should be sanctioned for this application.
Sanctioned Tenure
Input sanctioned tenure in months.
Sanctioned Interest
Choose
Sanctioned Interest
that should be applicable on the loan aseither
Scheme Based or Other
.
Also input the
Interest Rate
.
Decision
*
Select decision as either
Approve
or
Reject
.
In case of
Approve
, also set the
Sanction Date
*
and
Sanction ExpiryDate
.
Reason
*
Select a reason for the selected decision.
Decision Commentsand Remarks
Input text to justify the decision taken.
Go to the
Personal Discussion
tab to view or modify details specifi c to the personal sales and credit discussion.
Note:
The
Personal Discussion
tab can be confi gured to appear or not for all the fi nance types.Moreover, the fi elds appear in this tab are dynamic fi elds that are also confi gured as per therequirements.

Save
to save the details.

On
Credit Approval
page, displays following tabs to view, edit or capture any required information:
Section/Sub-Section Description and Usage
Applicant Details
This section displays the details of the applicant in the following tabs:
Personal
: Displays the personal details of the applicant, such as Name, Age, Gender,Date Of Birth, Constitution, Nationality and Resident Type.
Employment
: Displays the employment details of the applicant, such as OccupationType, Employer Name, Industry, Employment Type, Employment Status and TotalWork Experience.
Income
: Displays the income details of the applicant, such as Monthly Net Income,Annual Net Income, Other Income and Annual Gross Income.
Bank Account
: Displays the bank account details of the applicant, such as BankName, Bank Branch, IFSC, Type of Account, Account Number, Average balance andAccount Status.
Other Credit Card
: Displays the details of other credit cards issued to the applicant.such as Card Issuer, Card Number, Card Limit and Card Expiry.
Applicant's Profi le
This section displays details of the Primary Applicant and also provides options to view or modifyall the information captured for Primary Application or Co-Applicant/Guarantor (if any).
Note
: Any existing information under
Applicant Information
can be updated as explained in
Lead Details
.
Applicants
Displays the customer details, such as applicant name, age,employment type, applicant type and mobile number.
CIF Number
Displays the customer identifi cation number of the applicant.
Credit Bureau
Displays the credit score of the applicant.
Know Your Customer
Displays the KYC details of the applicant.
FI
Displays the FI details of the applicant.
RCU
Displays the RCU details of the applicant.
Dedupe
Displays the dedupe process result. Click ellipses (
...
) to view thematched and Re Run the dedupe process.
Score(In./Ex.)
Displays the credit score of the applicant.
Underwriter Decision
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 473/766
Note
: Click
Re-initiate
to re-initiate the credit score of therespective applicant.
Exposure
Displays the exposure amount.
Click ellipses (
...
) to view the exposure details, exposure historyand Re Initiate to update the exposure details.
Work Experience
Displays the total work experience of the applicant.
Monthly Income
Displays the monthly income of the applicant.
Residential Status
Displays the residential status of the applicant.
Residential Vintage
Displays the number of year the applicant is living in the specifi edresidence.
Negative Area
Displays the whether applicant lives in a negative area or not.
Account No
Displays the account number of the applicant.
Account Balance
Displays the account balance of the applicant.
Eligibility
Displays the amount for which the applicant is eligible.
Click to view the following details of the applicant:
View Details
: Select this option to view the detailedinformation of the applicant, such as PersonalInformation, Employment Details, Financial Details andBank/Credit Card Details.
Applicant Details
: Select this option to view the basicdetails of the applicant, such as name, cust ID, mobilenumber and so on.
Ability Indicators
This section displays the ability indicators calculated by the system for the Applicant like, LTV(Loan To Value ratio), IIR (Installment to Income Ratio), Aggregate LTV and DBR (Debt BurdenRatio).
Gross LTV
Displays the gross loan to value ratio of the applicant.
DBR
Displays the Debt Burden Ratio of the applicant.
IIR
Displays the Installment to Income Ratio of the applicant.
Aggregate LTV
Displays the aggregate loan to value ratio of the applicant.
Net LTV
Displays the net loan to value ratio of the applicant.
Cheque Bounce
Displays the amount of the bounced cheque of the applicant.
ABB/EMI
Displays the Average Bank Balance (ABB) and Equation MonthlyInstallments (EMI).
Average Credit
Displays the average credit of the applicant.
Note
: In addition to these fi elds, if more ability indicators are mapped, then they will appearin the section, if applicable.
Credit Card Details
This section displays the details of the credit card issued to the applicant including parent andchild corporate credit cards.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 474/766
Card Type
Displays the type of the credit card.
Network Gateway
Displays the network gateways used for making transaction withthe credit card.
Card Limit
Displays the limit of the credit card.
Balance Transfer
Click ellipses (...) to view the additional information, such asIssuer Name, From Card, Name On Card and Expires On.
Auto Debit
Click ellipses (...) to view the auto debit information, such as BankName, Branch Name, Auto Debit Acc No. and Debit Type.
Money Transfer
Click ellipses (...) to view the money transfer information, such asBank Name, Branch Name, Money Transfer Acc No. and Amount.
AddOn
Displays the add on amount on the credit card.
Note:
This fi eld does not appear for corporate credit cards.
More Details
Displays the additional details.
Click ellipses (...) to view the additional information, such as CardType, Network Gateway and Card Limit.
Add On Card
This section displays the details of any add on credit card issued to the applicant.
Name
Displays the name of the add on card holder.
Relationship
Displays the relationship of the primary applicant with the add oncard holder.
Card Type
Displays the type of the add on card.
Network Gateway
Displays the network gateways of the add on card.
Card Limit
Displays the limit of the add on card.
Chip Card
Displays whether the add on card is a chip card or not.
Expires On
Displays the expiry date of the add on card.
Documents Details
This section displays the details of documents collected/captured in previous stages of theapplication and also provides an option to collect the pending required documents.
Applicant
Displays the name of the applicant to whom the document isassociated.
Loan Documents
Displays the number of required documents and receiveddocuments related to the loan application.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Applicant Documents
Displays the number of required documents and receiveddocuments related to the applicants.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Collateral Documents
Displays the number of required documents and receiveddocuments related to the collateral.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Other Documents
Displays the number of other required and received documents.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 475/766
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Collect Document
Click this link to collect the required pending documents.
Deviation Details
This section is used to view and approve or reject the deviations raised in the system. Thissection displays the deviations in following categories:
Credit
: Displays the credit deviations, which is Approved/Rejected by theinternal/external credit team.
Business
: Displays the sales deviations, which is Approved/Rejected by theinternal/external sales team.
Approval Sheet
: The user can raise the deviations and add users in Through, To andSeen categories to authorize the deviations. In this section, a consolidated statusalso appears that displays the details of Through, To and Seen users along with thedecision comments.
Deviation Name
Displays the name of the deviation.
Deviation Level
Displays the level of the deviation.
Source System
Displays the source system to which the deviation is associated.
Assigned To
Displays the name of the user to whom the deviation is assigned.
Entity Name
Displays the name of the entity.
Status
Displays the current status of the deviation.
Action Date
Displays the date on which last action was performed on thedeviation.
Action By
Displays name of the user who performed some action on thedeviation.
Actions
Displays the icon to delete the deviation.
View History
Click this link to view the deviation history.
Raise Deviation
Click to manually raise deviations.
Re Run Policy
Click re run and refresh the deviation policy.
Re Run External Deviation
Click to re run the external deviation.
Non Critical Deviation
Check if the deviation is a non critical deviation.
Note
: All non critical deviations are auto approved by thesystem.
Decision History
This section displays the following details in separate tab:
Decision history
: Displays the log of decisions taken on the Application in the previousstages of the Application
.
Forward History
: Displays the log of the forward history of the Application
.
Committee Decision History:
Displays
the details of members of the committee and listof committee decisions taken along with comments and special conditions
.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 476/766
Underwriter Decision
Click
at the bottom right side of the screen and provide details in the following sections:
Special Condition
: Use this section to add single or multiple Special Conditions tothe application or applicant that must be met till the specifi ed Application Stage.These conditions are tracked at post approval and disbursal activities
.
Card Decision
: Use this section to mark the decision on cards as either Approve orReject and also specify the Approved Limit.
Decision
: Use this section to mark the credit approval decision as either
Approve
or
Reject
and specify the reason for the respective action.
On approval/rejection, thesystem automatically generates a notifi cation, informing the user about theapproval/rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
This section also allows the credit underwriter to
Forward
the application toanother underwriter to provide consent or approval for the same and
Hold
theapplication (also mention the hold reason, reassignment date and time andremarks).
On the
Credit Approval
page, view all the information as displayed under
Off er
,
Key Credentials
,
Customer
Address/WorkDetails
,
FI/TVR
,
Checks
and
Checks With Negative Result
sections on the screen.
If required, the credit manager can also raise deviations and mark mitigants against all pending deviations in the
Non-Financial Authorizations
and
Mitigants
sections respectively.
Note
: The system doesn't allows to move the Application to the next stage, if there are anypending deviations.
In the
Recommendation Verdict
section, view the decision and its reason, as taken by a bank user at the
Recommendation
stage.
In the
Linkages
section, if the customer already holds a card or an account in the same bank, select
Existing Customer
as
Yes
and also choose a
Customer ID
from the drop down list.
Also, in this case, select whether the
New Account Number
is required or not.
Finally, in the
Decision
section, choose
Approve
or
Reject
as required and input comments to justify the decision taken. Incase of
Approve
, also set the
Sanction Date
*
.
On approval/rejection, the system automatically generates a notifi cation, informing the user about theapproval/rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
A credit manager can also
forward
the Application to another user, to get input on certain parameters that doesn't fallunder his/her authority. For example, if there are any pending deviations that can only be approved by any other userof higher authority.
Also, if required, use the
Generate PDF
button to download the CAM report in PDF format.
Click
Save
to save the decision.

to
add the Application in the

list.

pop-up screen that appears, select

gets automatically populated.

Additionally, add the additional comments and click

.

a
Success
message appears on the screen andthe system redirects to the

screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.

1.
Go to
Applications >> Cancelled Applications
.
2.
Click
Application ID
link to view the details of the cancelled application.
3.
On the
Review Cancelled Application
page, select and action from the Select Actiondrop-down list as either
Confi rm Cancellation
or
Send Back for Processing to activity
.
In case
Send Back for Processing to activity
. is selected, select an activity from the
Select Activity
drop-down list to send to the application to that stage.

and click

SMS
or

.

Workfl ow History Records

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 478/766
Click
More Actions >>
Hold Application.
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Application stops until the Application is onHold.
On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold from thedrop down list.
Specify the
Reassignment Date, Time of Reassign
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
: The application can be put on hold for the current date as well.
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date. On thisdate the hold on the Application gets removed and it is re-assigned to theApplication user.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screenand the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
On the
Applications
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. TheStatus of the Application is displayed as
HOLD
.
Note
:
To unhold a Application,
click
Application
Number
in the grid andthen click
More Options >> Unhold
.
Only user with appropriate authority can hold/unhold the applications.
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing.
On successfully completing theaction,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
hesystem redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listedeither in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
Task Assignment Master
.
Note:
A notifi cation is sent to inform the Relationship Manager regarding the current stage of theapplication.
An automatic mail is sent to the primary e-mail ID captured while creating the application.

and

in therespective fi elds.

fi eld for whom the query is being raised.

4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 480/766
Use this stage to validate the serial number of the asset, before disbursal. A service is called,which integrates with third party system to identify whether the asset is original or not.
Note
: A serial number once used for an asset cannot be entered again.
Follow these steps to validate the asset serial number :
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
On the
Applications
page, in the
Assigned, Pool and Hold
tabs appear and the grid on these tabs displays the followingdetail:
Columns
Description
Application ID
Displays the unique application ID clicking which user can viewthe application details and further process the application.
Neo Cust ID
Displays the customer ID associated with the respectiveapplication.
Customer Name
Displays the name of the customer.
Stage
Displays the current stage of the application.
In-Queue Status
Displays the status of the application whether the application isnewly introduced or returned from a stage.
Product Type
Displays the product type associated with the respectiveapplication.
Product Name
Displays the product name associated with the respectiveapplication.
Account Type
Displays the account type as
FAS
,
FOS
, or
BOTH
in case of the FASapplication.
Request Type
Displays the application request type.
Phone No.
Displays the phone number of the customer.
Sourcing Date
Displays the date on which the application is created.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the channel through which the application is created.
Key Product Processor
Displays the product processor for the application. For example:Internal, mCAS and so on.
Branch
Displays the branch at which the application is created.
Assignee
Displays the name of the user to whom the application assigned.
Created By
Displays the name of the user who created the application.
Priority
Displays the priority of the application.
Actions
Click
Reassign For User
to assign the application to another useror click
Assign To Pool
to assign the application to pool.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Asset Validation
page.
Grid Columns and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 481/766
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Asset Validation
stage.
On the
Asset Validation
page, set the following fi elds :
Columns
Description
Asset Category
Displays the category of the asset, as captured while creatingthe proposal.
Manufacturer
Displays the manufacturer o the asset, as captured whilecreating the proposal..
Asset Model
Displays the model of the asset, as captured while creating theproposal.
Asset Variant
Displays the variant of the asset, as captured while creating theproposal.
Asset Serial Number
Input the asset serial number.
Note
: This fi elds is non editable only if the serial number hasbeen captured and validated in previous stages.
Is Validated
The check box appears ticked if the asset serial number hasalready been validated.
Actions
Click
Validate
to validate the asset serial number. A notifi cationappears based on successful or failed validation of the assetserial number
Click
Save
to save the captured details.

The Applications that have been '
Approved In-Principle'
at the Credit Approval stage move to
ICD
stage. At this stage, the user can capture the details of the property, which was previouslymarked as Non-Identifi ed.

Applications
.

Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like

,
for each application.

:

SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.

:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.

Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the

screenfor application processing.

: Use the
Stage
column to make sure the selected application is at the
IncompleteCollateral Details Activity
stage.

child tab and the

(

:
(a) TCT stands for Transfer Certifi cate of Title and CCT stands forCondominium Certifi cate of Title.
(b) A Lot is a portion of a subdivision, tract, or other parcel ofland considered as a unit for the purpose of transferring legaltitle from one person or entity to another.
Percentage Share

Current Usage
Choose the current usage of the property from the drop downlist.

(

Update checklist

Reference
Type
,
Entity
Type
and
Documents
. The additional document canalso be marked as
Mandatory
.
Download

Download All
todownload the selected document(s).
Note
:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Documents

: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:

Save as PDF
, if required.

and selecting the required option.

Pending (
)

and mark the document as

, if required.

)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:

)
: Select when the document is no more required from the customer.Also, select the

and input the

*
and other

in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark the document as
Mandatory
.
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by the customeron a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Note
:
To mark the status of multiple documents, select multipledocuments using the check box, click
Mark Status
that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.
Incomplete Doc (
): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.

: Click
to add images of the documentsthat are
Received

section atthe right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent version of the documents

for hold from thedrop down list.

and

, if any.

To Re Run priority

Once
ICD activity
is completed, the Application either moves back to
Credit Approval Stage
for re-approval by theunderwriter or it moves to
Post Approval Activity Stage
depending on the next activity rules.
See Next
Post ApprovalActivity
See Also
Credit Approval
Reconsideration
As the Application is
approved
or
in-principle approved
, it moves to the
Reconsideration
stage.
At this stage, an underwriter can review the decision, and edit details, if required using the
Update Details
check box. At this stage, an underwriter can capture additional remarks butcannot change or take a decision on the Application.
Additionally, an underwriter can also download a CAM Report,which
provides a summary of thefi nance details entered by diff erent users at diff erent stages and helps in taking a decision. Ifrequired, an underwriter can also regenerate the Sanction Letter in case of Personal FinanceProduct Type.
Based on the conditions and rules as specifi ed in workfl ow confi guration, the Application eithermoves back to the
Credit Approval
stage or it moves to the
next
stage.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,Customer Name, Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type,
Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Reconsideration
screen for applicationprocessing.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 495/766
Note
: Use the
Stage
column to make sure the selected application is at the
Reconsideration
stage.
On the
Reconsideration
page, check/uncheck the
Update Details
check box, if required and go to the following sections toview or edit details respectively.
Section Description and Usage
Loan Information section
(Editable)
(This section displays Product, Scheme, Amount, Installment, Tenure, Purpose of Loan andprovides links to view Sourcing Details, Loan Parameters, Receipt Details, Property/Asset Details,Financial Summary, Personal Discussion and Collateral Details)
Applicant Information section
(Editable)
(This section displays Name, Age, Cust ID, CIF Number and Marital Status of the PrimaryApplicant. It also provides Applicant Details link to view all the information captured for PrimaryApplication or Co-Applicant/Guarantor (if any). It also provides an option to add a Co-Applicant/Guarantor (if required) )
Ability Indicators section
(Read-Only)
(
This section display the ability indicators calculated by the system for the Applicant like, LTV(Loan To Value ratio), IIR (Installment to Income Ratio), Aggregate LTV and DBR (Debt BurdenRatio).
)
Underwriter Decision tab >> Exposure Details section
This section displays the Exposure Details, received from LMS or External Service, of PrimaryApplicants, Co-applicant and Guarantor of an application so that the data can be used forfurther calculation.
The exposure details of each individual including liability details can beviewed by clicking the customer name link. The section also provides a button to Re-Initiatethe Exposure Details and a link to view the breakup of Exposure Details of the applicant.
Intent Indicators section
(Editable)
(
This section displays the results for Credit Approval Policy run, Field Investigation, CollateralVerifi cation, RCU
,
Credit Bureau Check, KYC Check,
and Credit Score.
These validations can be re-run by the Approver/Underwriter, if required.
Additionally, this section also provides option to generate CAM (Credit Approval Memo) Reportthat displays the application information that has been captured till this stage and the CollectedDocument button to view the documents collected for the Application.
)
Underwriter Decision tab >> Miscellaneous Information section
(
This section displays the miscellaneous information captured in previous stages.)
Decision/Forward History section
(Read-Only)
(
Use this section to view log of decisions taken on the Application in the previous stages and theforward history of the Application
.
)
Approval Checklist section
(Editable)
(
Use this section to view or edit the response marked against each of the points listed in theApproval Checklist as marked at the Credit Approval stage.
Click
Submit/Update Deviations
, if edited
and the system automatically raises the Deviationsbased on the response marked.
Optionally, click the
Print Checklist
link to view and print the checklist of current as well theprevious stages on which the checklist is marked.)
Decision Justifi cation section
(Editable)
(
Use this section to view or edit the strengths and weaknesses of the applicant and the executive
Section(s) Name and Usage

summary
.
)

(
Use this section to view the result of eligibility calculation for the Primary Applicant based onthe eligibility set mapped to the product/ scheme
.
It also provides an option to recalculate eligibility, if required.)
Decision Details section
(
Use this section to view the credit approval decision as marked at the Credit Approval stage.The decision marked cannot be changed.
But, the loan amount, tenure and the interest rate can be edited, if required.
This section off ers the following additional options:
Allows an underwriter to add
Special Condition
to the Application that must be met tillthe specifi ed Application Stage.
Also, incase of deleting a special condition that wasadded prior to this stage, it is mandatory to add a note code in the Activity drop downsection, so that it can be tracked in the system, if required.
T
here is a provision to
Forward
the application to another underwriter to provideconsent or approval for the same
.
The user can click
Raise Deviation
to manually raise the Credit or Business deviations,re-run deviation policy and view history of the deviations raise internally or externally.
The user can click
Approval Sheet
to raise the deviations and add users in Through, Toand Seen categories to authorize the deviations.
The user can click
Hold
to hold the application. On clicking
Hold
, mention the holdreason, reassignment date and time and remarks in the pop-up screen.
The user can click
Initiate Committee Approval
to allocate the loan application to acommittee which can be based on the defi ned Assignment Matrix or one that isselected by the user. The Committees listed for selection would be fi ltered based onProduct Type, Branch, Product and Scheme. If the Committee Mapping master is notmaintained, then all the committees would be listed for selection.
The user can view
Decision
*
,
Sanction
Date
*
,

Expiry Date
,

Date
*
,

,

,

*

*
and

.

:

pop-up screen appears showing the list of all the activitiesperformed on the application.
To Cancel the Application
Click
More Actions >> Cancel Application
to
add the Application in the
Cancelled Applications
list.
On the
Cancel Application
pop-up screen that appears, select
Reason Code
(reason forcancellation) from the drop down list. The
Reason Description
gets automatically populated.
1.
Additionally, add the additional comments and click
Done
.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen andthe system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
To View the Cancelled Application:
1.
Go to
Applications >> Cancelled Applications
.
2.
Click
Application ID
link to view the details of the cancelled application.
3.
On the
Review Cancelled Application
page, select and action from the Select Actiondrop-down list as either
Confi rm Cancellation
or
Send Back for Processing to activity
.
In case
Send Back for Processing to activity
. is selected, select an activity from the
Select Activity
drop-down list to send to the application to that stage.

Cancel Application

4.

Remarks
and click

.

SMS
or

.
To Put an Application on Hold
Click
More Actions >>
Hold Application.
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Application stops until the Application is onHold.
On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold from thedrop down list.
Specify the
Reassignment Date, Time of Reassign
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
: The application can be put on hold for the current date as well.
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date. On thisdate the hold on the Application gets removed and it is re-assigned to theApplication user.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screenand the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
On the
Applications
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. TheStatus of the Application is displayed as
HOLD
.
Note
:
To unhold a Application,
click
Application
Number
in the grid andthen click
More Options >> Unhold
.
Only user with appropriate authority can hold/unhold the applications.
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing.
On successfully completing theaction,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
hesystem redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listedeither in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
Task Assignment Master
.
Note:
A notifi cation is sent to inform the Relationship Manager regarding the current stage of theapplication.
An automatic mail is sent to the primary e-mail ID captured while creating the application.
Hold Application
Move Application to Next Stage

To Raise a Query
Click
More Actions >>
Raise Query
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Note
:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.
To Unquery
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
Select
Reason
from the drop down list and input
Comments
.
Click
Done
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk unquery by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Unquery
above the grid.
To Reassign Query
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
To Reassign a query user has to select either
Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
orcombination of both.
Input
Remarks
and click
Reassign Query
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk reassign by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Reassign
above the grid.
Raise a Query
Re Run Priority

The Applications approved at the
Credit Approval
stage then moves to

stagefor getting the operations checklist fi lled. After the Application gets approved at this stage, itmoves to

.
Also, at this stage, the following additional actions can be taken on the Application, if required:

CCDE
stage.
Move the Application back to
Recommendation
stage.

stage.

drop down list. For Omni Loans youcan send multiple credit cards to the Card Management System (CMS).

Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.

Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,
Customer Name,
Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for each application.

:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click

placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.

:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Operations Check
screen for applicationprocessing.
Note
: Use the
Stage
column to make sure the selected application is at the
Operations Check
stage
.
On the
Operations Check
screen, specify answers for all checklist questions as displayed on the screen.
Select
Decision
as
Approve
or
Reject
and select a
Reason
to justify the decision taken.
If required, also input the decision comments.
Click
Save
to save the details.

Click

to
move the Application to
Card Management System
.
In case, where the Card Management System is connected online to the CAS system, theApplication directly moves to Card Management System.
Or,
Where there is no online connectivity between the Card Management System and CAS System,the Application moves to the
Send to Card Management System
interface, from where theApplication can be manually sent to the Card Management System.

Send To CardManagementSystem

Applicable only for
Finance
Product Types
The Applications approved at the
Credit Approval
stage then moves to
Post Approval Activity
stage. At this stage, the following post approval actions can be taken to process the applicationfor disbursal:
Post Approval Documents collection
Special Conditions tracking
Instrument Details capturing
This activity is generic for Finance Product types.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.

*

(

.
)
(

to choose the charges from the

Charge Nature
for each selected charge.

Click to collect the chosen charges.
In case of Non collectible and Deductible method of charge, it is non-mandatory to collect the IMD or short receive from the disbursement.

Displays the diff erential amount for the charges that can be editable.

Displays the diff erential rate for the charges that can be editable.
Eff ective ChargeAmount
Displays the eff ective charge amount.

: After specifying all the loan parameters, the system calculates and displays the
CapitalFunded
amount. Click

to calculate the installment amount.

Finance
and

VAPs linked to the loan account, the system displaysa consolidated value.

Click to view details of the default and optional building payment plans asdefi ned in the Building master. The Building Payment Plan pop-up isdisplayed on clicking the button.
Column Name
Description

Displays the uniquepayment plan code.
Name
Displays the payment planname.

4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 517/766

CalculateInstallment
Click to calculate the installment amount and generate
RepaymentSchedule
based on the specifi ed loan parameters. This fi eld appearsdisabled.
Note
: In case of multiple installments, the Installment value thatpopulates on the screen is the same installment value that appears in thefi rst row of the repayment schedule.
Combinedrepayment schedulewith VAP
Click to view the repayment schedule combined with VAP linked with theloan account.
Combinedrepayment schedule
Click to view the combined repayment schedule.
View RepaymentSchedule
Click to view the
Repayment Schedule
on a separate screen. This fi eld isenabled for single disbursal type loan cases.
The Repayment Schedule as generated here is a consolidated scheduleincase of Multiple Asset Loan Application Type and Any Loan Applicationlinked with VAP.
Repayment Upload
Click to open the Repayment Schedule Upload pop-up. The user canupload and edit repayment schedule on this pop-up screen. There is alsoprovision to create structured schedule on the screen itself without usingthe Upload option. This is enabled for structured loans.
In the Repayment Schedule pop-up, the user can Browse and select therepayment schedule or drag and drop in the space provided. The usercan then input
Remarks
* fi eld and click
Upload
. The RepaymentSchedule will be uploaded. In the Edit Repayment Schedule section, theuser can view and edit the uploaded repayment schedule. This sectionhas the following fi elds and columns:
Field Name
Description
Loan Amount/VAPAmount
Displays the total loan amount.
Tenure
*
Displays the tenure of the loan.
Sheet Name
*
Displays the name of the excelsheet uploaded.
Interest Start Date
*
Displays the date on which theinterest calculation was started.This fi eld can be edited.
First InstallmentDate
*
Displays the fi rst installmentdate.
Auto-Correct
Click to auto-correct.

and

ocuments

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 534/766
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.
Cancel Rescan Request
: Click the
Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.
Request Status
: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
Columns Name
Description
Initiator Name
Displays the name of the user whoinitiated the rescan request.
Initiation Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is initiated.
Completion Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is completed.
Requested Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescan request is initiated.
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Completed Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescanning request iscompleted
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Remarks
Displays the remarks to rescan thedocument.
Cancel Remarks
Displays the remarks to cancel therescanning request.
Status
Displays the current status of the rescanrequest.
Action
Click the
Cancel Request
link to cancel allthe available rescan requests.
Click the
Show Cancelled Details
link toview the details of the cancelled rescanrequests.

View Summary
: Use this option to displays the summary of all the documents of theloan application. User can
Print
the summary and
Save as PDF
, if required.
View and Select Documents
: List of documents are displayed in the left pane groupedby Customer and Applicant. Select the document to change the status of the documentin the middle pane.
Link and Swap Images
: The document images can be linked and swapped with otherdocument images by clicking the
and selecting the required option.
Update Document Status
: Select a document in the left pane and select any of thefollowing status:
Pending (
)
: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Received On
*
Select the date on which the document isreceived.
Original/Photocopy
Select Original if the original document isreceived or select Photocopy if the photocopyof the document is received.
Verifi cation Status
*
Select a verifi cation of the document.
Processing State
Select a processing state of the document.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
WaivedOff (
)
: Select when the document is no more required from the customer.Also, select the
Waive Off Reasons
and input the
Waive Off Remarks
*
and other
Remarks
in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark the document as
Mandatory
.
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by the customeron a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Deferred Reasons
Select reasons to mark this document asdeferred.
Tentative Date
*
Select a date on which this document isexpected to be received.
Deferred Remarks
*
Input remarks of marking the document asdeferred.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
Fields Name and Description
Fields Name and Description

that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.
The documents that are marked as RCU Eligible are displayed in thedocument list with RCU identifi cation tick.
Incomplete Doc (
): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.
Complete Doc
: Click to mark an incomplete document as complete. This option appearsonly if a received documents has been marked as incomplete.
Add, View, Edit and Compare Documents
: Click
to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are
Received
. User can view and edit the image in the
DocumentViewer
section at the right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent versionof the documents and their details. Moreover, there is a drop down list with the name ofall the uploaded images. Select the fi le to view in the document viewer.
Note
: Only users with the appropriate authority can view thedocument images.
Once the details of the documents is updated, click
Submit
.

The system displays the details of the signer.
Initiate
Click
button to initiate the E-sign request.
The system initiates the E-sign request for the document.
On successfully completing the action, a Success messageappears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
State
*
Displays the name of the state for which e-stamping isapplicable.
Denomination
*
Displays the stamp value/ denomination for e-stamping.
Change Stamp Details
Mark the check box if you want to Change the Stamp Details.
The State and Denomination fi eld gets enabled.
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
While processing, the system also splits the applicationas per the applicable
Multiple Assets Single Application Policy
.
Also, t
he system redirects back to the
Application
screen,where the Application can been seen listed either in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
TaskAssignment Master
.
On clicking
Move to Next Stage
, the Sanction Letter/Delivery Order gets generated automatically (based onconfi guration) and appears in the
Customer Communication History
section of the next stage. To print a generatedreport, right click anywhere in the report and select
Print
. The print option appears, only if the user has the printingauthorities.
To Generate CAM Report
Click
to
manually generate the CAM report.

1.

.

1.

Applications >> Cancelled Applications
.
2.

Application ID
link to view the details of the cancelled application.

On the
Review Cancelled Application
page, select and action from the Select Actiondrop-down list as either
Confi rm Cancellation
or
Send Back for Processing to activity
.
In case
Send Back for Processing to activity
. is selected, select an activity from the

drop-down list to send to the application to that stage.

and click

.

SMS
or
Email
.

for hold from thedrop down list.

pop-up screen that appears, input

and
Query
in therespective fi elds.

fi eld for whom the query is being raised.

link to view the query details.

in the Action column of the grid.

from the drop down list and input

to update the priority of the application on the basis ofchanged parameters.
To Generate a Delivery Order
Click
More Actions >>
Generate Delivery Order
to generate the delivery order report.
Note
: Only authorized users can generate and/or print the Delivery Order.
A Delivery Order in PDF format is generated and saved in the Customer CommunicationHistory section. The user can also send the Delivery Order as communication for each
Re Run Priority
Generate Delivery Order
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 548/766
disbursal.
Note
: In case asset wise split is mentioned in the application, the systemgenerates Delivery Orders for each asset. Similarly, for dealer wise split in theapplication, a Delivery Order is generated for every dealer with page breaksinserted for every asset in case the application also contains multiple assets.
Note
: The user can send the Delivery Order as a communication wheninitiating disbursal for loans with multiple tranches. Delivery orders can alsobe sent to dealers of approved tranches in case some of the tranches of aloan are still pending approval.
Note
: To print a generated delivery order, right clickanywhere in the report and select
Print
.
The print option appears, only if the user has the printingauthorities.

Click
More Actions >>
Generate Sanction Letter
to generate the sanction letter of theapplication.

: Only authorized users can generate and/or print the Delivery Order.

Facility Initiation
At this stage, user can view the details of deal limit and sanctioned facilities limit details. Usercan also initiate the single or multiple facilities. Once deal is created in respective third partysystem, then send back will not be allowed. Now, facility and deal details can be updatedthrough app update processes.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >> Applications
.
The
Assigned
and
Pool
tab appear in the grid displaying details like
Finance Account Number, Application Form Number,Neo Cust ID, Identifi cation No., Customer Name,
Finance Amount, Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, ProductName, Scheme, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No., Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Branch, Assignee, Created
Download Key fact Sheet
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 550/766
By, Priority, Disbursal Status, Group Description, Image Based Processing, Product Processor, Credit , Offi cer,Agreement Booklet SNo, FI Status, Collateral Status, RCU Status and Actions
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
Show/ Hide
placed at the top right of the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate
to the
Facility Initiation
screen for applicationprocessing.
On the
Facility Initiation
page, displays following tabs to view, edit or capture any required information:
Fields Name
Description
Deal Limit Details
This section displays the deals limit details.
Deal ID
Displays unique deal id.
Sanctioned Amount
Displays the sanctioned amount of the deal.
In Process Limit
Displays the deal limit amount that is in process.
Consumed Limit
Displays the amount consumed.
Available Limit
Displays the available amount.
Limit Expiry Date
Displays the date on which the deal limit gets expire.
Facility Limit Details
This section to display facility limit details.
Facility ID
Displays unique id of the facility.
Parent Facility ID
Displays the parent id in case the facility type is child.
Sanctioned Amount
Displays the sanctioned amount of the facility.
Drawing Power
Displays drawing power of the customer.
In Process Limit
Displays the facility limit amount that is in process.
Consumed Limit
Displays the amount consumed.
Available Limit
Displays the available amount.
Facility Details
Use this section view and initiate the facility. In addition, Repayment Parameters and otherparameters, such as Charges, Receipts, Facility Sourcing, Covenant, etc.
Deal Create
Click to create a deal in third party system. After creating thedeal, user can initiate the facility.
Initiate
Click to initiate particular facility workfl ow. This button appearsafter clicking the
Deal Create
button.
Facility ID
Displays the unique facility id.
Click to capture and view various facility parameters indiff erent tabs.
Product Type
Displays product type of the facility.
Facility Type
Displays whether the facility is Fund Based or Non Fund Based.
Facility Initiation
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 551/766
Facility Group
Displays group of the facility.
Product
Displays the product associated with the facility.
Facility Sub Group
Displays sub group of the facility.
Scheme
Displays the scheme mapped with the facility.
Sanction Details (Amount / ROI/ Tenure)
Displays the sanctioned amount, rate of interest and tenure ofthe facility.
Parent Facility ID
Displays the parent facility id in case the current facility type ischild.
Facility Expiry Date
Displays expiry date of the facility.
Facility Status
Displays current status of the facility
Disbursal Status
Displays current disbursal status of the application.
Initiate
Click to initiate the selected facilities in the grid. User caninitiate single or multiple facilities by selecting them in thegrid.
This tab displays all the details captured at
Credit Approval
stage.
Use the
Document
tab to add the documents required for the loan application.
Note:
This tab is common to both
Individual
and
Non Individual
Applicant Type.
On this tab, user can perform the following tasks:
Update checklist
: Click this option to update the document checklist.
Accept Additional Document
: Use this option to accept multiple additional documentsby providing
Reference
Type
,
Entity
Type
and
Documents
. The additional document canalso be marked as
Mandatory
.
Download
: Select the documents in the left pane and click the
Download All
todownload the selected document(s).
Note
:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.
Underwriter Decision
Documents
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 552/766
Cancel Rescan Request
: Click the
Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.
Request Status
: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
View Summary
: Use this option to displays the summary of all the documents of theloan application. User can
Print
the summary and
Save as PDF
, if required.
View and Select Documents
: List of documents are displayed in the left pane groupedby Customer and Applicant. Select the document to change the status of the documentin the middle pane.
Link and Swap Images
: The document images can be linked and swapped with otherdocument images by clicking the
and selecting the required option.
Update Document Status
: Select a document in the left pane and select any of thefollowing status:
Pending (
)
: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
WaivedOff (
)
: Select when the document is no more required from the customer.Also, select the
Waive Off Reasons
and input the
Waive Off Remarks
*
and other
Remarks
in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark the document as
Mandatory
.
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by the customeron a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Note
:
To mark the status of multiple documents, select multipledocuments using the check box, click
Mark Status
that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.
Incomplete Doc (
): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.
Complete Doc
: Click to mark an incomplete document as complete. This option appearsonly if a received documents has been marked as incomplete.
Add, View, Edit and Compare Documents
: Click
to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are
Received
. User can view and edit the image in the
DocumentViewer
section at the right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent versionof the documents and their details. Moreover, there is a drop down list with the name ofall the uploaded images. Select the fi le to view in the document viewer.
Note
: Only users with the appropriate authority can view thedocument images.

and can be viewed using
Rejected Applications
option. Also, on rejection, the system automatically generates anotifi cation informing the user about the rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
To Put an Application on Hold
Click
More Actions >>
Hold Application.
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Application stops until the Application is onHold.
On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold from thedrop down list.
Specify the
Reassignment Date, Time of Reassign
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
: The application can be put on hold for the current date as well.
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date. On thisdate the hold on the Application gets removed and it is re-assigned to theApplication user.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screenand the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
On the
Applications
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. TheStatus of the Application is displayed as
HOLD
.
Note
:
To unhold a Application,
click
Application
Number
in the grid andthen click
More Options >> Unhold
.
Only user with appropriate authority can hold/unhold the applications.
To View Workfl ow History Record of the Application
Click
More Actions >>
Workfl ow History Records
to view the workfl ow history of theApplication.
Hold Application
Workfl ow History Records
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 560/766
The
Workfl ow History Records
pop-up screen appears showing the list of all the activitiesperformed on the application.
To Send Back the Application
Click
More Actions >>
Send Back
to send the application back to previous stages.
On the
Send Back Application
pop-up screen that appears, select a stage from
SelectStage to Send Back
to send the application back to the selected stage.
Select a
Reason Code
*
which auto populates the
Reason Description
*
.
Optionally, click
to add multiple reasons and click
in the
Actions
columnto delete the reason.
Input
Remarks
and Click
Send Back Application
.
Note:
In case of multiple assets, the application can be sent to any of theprevious stages for modifi cation.
Note:
If confi gured in the system, the application is assigned back to theRelationship manager who sourced the application or to their team forfurther rectifi cation or to the next task as defi ned in the
Task AssignmentMatrix
.
To Raise a Query
Click
More Actions >>
Raise Query
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Note
:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.
Send Back
Raise a Query
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 561/766
To Unquery
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
Select
Reason
from the drop down list and input
Comments
.
Click
Done
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk unquery by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Unquery
above the grid.
To Reassign Query
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
To Reassign a query user has to select either
Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
orcombination of both.
Input
Remarks
and click
Reassign Query
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk reassign by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Reassign
above the grid.
To Re Run priority
Click
More Actions >>
Re Run Priority
to update the priority of the application on the basis ofchanged parameters.
To Re-Assign the Application
Click
More Options >> Re-Assign Application
.
On the pop up screen that appears, select
Team
*
, Assign To
*
, Reason Code
and
ReasonDescription
in the respective fi elds.
Optionally, click
Assign to RM
to assign the application to the RM.
Input
Comments
and click
Submit.
The
Re-Assignment History
section displays the details of previous re-assignments of thisapplication.
Re Run Priority
Re-Assign Application

Tranche Initiation
At this stage multiple tranches of an application can be created and initiated in the system.
Process an application at this stage using following steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,
Customer Name,
Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Tranche Initiation
page for applicationprocessing.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Tranche Initiation
stage.
On the
Tranche Initiation
page, use the following tabs to view or edit the required details:
Fields Name
Description
Limit Amount
*
Displays the limit amount of the tranche.
Drawing Power
*
Displays the drawing power of the tranche.
Consumed Limit
*
Displays the consumed limit.
Click
Details
to view the details of the consumed limit.
In Process Limit
*
Displays the amount that is not yet disbursed.
Available Limit
*
Displays the total amount of Consumed Limit and In Process Limit.
Limit Expiry Date
*
Displays the limit expiry date.
Refresh
Click to refresh and update the amount.
Limit Setup
Click to setup the consumption limit.
Tranche Request Date
Input the date on which the tranche is requested.
Request Tranche Amount
Input the amount requested for this tranche.

Click
Done
to add a new tranche of the application.

(Use this section to view and update the tranche disbursal schedule for the loan. Click
to

*
This gets auto-populated as per the repayment policyattached. However, it can also be edited and set to anyvalue less than the displayed value.

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 575/766
Rate (%)
This fi eld gets auto populated and can be edited ifrequired.
Note:
In case of the Installment Based loans, this fi eldappears as read only.
Re-Run PricingMatrix
Click to update the rates as per the matrix defi ned in the
Rule Matrix Master
.
Spread
*
This fi eld gets auto-populated. The value of this fi eld is thediff erence between the Rate and Anchor Rate.
Rate Lock
Check to freeze the interest rate.
Locked InterestRate
Input the interest rate to be calculated during the lockduration.
Locked RateExpiry Date
Select the date on which rate lock expire.
TDS Applicable
Check to apply TDS on the loan installments.
Note
: This check box appears when the TDS isapplicable on the loan application. If in the policies BrokenPeriod and Charge Separately checkbox is selected,TDS isapplicable on broken period interest as well.
Tax ComputedOn Type
Select as either
EMI
or
Interest
on which the tax iscomputed.
TDS Policy
Select a TDS policy to be applicable.
Linked Applicant
Select the name of the applicant to whom TDS isapplicable.
TDS Rate
This fi eld gets auto-populated as per the selected TDSPolicy.
Due Day
*
Choose installment due date from the drop down list.
Note:
The fi eld appears as Interest Due day in case ofOD Product Type.
Upfront Interest
Check this check box to calculate the upfront interest.
Number ofUpfrontInstallments
*
Input the number of upfront installments.
Interest StartDate
*
and FirstInstallmentDate
Input the interest start date and the fi rst installment date.
By default,
Interest Start Date
is set as the
currentbusiness date
.
Credit Days
Input the credit days for the manufacturer/dealers.

Yes
or
No
, as required.
Note:
Users with the relevant authority can change thevalue of this fi eld. Otherwise, the fi eld displays the valueconfi gured in respective

in Read-Onlymode.

VAP Details
section
(This section display the list of the VAPs linked to the loan account. The gridin this section displays VAP Number, VAP Category, VAP Type, VAPTreatment, Charge Code, VAP Amount, Follow Contract Parameters,Payout/Commission and Repayment Schedule for each VAP.)
(In case for a specifi c VAP, if the Follow Contract Parameters option is set toNo, the system provides an option to View and Edit details of thoseparameters using the Add/View Details link as provided in the column. (Additionally the system also provides the Payout/Commission link in thePayout/Commission column, to view the Payout/Commission policy attachedto each VAP Product.)

Repayment Schedule
based on the specifi ed loanparameters. This fi eld appears disabled.

: In case of multiple installments, the Installmentvalue that populates on the screen is the same installmentvalue that appears in the fi rst row of the repaymentschedule.

Click to view the
Repayment Schedule
on a separatescreen. This fi eld is enabled for single disbursal type loancases.
The Repayment Schedule as generated here is aconsolidated schedule incase of Multiple Asset LoanApplication Type and Any Loan Application linked withVAP.
RepaymentUpload
Click to open the Repayment Schedule Upload pop-up.The user can upload and edit repayment schedule on thispop-up screen. There is also provision to create structuredschedule on the screen itself without using the Uploadoption. This is enabled for structured loans.
In the Repayment Schedule pop-up, the user can Browseand select the repayment schedule or drag and drop inthe space provided. The user can then input
Remarks
*fi eld and click
Upload
. The Repayment Schedule will beuploaded. In the Edit Repayment Schedule section, theuser can view and edit the uploaded repaymentschedule. This section has the following fi elds andcolumns:

Use the
Document
tab to add the documents required for the loan application.
Note:
This tab is common to both
Individual
and
Non Individual
Applicant Type.
On this tab, user can perform the following tasks:
Update checklist
: Click this option to update the document checklist.
Accept Additional Document
: Use this option to accept multiple additional documentsby providing
Reference
Type
,
Entity
Type
and
Documents
. The additional document canalso be marked as
Mandatory
.
Download
: Select the documents in the left pane and click the
Download All
todownload the selected document(s).
Note
:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.
Cancel Rescan Request
: Click the
Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.
Request Status
: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
View Summary
: Use this option to displays the summary of all the documents of theloan application. User can
Print
the summary and
Save as PDF
, if required.

ocuments
Grid Columns and Description

View and Select Documents
: List of documents are displayed in the left pane groupedby Customer and Applicant. Select the document to change the status of the documentin the middle pane.
Link and Swap Images
: The document images can be linked and swapped with otherdocument images by clicking the
and selecting the required option.
Update Document Status
: Select a document in the left pane and select any of thefollowing status:
Pending (
)
: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
WaivedOff (
)
: Select when the document is no more required from the customer.Also, select the
Waive Off Reasons
and input the
Waive Off Remarks
*
and other
Remarks
in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark the document as
Mandatory
.
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by the customeron a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Note
:
To mark the status of multiple documents, select multipledocuments using the check box, click
Mark Status
that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.
Incomplete Doc (
): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.
Complete Doc
: Click to mark an incomplete document as complete. This option appearsonly if a received documents has been marked as incomplete.
Add, View, Edit and Compare Documents
: Click
to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are
Received
. User can view and edit the image in the
DocumentViewer
section at the right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent versionof the documents and their details. Moreover, there is a drop down list with the name ofall the uploaded images. Select the fi le to view in the document viewer.
Note
: Only users with the appropriate authority can view thedocument images.
Once the details of the documents is updated, click
Submit
.

or

.

Actions

to remove the record.

*

Book Debts
(Use this section to to capture the details of Sundry Debtors, Bills Discounted by the organizationand Sundry Creditors.)
S.No.
Displays auto generated serial number.
Debtors As Per Age
*

Amount

Consider For Computation

to remove the record.

Drawing Power Computation
(This section displays the detailed computation and breakup of maximum drawing power thatcan be utilized from the cash credit facility.)
Field(s) Name
Description
VAP Product
*
Select the VAP product whose details needs to be viewed,deleted or edited.
VAP Details
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 589/766
VAP ID
Input a unique VAP ID.
VAP Product
Input the VAP Product name.
VAP Category
Select the VAP Category.
VAP Type
Select the VAP Type.
VAP Treatment
Select the VAP treatment captured for the selected VAP.
VAP Amount
Input the VAP amount.
Actions
Lists the actions that can be performed on the VAP record.
Note:
In case of
limit loans with multiple Finance Account Numbers (LAN)
, with everytranche having a unique Finance Account Number (LAN), the user can add a VAP only atthe tranche level only if the VAP policy is applicable on the fi nance.
In case of
limit loans with a single unique LAN
, the user can add a VAP only at thefi nance level just once. VAP cannot be added in subsequent tranches.
In case of
Member Split
, the user can add a VAP at the application level and then mapthe VAP with the tranches using the Multi Tranche VAP Linking feature.
In case there is
no member split
, the user can add a VAP at the application level.
Tranche Approval
At this stage the tranches get approved that are initiated in the system at
Tranche Initiation
stage.
Process an application at this stage using following steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,
Customer Name,
Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Tranche Approval
page for applicationprocessing.
On the
Tranche Approval
page, set the following fi elds on the
Tranche Approval
tab:
Fields name and Description

Application Details
This section displays details of the application. User can click
CAM Report
and
ApplicationSummary
to generate the respective reports. Moreover, click vertical ellipses (
) to view thedetails captured in Sourcing Details, Loan Parameters, Receipt Details, Property Details, FinancialSummary, Personal Discussion and Collateral Details.

: There are various confi guration in CAS to display the Application Summary for allproduct types or for some specifi c product types.

Displays the purpose of the loan.
Loan Amount
Displays the total amount of the loan.
ROI/Tenure
Displays the ROI and tenure of the loan.
Installment
Displays the loan installment
Eligibility
Displays total eligibility amount.
Total Exposure
Displays the total exposure amount.
Note
: Click
to refresh the amount and click ellipses (...)to view the total exposure and posidex exposure and viewExposure History in the pop-up screen.

Re RunPolicy
.

: Any existing information under
Applicant Information
can be updated as explained in
Lead Details
.

Displays the customer details, such as applicant name, age,employment type, applicant type and mobile number.

KYC
Displays the KYC details of the applicant.
FI
Displays the KYC details of the applicant.

Displays the KYC details of the applicant.

Displays the dedupe process result. Click ellipses (
...
) to viewthe matched and Re Run the dedupe process.

Exposure

Click ellipses (
...
) to view the exposure details, exposurehistory and Re Initiate to update the exposure details.

Property Details
This section displays details of the property capture in previous stages of the application. Thissection displays in case of Home Loan.
Property Type
Displays the type of the property.
Nature of Property
Displays the nature of the property.
Property Sub Type
Displays the sub type of the property.
Builder/Seller
Displays the name of the builder or seller who is selling theproperty.

Asset Details
This section displays details of the asset capture in previous stages of the application. Thissection displays in case of Auto Loan.
Asset Category
Displays the category of the vehicle.
Make/ Model/ Version
Displays the make, mode or version of the asset.
Asset Type
Displays the type of the asset.
Dealer Name
Displays the name of the dealer who sold the asset.

Displays whether the asset accessories also included. Clickellipsis (...) to view the details of the accessories.

Collateral Details
This section displays the collateral details captured in previous stages of the application and alsoprovides options to re-run collateral investigation process and view more details of thecollateral.
Type
Displays the type of the collateral.
Sub Type
Displays the sub type of the collateral.
Address
Displays the address of the collateral.
Lien Amount
Displays the total lien amount of the collateral.

Displays the address of the property.
Valuation

Legal Verifi cation
Displays the legal verifi cation status of the collateral.
