Ability Indicators
This section displays the ability indicators calculated by the system for the Applicant like, LTV(Loan To Value ratio), IIR (Installment to Income Ratio), Aggregate LTV and DBR (Debt BurdenRatio).
Gross LTV
Displays the gross loan to value ratio of the applicant.
DBR
Displays the Debt Burden Ratio of the applicant.
IIR
Displays the Installment to Income Ratio of the applicant.
Aggregate LTV
Displays the aggregate loan to value ratio of the applicant.
Net LTV
Displays the net loan to value ratio of the applicant.

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 599/766

ABB/EMI
Displays the Average Bank Balance (ABB) and EquationMonthly Installments (EMI).
Average Credit
Displays the average credit of the applicant.
Note
: In addition to these fi elds, if more ability indicators are mapped, then they will appearin the section, if applicable.
Documents Details
This section displays the details of documents collected/captured in previous stages of theapplication and also provides an option to collect the pending required documents.
Applicant
Displays the name of the applicant to whom the document isassociated.
Loan Documents
Displays the number of required documents and receiveddocuments related to the loan application.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Applicant Documents
Displays the number of required documents and receiveddocuments related to the applicants.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Collateral Documents
Displays the number of required documents and receiveddocuments related to the collateral.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Other Documents
Displays the number of other required and receiveddocuments.
Click the link to view the details of the document.
Collect Document
Click this link to collect the required pending documents.
Deviation Details
This section is used to view and approve or reject the deviations raised in the system. Thissection displays the deviations in following categories:
Credit
: Displays the credit deviations, which is Approved/Rejected by theinternal/external credit team.
Business
: Displays the sales deviations, which is Approved/Rejected by theinternal/external sales team.
Approval Sheet
: The user can raise the deviations and add users in Through, To andSeen categories to authorize the deviations. In this section, a consolidated status alsoappears that displays the details of Through, To and Seen users along with the decisioncomments.
Deviation
Displays the name of the deviation.
Deviation Level
Displays the level of the deviation.
Source System
Displays the source system to which the deviation isassociated.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 600/766
Assigned To
Displays the name of the user to whom the deviation isassigned.
Entity Name
Displays the name of the entity.
Status
Displays the current status of the deviation.
Action Date
Displays the date on which last action was performed on thedeviation.
Action By
Displays name of the user who performed some action onthe deviation.
Actions
Displays the icon to delete the deviation.
View History
Click this link to view the deviation history.
Raise Deviation
Click to manually raise deviations.
Re Run Policy
Click re run and refresh the deviation policy.
Re Run External Deviation
Click to re run the external deviation.
Asset Approval Decision List section
(
This section appears only in case
Multiple Assets
loan application type and it displays the list ofAssets Requested by the Applicant. An underwriter is required to either approve or reject anysingle, multiple or all requested assets.)
Miscellaneous Information
This section displays miscellaneous information captured in the previous stages of theapplication.
Decision History
This section displays the following details in separate tab:
Decision history
: Displays the log of decisions taken on the Application in the previousstages of the Application
.
Forward History
: Displays the log of the forward history of the Application
.
Committee Decision History:
Displays
the details of members of the committee and listof committee decisions taken along with comments and special conditions
.
Tranche Approval Decision
Click
at the bottom right side of the screen and provide details in the following sections:
Justifi cation
: Use this section to input strengths and weaknesses of the applicant andthe executive summary.
Special Condition
: Use this section to add single or multiple Special Conditions to theapplication or applicant that must be met till the specifi ed Application Stage. Theseconditions are tracked at post approval and disbursal activities
.
Course Approval:
Use this section to view the course fee details and update theapproved amount in the respective fi eld. In addition, click the
Payment
link and inputthe amount in case the course fee is to be paid in multiple payments.
Note
: This section appears in case of Education Loan only.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 601/766
Approval Checklist
: Use this section to check the status of the points listed in theApproval Checklist and mark response against each. Based on the response marked,the Deviations for the Application are raised automatically by the system. A responsecan be marked as either
Yes
,
No
or
NA
against any checklist point.
Tranche Approval Decision
: Use this section to mark the credit approval decision aseither
Approve
,
Reject
or
In-Principle Approve
and specify the loan amount, tenureand the interest rate that should be sanctioned for this Application.
Onapproval/rejection, the system automatically generates a notifi cation, informing theuser about the approval/rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
This section also displays the tranche schedule and tranche history details.
This section also allows the user to
Forward
the application to another user to provideconsent or approval for the same,
Hold
the application (also mention the hold reason,reassignment date and time and remarks),
Initiate Committee Decision
to allocate theloan application to a committee which can be based on the defi ned Assignment Matrixor one that is selected by the user in
Committee Code
*
fi eld,
Auto Initiate CommitteeDecision
to automatically initiate the committee approval process
Note
: The
Tranche Approval
stage also displays other details, such as Underwriter Decision,Deal & Facility Details, Bill Details, etc. on the respective tabs.
Go to the
Tranche Details
tab to view the details of previously disbursed tranches associated with the application.
Curing
In case an application doesn't satisfy certain parameters as confi gured in the system whilemoving to the
Disbursal Author
stage during both Manual and Auto Approval, it automaticallymoves to the
Curing
stage to fi ll in those gaps for satisfying the parameters and for adding anypending or additional information.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,Customer Name, Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type,
Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Curing
screen for application processing.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 602/766

: Use the

column to make sure the selected application is at the

VAP Details
section
(This section display the list of the VAPs linked to the loan account. The grid in this sectiondisplays VAP Number, VAP Category, VAP Type, VAP Treatment, Charge Code, VAP Amount,Follow Contract Parameters, Payout/Commission and Repayment Schedule for each VAP.)
(In case for a specifi c VAP, if the Follow Contract Parameters option is set to No, the systemprovides an option to View and Edit details of those parameters using the Add/View Details linkas provided in the column. Also, in case it is set to Yes, the details of contract parametersappears in read-only mode.)
(Additionally the system also provides the Payout/Commission link in the Payout/Commissioncolumn, to view the Payout/Commission policy attached to each VAP Product.)
Insurance Funded
Check this option to indicate the same.
Also, capture the details of the insurance in
Insurance Details
section.
Repayment Parameters >> Insurance Details section
(
Use this section to capture insurance information.
)
Type OfInsurance
*
Select the type of insurance.
Insured
*
Select the name of the member being insured.
Note:
In case of SHG, name of each member appears.
Sum Insured
*
Input the total amount of insurance.
InsuranceProvider
*
Select the name of the insurance company
PremiumAmount
*
Input the amount to be paid as premium.
Start Date andEnd Date
Select the start and end date of the insurance inrespective fi elds.

PolicyNumber

CoverageType

Input the date on which thecover note is issued by theinsurance company.
This fi led is mandatory if theCover Note Number isprovided.

*
Input the name of theinsurance company who willpay the claim.

Input the name of thenominee.
NomineeRelationship
Select the relation of thenominee with the insurer.
Gender
Select a gender of thenominee.
Date of Birth
Input the nominee date ofbirth.
Is Minor?
Check if the nominee isminor.
AppointeeName
Input the name of theappointee of minornominee.
This fi eld enables in case
IsMinor?
is checked.
AppointeeRelationship
Select the relation of theappointee with the minornominee.
This fi eld enables in case
IsMinor?
is checked.
AppointeeDOB
Select the appointee date ofbirth.

Repayment Parameters >> FAS
Limit Expiry Date
Input the limit expiry date, which is auto computed based on the sourcingdate of the application
However, it can be edited.
Note
: This fi eld appears for FAS Product Type only.
FAS Tenure
FAS tenure is auto populated on the basis of
Limit Expiry Date
but can beedited.
Note
: This fi eld appears for FAS Product Type only.
Grace Flag
Select increase or decrease to increases or decrease the systemcalculated credit limit.
Note
: This fi eld appears for FAS Product Type only.
Credit Limit Grace%
Input the percentage of grace limit, by which the credit limit has to beincreased or decreased based on
Grace
Flag
.
Note
: This fi eld appears for FAS Product Type only.
Repayment Parameters >> Charge & Fee section
(
Use this section to
add a receivable/payable charge to be included in the Application
.
)
(
Click
to choose the charges from the
Add New Charge
pop-up screen that appears.
Also, choose
Charge Nature
for each selected charge.
)
Collect Charges
Click to collect the chosen charges.
In case of Non collectible and Deductible method of charge, it is non-mandatory to collect the IMD or short receive from the disbursement.
Diff erential Amount
Displays the diff erential amount for the charges that can be editable.
Diff erential Rate
Displays the diff erential rate for the charges that can be editable.
Eff ective ChargeAmount
Displays the eff ective charge amount.
Collect ContractualCharges
Click to collect the chosen contractual charges.
Diff erentialRate/Amount
Displays the diff erential rate/amount of the contractual charges that canbe editable.

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 630/766

: After specifying all the loan parameters, the system calculates and displays the
CapitalFunded
amount. Click

to calculate the installment amount.
In case there are any
Finance
and
Revenue
VAPs linked to the loan account, the system displaysa consolidated value.
Builder Plan
Applicable for home loan and Micro Housing Finance loans only
Click to view details of the default and optional building payment plans asdefi ned in the Building master. The Building Payment Plan pop-up isdisplayed on clicking the button.
Column Name
Description
Code
Displays the uniquepayment plan code.
Name
Displays the payment planname.
Description
Displays the payment plandescription.
IsDefault
Select the radio button tomake a payment plan asdefault.
Stage
Displays the stage ofconstruction.
Completion %
Displays the percentcompletion that needs tobe considered for thestage.
Builder Demand %
Displays the percentage ofpayment to be disbursed tothe builder in the tranche.
Cumulative BuilderDemand %
Displays the total builderdemand percentage tilldate.
CalculateInstallment
Click to calculate the installment amount and generate
RepaymentSchedule
based on the specifi ed loan parameters. This fi eld appearsdisabled.
Note
: In case of multiple installments, the Installment value thatpopulates on the screen is the same installment value that appears in thefi rst row of the repayment schedule.
Combinedrepayment schedulewith VAP
Click to view the repayment schedule combined with VAP linked with theloan account.
Combinedrepayment schedule
Click to view the combined repayment schedule.
Field(s) Name and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 631/766
View RepaymentSchedule
Click to view the
Repayment Schedule
on a separate screen. This fi eld isenabled for single disbursal type loan cases.
The Repayment Schedule as generated here is a consolidated scheduleincase of Multiple Asset Loan Application Type and Any Loan Applicationlinked with VAP.
Repayment Upload
Click to open the Repayment Schedule Upload pop-up. The user canupload and edit repayment schedule on this pop-up screen. There is alsoprovision to create structured schedule on the screen itself without usingthe Upload option. This is enabled for structured loans.
In the Repayment Schedule pop-up, the user can Browse and select therepayment schedule or drag and drop in the space provided. The usercan then input
Remarks
* fi eld and click
Upload
. The RepaymentSchedule will be uploaded. In the Edit Repayment Schedule section, theuser can view and edit the uploaded repayment schedule. This sectionhas the following fi elds and columns:
Field Name
Description
Loan Amount/VAPAmount
Displays the total loan amount.
Tenure
*
Displays the tenure of the loan.
Sheet Name
*
Displays the name of the excelsheet uploaded.
Interest Start Date
*
Displays the date on which theinterest calculation was started.This fi eld can be edited.
First InstallmentDate
*
Displays the fi rst installmentdate.
Auto-Correct
Click to auto-correct.
Reset
Click to reset fi elds.
ColumnName
Values
InstallmentNo.
Displays the installment number.
InstallmentDate
Displays the installment date. This fi eldcan be edited.
InstallmentAmount
Displays the installment amount.
PrincipalAmount
Displays the installment principalamount. This fi eld can be edited.
InterestAmount
Displays the loan interested amount.This fi eld can be edited.
Rate%
Displays the interest rate percentage.This fi eld can be edited.

ocuments

Note:
This tab is common to both
Individual
and
Non Individual
Applicant Type.
On this tab, user can perform the following tasks:
Update checklist
: Click this option to update the document checklist.
Accept Additional Document
: Use this option to accept multiple additional documentsby providing
Reference
Type
,
Entity
Type
and
Documents
. The additional document canalso be marked as
Mandatory
.
Download
: Select the documents in the left pane and click the
Download All
todownload the selected document(s).
Note
:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.
Scan/Rescan
: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.
Cancel Rescan Request
: Click the
Cancel Request
link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.
Request Status
: Use this option to view the current status of the rescan requests:
Columns Name
Description
Initiator Name
Displays the name of the user whoinitiated the rescan request.
Initiation Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is initiated.
Completion Date
Displays the date on which the rescanrequest is completed.
Requested Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescan request is initiated.
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Completed Document
Displays the number of documents onwhich the rescanning request iscompleted
Grid Columns and Description

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 641/766
Click the number to display the list ofdocuments.
Remarks
Displays the remarks to rescan thedocument.
Cancel Remarks
Displays the remarks to cancel therescanning request.
Status
Displays the current status of the rescanrequest.
Action
Click the
Cancel Request
link to cancel allthe available rescan requests.
Click the
Show Cancelled Details
link toview the details of the cancelled rescanrequests.
View Summary
: Use this option to displays the summary of all the documents of theloan application. User can
Print
the summary and
Save as PDF
, if required.
View and Select Documents
: List of documents are displayed in the left pane groupedby Customer and Applicant. Select the document to change the status of the documentin the middle pane.
Link and Swap Images
: The document images can be linked and swapped with otherdocument images by clicking the
and selecting the required option.
Update Document Status
: Select a document in the left pane and select any of thefollowing status:
Pending (
)
: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Received On
*
Select the date on which the document isreceived.
Original/Photocopy
Select Original if the original document isreceived or select Photocopy if the photocopyof the document is received.
Verifi cation Status
*
Select a verifi cation of the document.
Processing State
Select a processing state of the document.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
Fields Name and Description

)
: Select when the document is no more required from the customer.Also, select the

and input the

*
and other

in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark the document as

.

)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by the customeron a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Fields Name
Description

Tentative Date
*
Select a date on which this document isexpected to be received.

*
Input remarks of marking the document asdeferred.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.

:

Mark Status
that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.

): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.
Complete Doc
: Click to mark an incomplete document as complete. This option appearsonly if a received documents has been marked as incomplete.
Add, View, Edit and Compare Documents
: Click
to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are
Received
. User can view and edit the image in the
DocumentViewer
section at the right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent versionof the documents and their details.
Note
: Only users with the appropriate authority can view thedocument images.

.

to send the application back to previous stages.

pop-up screen that appears, select a stage from

Select a

*
which auto populates the
Reason Description
*
.

to add multiple reasons and click
in the

columnto delete the reason.

and Click

.

.

and

to send the Application to

.

. On successfully completing the action,

messageappears on the screen and the system redirects to the

screen. Else, errors (if any)are notifi ed.

screen, click

tab to view the rejected Application in the grid. The

of the rejected Application is displayed as

.

Send Back
in the

column, to send the Application back to the lead user. Or, toremove the Application from the system, click

in the

column.
For
Decision
as
Reject,
the Application moves to

and can be viewed using

option. Also, on rejection, the system automatically generates anotifi cation informing the user about the rejection via

or

At this stage, linking of individual VAP products with individual assets need to be done beforedisbursement of fi nance.

Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Neo CustID, Identifi cation No.,
Customer Name, Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name,
Account Type,
RequestType
,
Phone No., Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority
and
Actions
foreach application.

:

SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.

:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Application Tranche VAP Linking
screen.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
ApplicationTranche VAP Linking
stage.
The
Application Tranche VAP Linking
page displays
Applicant Information
and
Link VAP
tabs to view, edit or capture anyrequired information.
Go to the
Applicant information
tab.
The details that were
captured at previous stages are auto populated. They can also be edited, if required.
Also, there are additional fi elds, that are required to be set at this stage. These fi elds appear inside various child tabs inthe main
Applicant Information
tab
, depending on the selected Applicant Type (
Individual
or
Non Individual
)
.
Click
View/Edit Detailed Information
to view, edit or capture the details in the following tabs:
Go to the
Link VAP
tab.
On this tab, the
Link VAP
grid displays,
Tranche Id, VAP Id, VAP Product, VAP Category, VAP Type, VAP Treatment
and
VAP Amount
for each linked VAP.
Input
Split Id
in the
Tranche Id
fi elds to link with VAP.
Click
Save Details
to save the details.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen.Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.

RCU Initiation
Applicable for
All
Product Types
The Risk Containment Unit user with appropriate rights can use this option to mark the result ofRCU Screening of the Applications as
Screening OK, Screening Not OK, Sampling Required, etc.
The Applications for which
Sampling Required
is marked at this stage move to the
RCUVerifi cation
stage, wherein, the RCU Sampling result is captured. Other applications will move tothe
RCU Referral
stage for fi nal decision.
Note
: If confi gured in FinnOne Neo CAS, the RCU Referral stage gets automatically completedbased on the Assignment Matrix defi ned. Also,
t
he rows of the RCU Grid can be colour codedbased on the priority of an application. The user must create a record in the Assignment Matrixmaster for a particular colour in a stage and then map an event code with the record forassigning the colour.
Note
: RCU can be initiated
automatically
at any given point in the Application workfl ow as perthe
Workfl ow defi nition
. In case of automatic RCU initiation, the appropriate agency/user is autoallocated by the system.
In case the application is rejected or cancelled, the system allows the user or agency to completethe RCU Verifi cation.
Follow these steps to mark the RCU screening result for Application (s):
Go to
Applications
>>
RCU
>>
RCU Initiation
.
On
RCU Initiation
page, search for the Applications using one or more of the search parameters, such as
Activity
(Application Stage)
, Application Id, Customer Name, Product Type, RCU Status
and
Screening Status
.
Check the
Advanced Search
check box to include additional search parameters, such as
Branch, Product, Scheme, Date
(Application Sourcing Date) and
End Date
.
Click
Search
to view the search results in the grid below.
The grid displays
Application Information,
Scheme / Card Type, RCU Status, RCU Result, Document Sampling Status,Reasons, RCU Agency, Attachments
and
RCU Details
for each Application in the search result.
Note
: If a user is already working on the application, it becomes unavailable to other usersand same is displayed in
RCU Result
*
column. If not assigned to any user, RCU Result can be
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 651/766
marked for multiple Applications.
In the grid, select an
RCU Result
as either
Screening OK, Screening Not OK, RCU Pending, Sampling Required
or
Waived
from the drop down list.
If
RCU Result
is selected as
Sampling required
, also select
RCU Agency
to which the
RCU Sampling
is to beassigned. Otherwise,
RCU Agency
fi eld is inactive.
Note
: The Applications for which Sampling is required move to
RCU Verifi cation
stage, whereinthe result of RCU sampling can be marked.
In case of RCU Not Initiated, RCU can be waived off at application level by selecting the check boxand clicking
Waive Off
.
Under RCU Initiated cases, document(s)
can also be waived off by selecting
Waived
from the
RCUResult
drop down list.
Additionally, check the
Document Sampling Status
check box to initiate, reinitiate or waive off RCU on documentlevel.
Note
:
Document Sampling Status
also displays the documents status and reason when all thedocuments are marked with the same status. Moreover, this fi eld also displays the Agency if allthese documents are assigned to the same agency.

Pending (

: Select when the document is pending and is yet to be received. Also,input
Remarks
and mark the document as
Mandatory
, if required.
Received (
)
: Select when the document is received by the customer. Also, set thefollowing Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Received On
*
Select the date on which the document isreceived.
Original/Photocopy
Select Original if the original document isreceived or select Photocopy if the photocopyof the document is received.
Verifi cation Status
*
Select a verifi cation of the document.
Processing State
Select a processing state of the document.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
WaivedOff (
)
: Select when the document is no more required from the customer.Also, select the
Waive Off Reasons
and input the
Waive Off Remarks
*
and other
Remarks
in the respective fi elds. If required, user can also mark the document as
Mandatory
.
Deferred (
)
: Select when the document is expected to be received by the customeron a future date. Also, set the following Fields:
Fields Name
Description
Deferred Reasons
Select reasons to mark this document asdeferred.
Tentative Date
*
Select a date on which this document isexpected to be received.
Deferred Remarks
*
Input remarks of marking the document asdeferred.
Remarks
Input remarks regarding the document.
Mandatory
Check to mark the document as mandatory, ifrequired.
Note
:
To mark the status of multiple documents, select multipledocuments using the check box, click
Mark Status
that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.
Incomplete Doc (
): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.
Fields Name and Description
Fields Name and Description

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 654/766
Complete Doc
: Click to mark an incomplete document as complete. This option appearsonly if a received documents has been marked as incomplete.
Add, View, Edit and Compare Documents
: Click
to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are
Received
. User can view and edit the image in the
DocumentViewer
section at the right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent versionof the documents and their details. Moreover, there is a drop down list with the name ofall the uploaded images. Select the fi le to view in the document viewer.
Note
: Only users with the appropriate authority can view thedocument images.
Once the details of the documents is updated, click
Submit
.
Click
Done
.
Click
Add
in the
Reason
column to add the reason for RCU Result on the pop up screen that appears.
On the pop-up screen, select
Reason
from the drop down list.
Description
will be populated automatically basedon the Reason selected and it is read only.
To add another Reason click
Add Reason
. To remove a Reason, click
.
Click
Save
.
Click
Save
.
A
Success
message is displayed at the top right corner of the screen notifying about the action performed.
Note
:
A notifi cation is sent to the agency to which the RCU is assigned.

RCU Verifi cation
RCU Verifi cation
Applicable for
All
Product Types
The Applications for which RCU Result is marked as
Sampling Required
at
RCU Initiation
stagemove to RCU Verifi cation stage.
The RCU Agency user to whom the RCU Verifi cation request has been assigned can use thisoption to mark the Sampling Status for the Application as
Positive, Negative or Neutral
and alsocapture the Verifi cation Date.
Note
: If confi gured in FinnOne Neo CAS, the RCU Referral stage gets automatically completedbased on the assignment matrix defi ned.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 655/766
Follow these steps to mark RCU Verifi cation/Sampling Status:
Go to
Applications
>>
RCU
>>
RCU Verifi cation
.
On
RCU Initiation
page, search for the Applications using the search parameters namely,
Activity
(Application Stage)
,Application Id, Customer Name, Product Type, RCU Status
and
Screening Status
.
Check the
Advanced Search
check box to include additional search parameters namely,
Branch, Product, Scheme, Date
(Application Sourcing Date) and
End Date
.
Click
Search
to view the search results in the grid below.
The grid displays
Application Information,
Scheme/Card Type, RCU Status, Reasons, Sampler/Initiator, Verifying AgentName,
Verifi cation Date, Sampling Status, Document Sampling Status
and
Attachments
for each Application in thesearch result.
Use check boxes to select the required Applications.
For each of the selected Applications, input
Verifi cation Date
for RCU Sampling.
Select
Sampling Status
for the application as either
Negative, Neutral, Positive
or
Cancel
from the drop down list foreach of the selected Applications.
Optionally, click
Remarks
in the
Sampling Status
column to add remarks about the application level.
Check
Document Sampling Status
and click the
View
link to provide the document status and remarks in the
Document Received
pop-up screen that appears.
The grid in the
Document Received
pop-up screen displays
Document Name, For, Documents, Remarks, RCUStatus, Status
and
Verifi er Remarks
.
Note
: RCU verifi cation can also be cancelled by selecting the RCU and clicking
Cancel RCU
.Users with appropriate access rights can waive off and cancel the RCU verifi cation.
Click the
RCU Details
link and click
Attach
to attach the fi les.
Optionally, use to
Customer Communication History
tab to send the notifi cation to the customer, if required.
Click
Save
.
A
Success
message is displayed at the top right corner of the screen notifying about the action performed.
See Next

Applicable for
All
Product Types

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 656/766
Once the RCU verifi cation for an application gets completed and the RCU Result gets saved in thesystem. A bank user is required to search for the application using the RCU Referral screen forviewing the verifi cation details and marking the fi nal decision.
Note
: If confi gured in FinnOne Neo CAS, the RCU Referral stage gets automatically completedbased on the assignment matrix defi ned.
Follow these steps to mark RCU Verifi cation/Sampling Status:
Go to
Applications
>>
RCU
>>
RCU Referral
.
On
RCU Referral
screen, search for the Applications using either of the search parameters, such as
Activity
(ApplicationStage)
, Application ID, Customer Name, Product Type, RCU Status
and
Screening Status
.
Use the
Advanced Search
check box to include additional search parameters, such as
Branch, Product, Scheme, Date
(Application Sourcing Date) and
End Date
.
In case the search parameters are left blank, the system displays all the applications which have undergone the RCUVerifi cation process in the grid below.
Click
Search
to view the search results in the grid below.
The grid displays
Application Information,
Scheme/Card Type, RCU Status, Reasons, Sampling Status, AgencyRemarks, Document Sampling Status, Reason, Final Decision
and
Attachments
for each Application in the searchresult.
Use check boxes to select the required Applications.
For each of the selected Application, view the
Reasons
for the decision taken and accordingly select a
Final Decision
as either
Positive, Profi le Decline, Document Decline or Could Not Verify
from the drop down list and add a
Reason
to justify the decision taken.
Click
Save
.
A
Success
message is displayed at the top right corner of the screen notifying about the action performed.

Disbursal Initiation
An Application reaches the
Disbursal Stage
after getting processed through various Applicationstages. At this stage, a bank user captures the disbursal details and initiates the disbursal of
Sanctioned Amount
as
single disbursal
or
multiple disbursal
.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 657/766
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,
Customer Name,
Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Credit Offi cer, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for eachapplication.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Disbursal Details
screen for applicationprocessing.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Disbursal Details
stage.
The
Disbursal Details
page displays following tabs to view, edit or capture any required information:
Note
: In case the fi rst disbursal of a fi nance is cancelled, then at the time of re-initiating thedisbursal some fi elds and tabs are editable/non-editable based on the business requirements.
Disbursal Details
(In this tab, the system displays read-only fi elds like
Total Sanctioned Amount, Sanction Date,Disbursal Type
(Single or Multiple),
Application Status
captured initially at previous stages.
Disbursal Status
is displayed as either Not Disbursed (when Disbursed Amount is zero), PartDisbursed (when Disbursed Amount is less than Sanctioned Amount), Fully Disbursed (whenDisbursed Amount is equal to Sanctioned Amount), Disbursal Cancelled (when the disbursal getscancelled from the servicing system) or Loan Cancelled (when the loan gets cancelled from theservicing system).
The System also displays the
Loan Account #, Disbursed Amount
and
First Disbursal Date
.
)
(
Disbursed Amount
gets reduced by the amount of disbursal, in case the disbursal getscancelled.)
Fields Name
Description
Disbursal Report
Click to generate the disbursal report that shows the disbursaldetails of the application.
CAM Report
Click to generate the Credit Approval Memo of the application.
Application Summary
Click to view summary of the application that containssummarized information of the application.
Note
: There are various confi guration in CAS to display theApplication Summary for all product types or for some specifi cproduct types.
Repayment Schedule
Click to view the repayment schedule of the application.
Structured Repayment
Click to Upload, Download, View and Edit the structuredrepayment schedule of the application.
Application Details
Disbursal Details

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 658/766
(This section displays the details of loan application.)
Sanctioned Amount
Displays the total sanctioned amount for the loan application.
VAP Amount
Displays the amount of VAP linked with the loan application.
Sanctioned Date
Displays the date on which the amount was sanctioned.
Available Amount
Displays the available amount for the loan application.
Maximum Sanctioned Amount
Displays the maximum amount limit sanctioned for the loanapplication.
Maximum Sanctioned Tenure
Displays the maximum tenure limit sanctioned for the loanapplication.
ROI/Tenure
Displays the rate of interest and tenure of the loan application
Disbursed Amount
Displays the amount already disbursed in case of multipledisbursal.
Disbursal Type
Displays the type of disbursal as either
Single
or
Multiple
.
Application Status
Displays the current status of the application.
Tentative Disbursal Schedule Details
(This section appears in case of Home Loan and Micro Housing Finance (MHF) to capture thedisbursal details of multiple tranche.)
Total Payment
*
Input the total payment made to the builder.
Disbursal Amount Required
*
Displays the amount required after deducting owner'scontribution from the total payment.
Owner's ContributionRequired
*
Displays the owner's contribution amount.
Directly To Builder
Check if the owner's contribution is paid directly to the builder.
Actual Owner's Contribution
Displays the actual amount of owner's contribution paid to thebuilder.
Actual Disbursal Amount
Displays the actual amount disbursed.
Diff erence(AOC-OCR)
*
Display the diff erence between the Owner's ContributionRequired and Actual Owner's Contribution.
Status
Displays the current disbursal status.
Disburse
Check to send the disbursal information to Disbursal Schedulesection.
Application Tranches section
(
This section displays list of tranches as created in the system during Application Split asconfi gured in the Deal Tranche Policy and Multiple Asset Policy. In case of Omni product type,this section displays the tranches on the basis of the added products.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 659/766
The grid in this section displays details like Split ID,, Loan Amount, Disbursal Status, ApplicationTranche Status and Loan Account Number for each tranche. In case of dealer-wise split, Dealerdetails appears in the grid and in case of asset-wise split, Bought From and Make/Model/Variantdetails appear in the grid. The grid also provides the Copy and Paste options for duplicatinginstrument details for multiple tranches. Additionally, there are options provided to Initiate,Validate/Re-Validate Payment Account, Validate/Re-Validate Repayment Account, View Reportsand Save to validate and revalidate customer's bank account and view and save the last fetchedvalidation report.
Click Split ID of each tranche to initiate its disbursal and to generate its Repayment Schedule.Also, if required, use the Charges, Receipts, Repayment Parameters, Documents, AssetRequested and Instrument Details tab to capture or modify details against each Split ID.
)

button a modal window is displayed.

: A service is created that searches for the bankaccounts linked with the Identifi cation details and bankaccount details provided by the customer and fetches thedetail of all the bank accounts that are linked with thoseidentifi cation details.

The
Deviations
raised as a result of
Credit Approval Policy
run and
Approval Checklist
response provided by the underwriter are available in these child tabs for approval.
Note
:
Only authorized users who have the access rights can view theproduct level Deviations and can take necessary decision
.
Tabs/Child Tabs Description and Usage
Approve Deviations tab >> Assigned To Me child tab
(
This tab displays the Deviations that have been assigned to the currentlyactive underwriter for this application. An underwriter can either Approve orReject the deviations assigned to him/her.
)
Note
:
At the time of system confi guration, there is a provision for defi ningallocation logic for allocating the Deviations to the application users. The
Deviation
Tab(s) Name and Usage
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 702/766
Deviations raised for the Application are thus allocated to users on the basisof the defi ned logic.
Approve Deviations tab >>All Deviations child tab
(
This tab displays all the Deviations raised for this application. Anunderwriter can either Approve or Reject the deviations assigned tohim/her.
)
Approve Deviations tab >> Pending Deviations child tab
(
This tab displays all the Deviations raised for this application that arepending for an action.
)
Additionally, an underwriter can also raise deviations for the Application manually by followingthese steps, if required at this stage:
Click
Raised Deviations
under the
Decision Details
section.
The
Deviation
page that appears, includes the following tabs:
Credit Deviation tab
: Displays the credit deviations, which isApproved/Rejected by the internal/external credit team.
Business Deviation tab
: Displays the sales deviations, which isApproved/Rejected by the internal/external sales team.
Click
Raise Manual Deviation
.
Select
Deviation Defi nition
*
,
Role
*
and
User
*
from the drop down lists. Theassigned
User
can then take an action on the Deviation later, using the
DeviationApproval
option.
Note
:
Only those users who have the access to the Branch & Product ofthe Application and whose Level is equal to or higher than the Level of theDeviation are displayed in the User drop down list.
Click on
Save
. The Deviation record will be added in the grid. The assigned user can
Approve or Reject
this Deviation.
Note
:
The assigned user can
Approve or Reject
multiple deviations bychecking all the required deviations and selecting the Status andMitigant on the pop-up that appears.

At this stage, there are options to initiate PPSR Registration and view the history of PPSRregistration.

PPSR Registration Details
pop-up screen that appears, select the required option andinput

to
move to next stage of processing.
On successfully completing theaction,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed. Also, t
hesystem redirects back to the
Application
screen, where the Application can been seen listedeither in
Assigned
or
Pool
tab, depending on the confi guration in the
Task Assignment Master
.

An automatic mail is sent to the primary e-mail ID captured while creating the application.

To Cancel the Application
Click
More Actions >> Cancel Application
to
add the Application in the
Cancelled Applications
list.
On the
Cancel Application
pop-up screen that appears, select
Reason Code
(reason forcancellation) from the drop down list. The
Reason Description
gets automatically populated.
1.
Additionally, add the additional comments and click
Done
.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen andthe system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
To View the Cancelled Application:
1.
Go to
Applications >> Cancelled Applications
.
2.
Click
Application ID
link to view the details of the cancelled application.
3.
On the
Review Cancelled Application
page, select and action from the Select Actiondrop-down list as either
Confi rm Cancellation
or
Send Back for Processing to activity
.
In case
Send Back for Processing to activity
. is selected, select an activity from the
Select Activity
drop-down list to send to the application to that stage.
4.
Input
Remarks
and click
Save
.
On cancellation, the system automatically generates a notifi cation informing the user aboutthe rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
To Reject the Application
Click
More Options >> Reject Application
to send the Application to
Rejection Queue
.
On the pop up screen that appears, select
Decision Reason
(reason for rejection) from thedrop down list and click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
messageappears on the screen and the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any)are notifi ed.
On the
Applications
screen, click
Reject
tab to view the rejected Application in the grid. The
Status
of the rejected Application is displayed as
REJECT REVIEW
.
Click
Send Back
in the
Actions
column, to send the Application back to the lead user. Or, toremove the Application from the system, click
Reject
in the
Actions
column.
For
Decision
as
Reject,
the Application moves to
REJECTION QUEUE
and can be viewed using
Rejected Applications
option. Also, on rejection, the system automatically generates anotifi cation informing the user about the rejection via
SMS
or
Email
.
Reject Application

To Put an Application on Hold
Click
More Actions >>
Hold Application.
Note
:
TAT calculation for the Application stops until the Application is onHold.
On the pop up screen that appears, select single or multiple
Reason(s)
*
for hold from thedrop down list.
Specify the
Reassignment Date, Time of Reassign
and
Remarks
, if any.
Note
: The application can be put on hold for the current date as well.
Reassignment Date cannot be less than the current business date. On thisdate the hold on the Application gets removed and it is re-assigned to theApplication user.
Click
Done
. On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screenand the system redirects to the
Applications
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
On the
Applications
screen, go to
Hold
tab and view the Application on hold in the grid. TheStatus of the Application is displayed as
HOLD
.
Note
:
To unhold a Application,
click
Application
Number
in the grid andthen click
More Options >> Unhold
.
Only user with appropriate authority can hold/unhold the applications.

Click
More Actions >>
Generate Sanction Letter
to generate the sanction letter of theapplication.
Note
: Only authorized users can generate and/or print the Delivery Order.
To Generate Disbursal Report
Click
More Actions >>
Generate Disbursal Report
to generate the disbursal report of theapplication.
Note
: Only authorized users can generate and/or print the Delivery Order.
Hold Application
Generate Sanction Letter
Generate Disbursal Report
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 711/766
Note:
Disbursal Report can be confi gured or customized accordinglyusing the
Letter Template
master setup.
To Generate a Delivery Order
Click
More Actions >>
Generate Delivery Order
to generate the delivery order report.
Note
: Only authorized users can generate and/or print the Delivery Order.
A Delivery Order in PDF format is generated and saved in the Customer CommunicationHistory section. The user can also send the Delivery Order as communication for eachdisbursal.
Note
: In case asset wise split is mentioned in the application, the systemgenerates Delivery Orders for each asset. Similarly, for dealer wise split in theapplication, a Delivery Order is generated for every dealer with page breaksinserted for every asset in case the application also contains multiple assets.
Note
: The user can send the Delivery Order as a communication wheninitiating disbursal for loans with multiple tranches. Delivery orders can alsobe sent to dealers of approved tranches in case some of the tranches of aloan are still pending approval.
Generate Delivery Order

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 712/766

: To print a generated delivery order, right clickanywhere in the report and select
Print
.
The print option appears, only if the user has the printingauthorities.

pop-up screen appears showing the list of all the activitiesperformed on the application.

and

in therespective fi elds.

fi eld for whom the query is being raised.

check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click

to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the

link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Note
:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.

in the Action column of the grid.

from the drop down list and input

.

above the grid.

Disbursal Author
Disbursal Author
The Disbursal initiated at
Disbursal Stage
reaches Disbursal Author for approval.
All the information captured till Disbursal Stage is available (
in view only mode
) to the DisbursalAuthor for reference. Based on this information, the Disbursal Author can Accept or Reject theDisbursal. There is also an option to Send Back the Application to Disbursal Stage.
To approve the disbursal, follow these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Disbursal Author
.
On the
Disbursal Author
page,
the grid displays the following details of the active applications that are on Disbursal Authorstage and assigned to the logged-in user or the team:
Download Key fact Sheet
Grid Columns and Description

Columns
Description
Application ID
Displays the unique ID of the application.
Application Form Number
Displays application form number defi ned while capturing thelead details.
Loan Account Number
Displays the Loan Account Number of the applications that areeither partially or fully disbursed.
Identifi cation No.
Displays the identifi cation number provided by the customer.

Displays the ID of the customer generated by FinnOne Neo
TM
.
Customer Name
Displays the name of the customer.
Stage
Displays the current stage of the application.

Displays the Current Stage Timestamp of the application.
Query Status
Displays the status of the query.
Query Responded
Displays the name of the user who has responded the query.
Query Raised By
Displays the name of the user who has raised the query.
Product Type
Displays the Product Type associated with the application.
Product Name
Displays the Product Name associated with the application.
Scheme
Displays the Scheme associated with the application.
Account Type
Displays the account type of the customer.
In-Queue Status
Displays the status whether the application is new or returnedfrom later stages.
Request Type
Displays the type of application requests, as follows:
New Application
: Displays when the application requests for anew loan.
Top-up without Closure
: Displays when the application requestsfor the top-up.
Multiple Assets
: Displays when the application requests formultiple assets.
Balance Transfer with Top-up
: Displays when the applicationrequests for transferring the loan balance amount to otherbank with top-up.
New Credit Card Application
: Displays when the applicationrequests for a new credit card.
Primary Mobile No.
Displays the primary mobile number of the customer.
Sourcing Date
Displays the date on which the sourcing is done for theapplication.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the sourcing channel of the application.
Branch
Displays the branch from where the application was initiated.
Parent Branch
Displays the name of the parent branch.
Current Status
Displays the current status of the application.
Credit Offi cer
Displays the name of the credit offi cer of the application.

or

.

Yes
or

indicating the image(s) received from ECM orany other external sources.

the buttons to reassign the application.

Description

or

.

Tentative Disbursal Schedule Details
(This section appears in case of Home Loan and Micro Housing Finance (MHF) to capture thedisbursal details of multiple tranche.)
Total Payment
*
Displays the total payment made to the builder.
Disbursal Amount Required
*
Displays the amount required after deducting owner'scontribution from the total payment.
Owner's Contribution Required
*
Displays the owner's contribution amount.
Directly To Builder
If checked, the owner's contribution is paid directly to thebuilder.
Actual Owner's Contribution
Displays the actual amount of owner's contribution paid tothe builder.
Actual Disbursal Amount
Displays the actual amount disbursed.
Diff erence(AOC-OCR)
*
Display the diff erence between the Owner's ContributionRequired and Actual Owner's Contribution.
Status
Displays the current disbursal status.
Disburse
If checked, the disbursal information is sent to DisbursalSchedule section.
Application Tranches section
(
This section displays list of tranches as created in the system during Application Split asconfi gured in the Deal Tranche Policy and Multiple Asset Policy. In case of Omni product type,this section displays the tranches on the basis of the added products.
The grid in this section displays details like Split ID, Loan Amount, Disbursal Status, ApplicationTranche Status and Loan Account Number for each tranche. In case of dealer-wise split, Dealerdetails appears in the grid and in case of asset-wise split, Bought From and Make/Model/Variantdetails appear in the grid.
Additionally, there are options provided to
Validate/Re-ValidatePayment Account, Accept, Sendback or Reject the tranche.
.
Click
Split ID
of each tranche to authorize, sendback or reject the disbursal and to generate itsRepayment Schedule. Other details, such as Charges, Receipts, Repayment Parameters,Documents, Asset Requested and Instrument Details are also available at the respective tab toview against each Split ID.
)

Click

to submit the checklist.

to generate the checklist PDF and print the checklist. The
is displayed.

: The system automatically raises the Deviations based on responsemarked. The raised
Deviation(s)
must be approved using

option before initiating the Disbursal.

10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 723/766
Principal Recovery
*
Check
Start Principle Recovery
option to start the recovery ofthe principle on the amount disbursed.
Also, select
From
(date) for principle recovery.
Note
:
From (date) for principle recovery cannot be lessthan the current business date.
Loan Curtailment
Check this option to reduce the loan amount to the extent ofalready Disbursed Amount at the maximum.
Note
: Principal Recovery and Loan Curtailment fl ags are available only in the case of multipleDisbursal.
In case of single Disbursal, Loan Curtailment fl ag is marked in the back end by default. Amountequal to (Sanctioned Amount less Disbursed Amount) is automatically curtailed.
Remarks
Enter free text as remarks, if any.
In case of
Property Under Construction
(Mortgage Loans), click
Verifi cation
Status
initiateproperty verifi cation, if required. A
Collateral Investigation
request is assigned to theappropriate Agency in the system.
In case of
multiple Disbursal
,
Credit Overdue
button is available after the fi rst disbursal has beenmade. Credit Overdue option is provided to adjust any overdue amount in the previousDisbursal in the next Disbursal to be initiated.
Initiate Disbursal
Click to initiate the corresponding Disbursal.
On successfully completing the action,
a message appears onthe screen
. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
Click
Actual Repayment Schedule
to view the
Repayment Schedule
on a separate window.
On this window, the grid displays
Installment No., Installment Date, Installment Amount, Principal Amount, InterestAmount, Closing Balance
and
Days Diff erence
for each installment in the repayment schedule.
Click
Save
to save the details of subsequent disbursal.
Note:
The fi elds marked as mandatory and non-mandaroty are tentative and can be changedas per the provision provided by the product.
DCC Scheduling
Applicable only for SHG/JLG Product Types
Disbursal Credit Counseling (DCC) is done to educate and enlighten SHG/JLG group members sothat they utilize disbursed funds in most eff ective manner to sustain livelihood.
The DCC workfl ow consists of two stages: DCC Scheduling and DCC Execution. In the DCCScheduling stage, a DCC session is scheduled by the RM user.
Note:
Only the DCC user fi eld on the Sourcing screen can be edited in the DCC Schedulingstage. All the other details displayed are as captured in the previous stages and are read only.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 724/766
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
On the
Applications
page, the
Assigned
tab appears as active by default and the grid on this tab displays the followingdetail:
Columns
Description
Application ID
Displays the unique application ID clicking which user can viewthe application details and further process the application.
Neo Cust ID
Displays the customer ID associated with the respectiveapplication.
Identifi cation No.
Displays the identifi cation number of the customer.
Customer Name
Displays the name of the customer.
Stage
Displays the current stage of the application.
In-Queue Status
Displays the status of the application whether the application isnewly introduced or returned from a stage.
Product Type
Displays the product type associated with the respectiveapplication.
Product Name
Displays the product name associated with the respectiveapplication.
Account Type
Displays the account type as
FAS
,
FOS
, or
BOTH
in case of the FASapplication.
Request Type
Displays the application request type.
Phone No.
Displays the phone number of the customer.
Sourcing Date
Displays the date on which the application is created.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the channel through which the application is created.
Sourcing Branch
Displays the branch at which the application is created.
Assignee
Displays the name of the user to whom the application assigned.
Created By
Displays the name of the user who created the application.
Priority
Displays the priority of the application.
Actions
Click
Reassign For User
to assign the application to another useror click
Assign To Pool
to assign the application to pool.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
Select Preference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
T
o learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, use
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
DCC Scheduling
screen for applicationprocessing.
Grid Columns and Description

stage.

,

,

, and

tabs. The tab details are as explained in

Go to
Finance Details
tab >>
Sourcing Details
tab to select the
DCC User
to whom the application needs to be assigned.

The DCC User fi eld requires an authority based access and will be displayed to users withappropriate authority.
Click
Save
to save the details.
On successfully completing the action,
a
Success
message appears on the screen. Else, errors(if any) are notifi ed.

: Apart from the tabs that are explained above,there are many miscellaneous tabs thatexist in the system and may appear as per the product defi nition confi guration. In such cases,information relevant for those tabs can be viewed and modifi ed as well.

to

stage.

pop-up screen appears showing the list of all the activitiesperformed on the application.

pop-up screen that appears, input

and
Query
in therespective fi elds.

fi eld for whom the query is being raised.

DCC Execution
DCC Execution
Disbursal Credit Counseling (DCC) is done to educate and enlighten SHG/JLG group members sothat they utilize disbursed funds in most eff ective manner to sustain livelihood.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 727/766
The DCC workfl ow consists of two stages: DCC Scheduling and DCC Execution. In the DCCExecution stage, the DCC session is approved by the DCC user and the application is moved tothe next stage.
Note:
Only the DCC Question Checklist can be edited in the DCC Execution stage. All the otherdetails displayed are as captured in the previous stages and are read only. The DCC User can viewand print all documents received in the various stages corresponding to the application andapplicants and print them but cannot accept any new documents. The user can also send backthe application to the DCC Scheduling stage if needed. The application cannot be rejected orcancelled at this stage.

Disbursal Details
(In this tab, the system displays read-only fi elds like
Total Sanctioned Amount, Sanction Date,Disbursal Type
(Single or Multiple),
Application Status
captured initially at previous stages.
Disbursal Status
is displayed as either Not Disbursed (when Disbursed Amount is zero), PartDisbursed (when Disbursed Amount is less than Sanctioned Amount), Fully Disbursed (whenDisbursed Amount is equal to Sanctioned Amount), Disbursal Cancelled (when the disbursal getscancelled from the servicing system) or Loan Cancelled (when the loan gets cancelled from theservicing system).
The System also displays the
Loan Account #, Disbursed Amount
and
First Disbursal Date
.
)
(
Disbursed Amount
gets reduced by the amount of disbursal, in case the disbursal getscancelled.)
Fields Name
Description
Disbursal Report
Click to generate the disbursal report that shows the disbursaldetails of the application.
CAM Report
Click to generate the Credit Approval Memo of the application.
Application Summary
Click to view summary of the application that containssummarized information of the application.
Application Details
(This section displays the details of loan application.)
Sanctioned Amount
Displays the total sanctioned amount for the loan application.
Sanctioned Date
Displays the date on which the amount was sanctioned.
Available Amount
Displays the available amount for the loan application.
Maximum Sanctioned Amount
Displays the maximum amount limit sanctioned for the loanapplication.
Maximum Sanctioned Tenure
Displays the maximum tenure limit sanctioned for the loanapplication.
ROI/Tenure
Displays the rate of interest and tenure of the loan application

4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 732/766
Disbursed Amount
Displays the amount already disbursed in case of multipledisbursal.

Single
or
Multiple
.

Tentative Disbursal Schedule Details
(This section appears in case of Home Loan and Micro Housing Finance (MHF) to capture thedisbursal details of multiple tranche.)
Total Payment
*
Input the total payment made to the builder.
Disbursal Amount Required
*
Displays the amount required after deducting owner'scontribution from the total payment.
Owner's ContributionRequired
*
Displays the owner's contribution amount.
Directly To Builder
Check if the owner's contribution is paid directly to the builder.
Actual Owner's Contribution
Displays the actual amount of owner's contribution paid to thebuilder.
Actual Disbursal Amount
Displays the actual amount disbursed.
Diff erence(AOC-OCR)
*
Display the diff erence between the Owner's ContributionRequired and Actual Owner's Contribution.
Status
Displays the current disbursal status.
Disburse
Check to send the disbursal information to Disbursal Schedulesection.
Application Tranches section
(
This section displays list of tranches as created in the system during Application Split asconfi gured in the Deal Tranche Policy and Multiple Asset Policy. In case of Omni product type,this section displays the tranches on the basis of the added products.
The grid in this section displays details like Split ID, Dealer, Loan Amount, Disbursal Status,Application Tranche Status and Loan Account Number for each tranche. The grid also providesthe Copy and Paste options for duplicating instrument details for multiple tranches. Additionally,there are options provided to Initiate, Validate/Re-Validate Payment Account, Validate/Re-Validate Repayment Account, View Reports and Save to validate and revalidate customer's bankaccount and view and save the last fetched validation report.
Click Split ID of each tranche to initiate its disbursal and to generate its Repayment Schedule.Also, if required, use the Charges, Receipts, Loan Parameters, Documents, Asset Requested andInstrument Details tab to capture or modify details against each Split ID.
)
Disbursal Details
(Use this section to capture the disbursal details of the application.)
Agreement Booklet SerialNumber
Input the serial number of agreement booklet provided to thecustomer which contains the loan details.
Linked Collateral Number
Displays all the linked collateral numbers.

,

and

. The additional document canalso be marked as

.

:
Only authorized users can download the documents. Thisbutton does not appear to the unauthorized users.

: Select the document(s) and click the
Scan/Rescan
option to initiate therequest of rescanning the document. Also, select Reason for Rescan, Scan for Mandatoryand Remark.
Note
: Once the scan/rescan is completed in FinnOne Neo ECM, theCAS user will receive a notifi cation for successful or failed scan.

: Click the

link to partially or fully cancel therescan request.

and selecting the required option.

that appears above theleft pane and select the status from the list. Also, set the required fi elds inthe respective pop-up screen. The icons of the respective status appearsalong with the name of the document.
Incomplete Doc (
): Click to mark a Received document as incomplete and input the
Incomplete Reasons
and
Remarks
in the pop up. A document can be marked asincomplete for various reasons such as blurred images, incomplete set of images.
Complete Doc
: Click to mark an incomplete document as complete. This option appearsonly if a received documents has been marked as incomplete.
Add, View, Edit and Compare Documents
: Click
to add single or multiple imagesof the documents that are
Received
. User can view and edit the image in the
DocumentViewer
section at the right side of the screen. Click
to compare the diff erent versionof the documents and their details. Moreover, there is a drop down list with the name ofall the uploaded images. Select the fi le to view in the document viewer.
Fields Name and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 749/766
Note
: Only users with the appropriate authority can view thedocument images.
Once the details of the documents is updated, click
Submit
.
The history of the CAM report with the last saved decision is maintained in the
Communication History
tab.
More Actions on Application
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
move to next stage of processing. In this case, if the all therequired details are provided and documents uploaded, the application moves to
DisbursalMaker
stage.
To View Workfl ow History Record of the Application
Click
More Actions >>
Workfl ow History Records
to view the workfl ow history of theApplication.
The
Workfl ow History Records
pop-up screen appears showing the list of all the activitiesperformed on the application.
To Raise a Query
Click
More Actions >>
Raise Query
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Move Application to Next Stage
Workfl ow History Records
Raise a Query
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 750/766

:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.
To Unquery
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
Select
Reason
from the drop down list and input
Comments
.
Click
Done
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk unquery by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Unquery
above the grid.
To Reassign Query
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
To Reassign a query user has to select either
Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
orcombination of both.
Input
Remarks
and click

.

:

above the grid.
