Send To Card Management System
Applicable only for
Credit Card
Product Type
Use the
Send to Card Management
interface to manually send Applications to the CardManagement System, where t
here is no online connectivity between the Card ManagementSystem and the CAS System. It also stores the responses as received from the Card ManagementSystem.
Card Management System is a platform to view the details of the card, the underwriter decision,intent indicators and so on. It also displays the raised deviations and its history for the respective
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 751/766
account.
Follow these steps to send Applications to the Card Management System:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Send To Card Management System
.
On the
Send To Card Management
page that appears, go to the
Pool
tab
and locate the Application that is to be send tothe card management system using its
Application ID
in the grid.
In Pool tab, the grid displays
Application ID
,
Customer Name
,
Request Type
,
Logo
,
Card Type
,
Sourcing Date
,
SourcingChannel
,
Created By
,
Approved Date
and
Actions
for all the existing Applications.
Click
Assign
icon in the
Actions
column to assign the located Application.
Or,
Alternatively, select
single
or
multiple
Applications using the check boxes provided in the grid and click
Assign to Me
that appears above the grid.
On successfully completing the action, a message appears on the screen and the system redirects to the
Send To CardManagement System
tab on the
Send To Card Management System
screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
The grid in the
Send To Card Management
tab displays
Application ID
,
Customer Name
,
Request Type
,
Logo
,
Card Type
,
Sourcing Date
,
Sourcing Channel
,
Created By
,
Approved Date
and
Actions
for each Application.
In the grid, locate the Application that is required to be send to the card management system and in the
Action
column,click
Send To Card Management System
.
Or,
In the grid, select
single
or
multiple
Applications using the check boxes provided in the grid and click
Send To CardManagement System
that appears above the grid.
On successfully completing the action, a notifi cation appears on the screen
accordingly
. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
Once an application is successfully sent to Card Management System, the response for that application as received fromthe Card Management System gets stored in the
Archived
tab. Use this tab to view details of the response.
Also, in case of
InstaCard Application
, the responses get stored in the
Insta Cards Archived
tab.
Raise Query
Use this option to raise query about an application at the transaction level. You can raise a queryto a user and to a team.
Follow these steps to submit response of a Query raised:
Go to
Application>>
Raise Query
.
On the
Raise Query
page that appears, input the
Application ID
of the application for which a query needs to be raisedand click
Search
. The application details get displayed on the page along with the queries already raised for theapplication.
To raise a query, set the following fi elds:
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 752/766
Field Name
Description
Query Code
*
Select the query code from the drop-down list.
Query Name
*
Displays the query name based on the selected querycode.
The fi eld is non-editable.
Query
*
Input the query that needs to be raised.
TAT Hold
Select the check box if the TAT needs to be on hold.
Stage
Select the stage to be mapped with the query. This fi eld isauto populated when the query is raised within theworkfl ow.
Available Documents
Click to select the document(s) to raise query.
Documents
Displays the name of the document against which thequery is raised.
Raise to Team
Select the team to whom the query needs to be raised.
Raised to User
Select the user to whom the query needs to be raised.
Choose File
Click and select the fi le that needs to be uploaded.
Click this button to add another fi le associated with thequery.
Click
Raise Query
.
On successfully raising the query, the queries grid is refreshed where the new Query can be seen listed in the grid. Thegrid displays the following details:
Column Name
Description
Query Code
Displays code of the raised queries.
Query Name
Displays name of the query.
TAT Hold
Displays whether the Turn Around Time (TAT) is on hold ornot.
Original Assignee
Displays name of the original assignee.
Raise To Team
Displays the team name to which the query is raised.
Raise To User
Displays the user name to whom the query is raised.
Raised On
Displays the date and time on which the query was raised.
Query Status
Displays the current status of query.
Actions
Displays following icons:
: Click to unquery the raised query. User need to selectsingle or multiple reasons from the
Reason
dropdownlist and input
Comments
.
Field(s) Name and Description
Grid Column(s) and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 753/766
: Click to reassign the query to another team or user.User need to mention the name of the team or user inthe
Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
fi elds andinput
Remarks
.
Response Query
Use this option to respond the queries raised on an application. Users with the appropriateauthority can respond the queries.
Follow these steps to submit response of the queries:
Go to
Application>>
Response Query
.
On the
Response Query
page that appears, the grid displays Application Id,
Application Number, Customer Name,Product Type, Scheme
and
Pending Queries
of all the Applications on which the queries are raised and assigned to thelogged in user or its team.
Click the
Application Number
link to view the query associated with the application.
A grid at the bottom of the page displays all the pending queries that are associated with the application.
Click the
Query Code
link from the grid below to display the query details in the respective fi elds.
Click
Select Documents
to select the document(s) associated with the query. The name of the selected documents willappear in the Documents fi eld.
Input the
Response
*
for the query.
Optionally, click
Choose File
to upload and send single or multiple fi les with the response.
Click the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions and responses provided.
Click
Submit Response
.
On successfully submitting the response, the grid at the bottom gets updated.
Note
: To forward the queries click the
icon in the grid and input the
Forward To Team
and /or
Forward to User
fi elds.
Unquery
Use this option to remove a query raised by the user himself or another user if the user has therequired authority. Using this option allows an application on which a query was raised earlier tobe moved to the next stage.
Follow these steps to unquery:
Go to
Application>>
Query Grid >> Unquery
.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 754/766
On the
Unquery
page that appears, input the
Application ID
and click
Search
. The application details get displayed on thepage along with the queries already raised for the application.
To unquery a record, click
in the
Action
column of the record in the queries grid.
The
Query Code
*
, Query Name
*
, Query
*
, Raised To Team, Raised to User
and
Reason Description
fi elds and
TAT Hold
check box gets auto-populated with the respective values in non-editable mode.
Note
:
To unquery multiple queries, select all the queries using the checkbox and click
unquery
above the grid.
Same reasons, reason description, comments and documents will be stamped inall the queries that are selected for bulk unquery.
Select a
Reason
*
to unquery and input the
Comments
*
.
Select
Choose File
to upload a fi le, if needed, and Click
Done
.
On successfully saving the Query, the
Query History Records
grid is refreshed and the query record is removed from thegrid.
Optionally, to view a query details, click the
Query Code
link and to view a query history, click the
Query History Records
link.
The Query History Records grid appears displaying the details like Query Code, Query Name, Raise to Team, Raised toUser, Raised On and Status fi elds.
Reassign Query
Use this option to reassign the queries from a user/team to another user/team.
Follow these steps to reassign query:
Go to
Application>>
Reassign Query
.
On the
Reassign Query
page, search the query by providing any of the following search parameters. The following searchparameters can be used to search a query:
Raise to Team
: Input the team to search all the queries assigned to that team.
Raise to User
: Input the user to search all the queries assigned to that user.
Application Number
: Input the application to search the queries raised on that application.
Product Type
: Select the product type to search all the queries raised for the respective product type applications.
Click
Search
to display the applications that match the search parameters.
Click the
Application Number
link to view the query raised on that application.
Queries raised on the application are displayed in a grid.
Click the
Query Code
link. This auto populates the
Query Code, Query Name, Query Description, Stage, TAT Hold, RaiseTo Team
and
Raise To User
fi elds.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk reassign by checking the checkbox and clicking
Reassign
above the grid.
Queries selected for bulk reassign will be reassigned to singleteam/user.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 755/766
Set the following fi elds to reassign the query:
Field Name
Field Value
Re-raise To Team
Input the name of the team to which the query is to be reassigned.
Re-raise To User
Input the name of the user to which the query is to be reassigned.
Remark
*
Input free text as remarks for reassigning query.
Optionally, click the
Query History Records
link to view the history of the query.
Click
Reassign Query
.
On successfully completing the action, a notifi cation appears on the screen. Else, errors (if any) are notifi ed.
Deviation Approval Sheet
Use this option to approve/reject the deviations raised on the
Credit Approval
or
DisbursalMaker
stage through Deviation Approval Sheet.
To approve/reject the deviation through approval sheet, follow these steps:
Go to
Application>>
Deviation Approval Sheet
.
The grid on the
Deviation Approval Sheet
page displays the following information:
Columns Name
Description
Application Number
Displays the application number on which the deviation israised.
Neo Cust ID
Displays the customer ID number.
Customer Name
Displays the customer name.
Phone Number
Displays the phone number of the customer.
Sourcing Channel
Displays the sourcing channel through which the customerapproached for loan.
Product Type
Displays the product type associated with the application.
Stage
Displays the current stage of the application.
Task Type
Displays the task type as either
THROUGH
,
TO
or
SEEN
.
Status
Displays the current status of the deviation approval process.
Field(s) Name and Description
Grid Columns and Description
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 756/766
Click the
Application Number
to open the details of the deviation on Approval Sheet page.
Approval Sheet
(Use this tab to view the details and authorize the raised deviation.)
Application Summary
Click this link to view the summary of the application on which thedeviation is raised.
Collateral
Click this link to view the details of the collateral pertaining to theapplication.
RCU
Displays the details of the RCU.
External Deviation
(
This section displays the deviations raised from external systems.
)
Internal Deviation
(
This section displays the deviations raised from
Credit Approval
or
Disbursal Maker
stage.
)
Through/To/Seen Employees
(Use this section to authorize the deviation by Through/To/Seen users as defi ned on the
CreditApproval
or
Disbursal Maker
stage.)
Role
*
Select a reason to cancel the disbursal memo.
User
Displays the description of the reason as defi ned in the
Reason Master
.
Decision
Select either
APPROVE
or
REJECT
to authorize the deviation.
Remarks
Input free text as remarks, if any.
Task Status and History
(This section displays the status of the deviation approval process.)
Click
Save
to save the decision as a draft or click
Save and Submit
to submit the decision.
Renewal Recommendation
Applicable only for
Finance
Product Types
At this stage, the user renews the sanctioned amount for the fi nance application. The fi nanceamount can be added as per the requirement of the customer. An application reaches this stagewhen the fi nance amount renewal request is initiated by the user using the
Application Renewal
option.
Application can be considered for renewal once it is disbursed and reaches the
Sent to Ops
stage.
Once the renewal request is initiated, it needs to be approved from the authorized user.
Learn to process an application at this stage using these steps:
Go to
Applications >> Application Grid >>
Applications
.
Approval Sheet
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 757/766
The
Assigned
tab appears as active by default, the grid in this tab display details like
Application ID, Customer ID,
Customer Name,
Stage, In-Queue Status, Product Type, Product Name, Account Type, Request Type, Phone No.,Sourcing Date, Sourcing Channel, Sourcing Branch, Assignee, Created By, Priority and Actions
,
for each application.
Note
:
To change the default appearance of the grid for a particular user, click
SelectPreference
placed below the grid and select the preferred columns on the popup screen.
Note
:
To learn how to assign an application, refer to
How to assign an application
tutorial
.
In the grid, click
Application ID
of a particular application to navigate to the
Renewal Recommendation
page.
Note
: Use the Stage column to make sure the selected application is at the
Renewal
Recommendation
stage.
On
Renewal Recommendation
page, go to the following sections to view, edit or add information and to mark the decisionas
Recommend
or
Not Recommended
:
Section/Sub-Section Description and Usage
Loan Information section
(
This section displays Product, Scheme, Amount, Installment, Tenure, Purpose of Loan andprovides links to view or modify Sourcing Details, Loan Parameters, Receipt Details, FinancialSummary, GST, Cattle Details (for Agriculture Loans) and Property/Asset Details.
)
Applicant Information section
(
This section displays Name, Age, Cust ID and Marital Status of the Primary Applicant. It alsoprovides Applicant Details link to view or modify all the information captured for PrimaryApplication or Co-Applicant/Guarantor (if any).
)
Ability Indicators section
(
This section display the ability indicators calculated by the system for the Applicant like, LTV(Loan To Value ratio), IIR (Installment to Income Ratio), Aggregate LTV and DBR (Debt BurdenRatio). It also, provides the Financial Details link to view or modify all the fi nancial informationpreviously captured under Income Details, Deductions, Assets, Liabilities and the Income ExpenseSummary.
)
Exposure Details section
This section displays the Exposure Details, received from LMS or External Service, of PrimaryApplicants, Co-applicant and Guarantor of an application so that the data can be used forfurther calculation.
The exposure details of each individual including liability details can beviewed by clicking the customer name link. This section also provides a button to Re-Initiatethe Exposure Details and a link to view the breakup of Exposure Details of the applicant.
Intent Indicators section
(
This section displays the results for Credit Approval Policy, Field Investigation, CollateralVerifi cation, RCU
,
Credit Bureau Check, Dedupe Check and Credit Score run.
These validations can be re-run by the user, if required.
Additionally, this section also provides option to generate (view and download) CAM (CreditApproval Memo) Report that displays the application information that has been captured till thisstage and the Collected Document button to view the documents collected for the Application.
)
Underwriter Decision tab >> Miscellaneous Information section
(
This section displays the miscellaneous information captured in previous stages.)
Decision/Forward History section
(
Use this section to view log of decisions taken on the Application in the previous stages and theforward history of the Application
.
)
Section(s) Name and Usage
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 758/766
Approval Checklist section
(
Use this section to check the status of the points listed in the Approval Checklist and markresponse against each.
Based on the response marked, the Deviations for the Application are raised automatically bythe system.
A response can be marked as either
Yes
,
No
or
NA
against any checklist point.
Click
Submit/Update Deviations
and the system automatically raises the Deviations based onresponse marked. These Deviations can be viewed in the
Approve Deviations tab
.
Optionally, click the
Print Checklist
link to view and print the checklist of current as well theprevious stages on which the checklist are marked.)
Eligibility Policy Setup section
(Use this section to determine the member-wise eligibility for a loan. The section displays theApplicant Type, Name, Criteria, Eligibility Setup Name and Eligible Amount.)
Decision Justifi cation section
(
Use this section to input strengths and weaknesses of the applicant and the executivesummary
.
)
Eligibility Policy section
(
Use this section to view the result of eligibility calculation for the Primary Applicant based onthe eligibility set mapped to the product/ scheme
.
)
Decision Details section
(
Use this section to mark the decision as either
Recommend
or
Not Recommended
and specifythe recommended renewed loan amount that should be sanctioned for this Application.
This section off ers the following additional options:
A
llows an underwriter to add
Special Condition
to the Application that must be met tillthe specifi ed Application Stage.
T
here is a provision to
Forward
the application to another underwriter to provideconsent or approval for the same
.
The user can click
Raise Deviation
to manually raise the Credit or Business deviations,re-run deviation policy and view history of the deviations raise internally or externally.
The user can click
Hold
to hold the application. On clicking
Hold
, mention the holdreason, reassignment date and time and remarks in the pop-up screen.
Sanctioned Amount
Displays the amount sanctioned for the loan.
Available Amount
Displays the available amount.
Disbursed Amount
Displays the amount disbursed against the loan application.
New SanctionedAmount
Input the renewed sanctioned amount.
Renew
Select this option in case the limit is renewed.
Review
Select this option in case the limit is reviewed.
Raised Deviations
Click to view any deviations raised for this application.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 759/766
Forward
Click to forward this application to another underwriter using the
Forward Application
screen that appears.
Decision
*
Select a decision for renewal as
Recommended
or
Not Recommended
.
Reason
*
Select an appropriate reason for recommending or not recommendingthe renewal.
Decision Comments
Input additional comments, if any.
Decision Details section >> Special Conditions sub-section
(
Use this section to add single or multiple Special Conditions to the Application that must be mettill the specifi ed Application Stage.
These conditions are tracked at post approval and disbursal activities
.
)
Click
Save
to save the details. Once saved, this application reaches to the

stage for authorization.
Go to

tab to view the renewal and review history of the fi nance account.
Go to

tab to apply charges on application renewal.

Exposure Details section
This section displays the Exposure Details, received from LMS or External Service, of PrimaryApplicants, Co-applicant and Guarantor of an application so that the data can be used forfurther calculation.
The exposure details of each individual including liability details can be
Section(s) Name and Usage

viewed by clicking the customer name link. This section also provides a button to Re-Initiatethe Exposure Details and a link to view the breakup of Exposure Details of the applicant.
Intent Indicators section
(
This section displays the results for Credit Approval Policy, Field Investigation, CollateralVerifi cation, RCU
,
Credit Bureau Check, Dedupe Check and Credit Score run.
These validations can be re-run by the user, if required.
Additionally, this section also provides option to generate (view and download) CAM (CreditApproval Memo) Report that displays the application information that has been captured till thisstage and the Collected Document button to view the documents collected for the Application.
)
Underwriter Decision tab >> Miscellaneous Information section
(
This section displays the miscellaneous information captured in previous stages.)
Decision/Forward History section
(
Use this section to view log of decisions taken on the Application in the previous stages and theforward history of the Application
.
)
Approval Checklist section
(
Use this section to check the status of the points listed in the Approval Checklist and markresponse against each.
Based on the response marked, the Deviations for the Application are raised automatically bythe system.
A response can be marked as either
Yes
,
No
or
NA
against any checklist point.
Click
Submit/Update Deviations
and the system automatically raises the Deviations based onresponse marked. These Deviations can be viewed in the
Approve Deviations tab
.
Optionally, click the
Print Checklist
link to view and print the checklist of current as well theprevious stages on which the checklist are marked.)
Eligibility Policy Setup section
(Use this section to determine the member-wise eligibility for a loan. The section displays theApplicant Type, Name, Criteria, Eligibility Setup Name and Eligible Amount.)
Decision Justifi cation section
(
Use this section to input strengths and weaknesses of the applicant and the executivesummary
.
)
Eligibility Policy section
(
Use this section to view the result of eligibility calculation for the Primary Applicant based onthe eligibility set mapped to the product/ scheme
.
)
Decision Details section
(
Use this section to mark the decision as either
Recommend
or
Not Recommended
and specifythe recommended renewed loan amount that should be sanctioned for this Application.
This section off ers the following additional options:
A
llows an underwriter to add
Special Condition
to the Application that must be met tillthe specifi ed Application Stage.
T
here is a provision to
Forward
the application to another underwriter to provideconsent or approval for the same
.
The user can click
Raise Deviation
to manually raise the Credit or Business deviations,re-run deviation policy and view history of the deviations raise internally or externally.
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 763/766
The user can click
Hold
to hold the application. On clicking
Hold
, mention the holdreason, reassignment date and time and remarks in the pop-up screen.
Sanctioned Amount
Displays the amount sanctioned for the loan.
Available Amount
Displays the available amount.
Disbursed Amount
Displays the amount disbursed against the loan application.
New SanctionedAmount
Displays the renewed sanctioned amount.
Raised Deviations
Click to view any deviations raised for this application.
Forward
Click to forward this application to another underwriter using the
Forward Application
screen that appears.
Decision
*
Select a decision for renewal as
Approve
or
Reject
.
Reason
*
Select an appropriate reason for approving or rejecting the renewal.
Decision Comments
Input additional comments, if any.
Decision Details section >> Special Conditions sub-section
(Use this section to add single or multiple Special Conditions to the Application that must be mettill the specifi ed Application Stage.
These conditions are tracked at post approval and disbursal activities
.
)
Click
Save
to save the details.
Go to
Renewal History
tab to view the renewal history of the fi nance account.
More Actions on Application
To Move Application to the Next Stage
Click
Move to Next Stage
to
complete the process of application renewal.
To View Workfl ow History Record of the Application
Click
More Actions >>
Workfl ow History Records
to view the workfl ow history of theApplication.
The
Workfl ow History Records
pop-up screen appears showing the list of all the activitiesperformed on the application.
Move Application to Next Stage
Workfl ow History Records
Raise a Query
4/1/26, 3:50 PM Applications
10.1.63.39:5151/finnone-webapp/webhelp/en_US/StartPage.htm?rhcsh=1#t=Applications.htm 764/766
To Raise a Query
Click
More Actions >>
Raise Query
On the
Raise Query
pop-up screen that appears, input
Query Code
and
Query
in therespective fi elds.
Input the name in the
Raised To
fi eld for whom the query is being raised.
Optionally, check the
TAT Hold
check box to hold the turn around time until the query isresolved.
Click
Raise Query
to add the query in the grid.
Optionally, click the
Query Code
link to view the query details.
Also, user can click on the
Query History Records
link to view the previous actions andresponses provided.
Note
:
From More Actions, user can unquery or reassign a raised query.
User also has an option to unquery or reassign multiple queries in bulk.
To Unquery
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
Select
Reason
from the drop down list and input
Comments
.
Click
Done
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk unquery by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Unquery
above the grid.
To Reassign Query
Click
in the Action column of the grid.
To Reassign a query user has to select either
Re-raise To Team
or
Re-raise To User
orcombination of both.
Input
Remarks
and click
Reassign Query
.
Note
:
Multiple records can be selected for bulk reassign by checking thecheckbox and clicking
Reassign
above the grid.
To Cancel Renewal
Cancel Application

Click
More Actions
and select
Cancel Renewal
to cancel the renewal process. On cancelling therenewal process, the application moves to the
Disbursal Initiation
stage.
